activities.intro: Openers, the two-sided frame, the example marker and the conclusion, each question is a sentence for the text you're about to write. Watch the cases hiding in the set phrases.

questions.prompt: Which phrase means 'in my opinion'?
questions.explanation: po mojem mišljenju = in my opinion. prema tome = accordingly, u svakom slučaju = in any case.

questions.prompt: Which pair correctly weighs two sides against each other?
options: s jedne strane … s druge strane
questions.answer: s jedne strane … s druge strane
questions.explanation: s jedne strane… s druge strane… is the two-sided frame. The others mix openers, examples and conclusions.

questions.prompt: S ___ strane, tehnologija nam olakšava život. (jedan, agree with strane)
questions.explanation: s + genitive in the set phrase: s jedne strane. strana to strane, jedan to jedne.

questions.prompt: Slažem se s ___ da tehnologija pomaže. (tvrdnja, instrumental)
questions.explanation: slagati se s + instrumental: tvrdnja to tvrdnjom.

questions.prompt: You want to introduce a concrete example. You write:
questions.explanation: na primjer (primjerice) introduces an example. Zaključno concludes, kao prvo opens, stoga draws a conclusion.

questions.prompt: Which sentence-initial word means 'however'?
questions.explanation: međutim = however. osim toga = besides, naime = namely, dakle = so/thus.

questions.prompt: Draw your conclusion: Therefore we should use technology in moderation.
questions.answer: Stoga bismo tehnologiju trebali koristiti s mjerom
questions.explanation: The conditional clitic bismo takes second position after Stoga; s mjerom = in moderation closes the thought.

questions.prompt: ___ ima nedostataka, prednosti prevladavaju. (although)
questions.explanation: iako = although, opens the concession clause before the main point.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Smatram da su prednosti veće od nedostatci.
Smatram da je prednosti veća od nedostataka.
Smatram da su prednosti veće nego nedostataka.
Smatram da su prednosti veće od nedostataka.
questions.answer: Smatram da su prednosti veće od nedostataka.
questions.explanation: Plural subject prednosti to plural verb su; comparison with od + genitive plural: nedostataka (from nedostatak).

days.title: Week 23 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate verbs and science theme; hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Review + real talk: the two families and the tricky presents must survive contact with conversation.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): science, technology, your favourite invention.
Record 2 minutes: 'Kako je tehnologija promijenila moj život'; listen back.
Cold-test both verb families (pisati, gledati).

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 recurring errors from your recording.
Conjugate sjesti and donijeti in the present.

activities.title: Week 23 sweep, verbs, science and back to speech
activities.intro: Everything week 23 built, ready to fire in tonight's long conversation: the pisati and gledati families, the tricky presents, and the science vocabulary of Croatian inventors. Read each line aloud, fast, recognition isn't the goal tonight, production is.

items.hr: Potpisao sam ugovor i zapisao datum u kalendar.
items.en: I signed the contract and noted the date in the calendar.
items.note: pisati family: potpisati (sign), zapisati (note down). The prefix carries the meaning; the base stays pisati.

items.hr: Liječnik me pregledao i sve mi je objasnio.
items.en: The doctor examined me and explained everything to me.
items.note: gledati family: pregledati = to examine, review. pre- = 'over, through'.

items.hr: U nedjelju smo razgledali stari dio grada.
items.en: On Sunday we went sightseeing in the old part of the city.
items.note: razgledati = to look around, sightsee. raz- spreads the looking around, in every direction.

items.hr: Čim stignem kući, sjednem i počnem kuhati.
items.en: As soon as I get home, I sit down and start cooking.
items.note: Three tricky presents chained: stignem (stići), sjednem (sjesti), počnem (početi). All triggered by čim.

items.hr: Sutra ću ti donijeti knjigu koju sam pročitao.
items.en: Tomorrow I'll bring you the book I've read.
items.note: donijeti (present donesem) and pročitati, aspect and irregular stem side by side.

items.hr: Nikola Tesla je hrvatski i američki izumitelj.
items.en: Nikola Tesla is a Croatian and American inventor.
items.note: izumitelj = inventor; izum = invention. Tesla was born in Smiljan, in today's Croatia.

items.hr: Slavoljub Penkala izumio je mehaničku olovku.
items.en: Slavoljub Penkala invented the mechanical pencil.
items.note: izumiti = to invent (perfective). Penkala also patented an early fountain pen (nalivpero).

items.hr: Znanost napreduje zahvaljujući istraživanju.
items.en: Science advances thanks to research.
items.note: znanost = science; istraživanje = research; napredovati = to advance. zahvaljujući + dative = thanks to.

items.hr: Njegovo najvažnije otkriće promijenilo je svijet.
items.en: His most important discovery changed the world.
items.note: otkriće = discovery (from otkriti, to discover); a neuter noun of the -će type. promijeniti = to change.

items.hr: Znanstvenici su izveli pokus u laboratoriju.
items.en: The scientists carried out an experiment in the laboratory.
items.note: znanstvenik = scientist; pokus = experiment; izvesti pokus = to carry out an experiment.

items.hr: Prestao sam koristiti stari mobitel i kupio novi.
items.en: I stopped using my old phone and bought a new one.
items.note: prestati (present prestanem) + infinitive = to stop doing. A stati-family verb, a preview of next week's family.

items.hr: Kako je tehnologija promijenila tvoj svakodnevni život?
items.en: How has technology changed your everyday life?
items.note: Your conversation seed for tonight. promijeniti = to change; svakodnevni = everyday.

activities.title: Week 23 mixed sweep
activities.intro: Prefix families, irregular presents and science vocabulary in one pass before you talk. If it comes out clean here, it will come out clean tonight.

questions.prompt: What does 'izum' mean?
questions.explanation: izum = invention; izumitelj = inventor. Discovery is otkriće, scientist znanstvenik, experiment pokus.

questions.prompt: The doctor ___ me. (examined)
questions.explanation: pregledati = to examine. pogledati = take a look, razgledati = sightsee, ugledati = catch sight of.

questions.prompt: Čim ___ kući, sjednem i počnem kuhati. (stići, prezent, ja)
questions.explanation: stići to stignem (-ći to -gnem). čim + perfective present has future meaning.

questions.prompt: Sutra ću ti ___ knjigu. (donijeti, infinitiv u futuru)
questions.explanation: Futur I keeps the infinitive: ću + donijeti. The tricky stem donese- only appears in the present.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to sign' (a contract)?
questions.explanation: potpisati = to sign; zapisati = note down; prepisati = copy; opisati = describe.

questions.prompt: Znanost napreduje ___ istraživanju. (thanks to)
questions.explanation: zahvaljujući + dative = thanks to (istraživanju). zbog + genitive = because of, unatoč + dative = despite.

questions.prompt: Ask the conversation opener: How has technology changed your life?
questions.answer: Kako je tehnologija promijenila tvoj život
questions.explanation: Question word Kako, then the clitic je in second position, then subject and the perfect participle promijenila.

questions.prompt: Prestao sam ___ stari mobitel. (to use)
questions.explanation: prestati + infinitive: prestao sam koristiti. The other forms are present, past and the verbal adverb.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Penkala je izumio mehanička olovka i patentirao nalivpero.
Penkala je izumio mehaničku olovku i patentirao nalivpero.
Penkala je izumeo mehaničku olovku i patentirao nalivpero.
Penkala je izumio mehaničku olovku i patentirala nalivpero.
questions.answer: Penkala je izumio mehaničku olovku i patentirao nalivpero.
questions.explanation: Accusative object mehaničku olovku, masculine participles izumio / patentirao agreeing with Penkala.

activities.title: Long conversation: technology and everyday life
activities.intro: Your week-23 conversation: science, technology and your favourite invention. Your partner keeps it flowing and pushes for reasons. Act it out, then keep going with your own gadgets and gripes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Danas pričamo o tehnologiji. Koji je izum, po tebi, promijenio svijet najviše?
lines.en: Today we're talking about technology. Which invention, in your view, changed the world the most?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bez sumnje internet. Zahvaljujući njemu mogu raditi, učiti hrvatski i razgovarati s obitelji u istom danu.
lines.en: Without a doubt the internet. Thanks to it I can work, learn Croatian and talk to my family all in the same day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažem se, ali ima i loših strana. Ne provodiš li previše vremena pred ekranom?
lines.en: I agree, but there are downsides too. Don't you spend too much time in front of the screen?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Iskreno, provodim. S jedne strane mi je koristan, a s druge me ponekad umara.
lines.en: Honestly, I do. On the one hand it's useful to me, and on the other it sometimes tires me out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A jesi li znao da je Nikola Tesla rođen u Hrvatskoj?
lines.en: And did you know that Nikola Tesla was born in Croatia?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam! Rođen je u Smiljanu. Njegova otkrića i danas pokreću naše kuće.
lines.en: I did! He was born in Smiljan. His discoveries still power our homes today.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vidiš kako lijepo koristiš riječ 'otkriće'. A koji ti je najdraži svakodnevni izum?
lines.en: See how nicely you use the word 'discovery'. And what's your favourite everyday invention?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aparat za kavu. Čim ujutro ustanem, uključim ga i odmah mi je dan bolji.
lines.en: The coffee machine. As soon as I get up in the morning, I switch it on and my day is immediately better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tipično! A misliš li da je tehnologija poboljšala naš život ili ga je zakomplicirala?
lines.en: Typical! And do you think technology has improved our life or complicated it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mislim da ga je poboljšala, ali samo ako je koristimo s mjerom. Alat je dobar koliko je dobar i čovjek koji ga drži.
lines.en: I think it has improved it, but only if we use it in moderation. A tool is only as good as the person holding it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je bila prava B1 rečenica. Prije tri mjeseca to ne bi mogao reći.
lines.en: That was a proper B1 sentence. Three months ago you couldn't have said that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam. Zahvaljujući svakodnevnom vježbanju, sve lakše smišljam takve rečenice.
lines.en: I know. Thanks to daily practice, I come up with sentences like that more and more easily.

days.title: Verb family: misliti
days.objective: Master misliti: pomisliti, izmisliti, razmisliti, smisliti.
days.paretoFocus: iz- (invent), raz- (think over), s- (come up with), thinking verbs power every opinion you'll ever give.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack, the misliti family.
One sentence per member; contrast razmisliti vs smisliti.
Say 'Let me think about it' (Moram razmisliti o tome).

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
What's the difference between izmisliti and smisliti?
Review opinion connectors from the writing day.

activities.title: Verb family: misliti, the thinking verbs
activities.intro: Thinking verbs power every opinion you'll ever give. One root, misliti, plus a handful of prefixes gives you 'invent', 'think over', 'come up with' and 'change one's mind'. Learn the family, contrast the near-twins, and say each sentence aloud.

items.hr: Misliti i govoriti nisu ista stvar.
items.en: To think and to speak are not the same thing.
items.note: misliti = to think (imperfective, a process). The base verb of the whole family.

items.hr: Odjednom sam pomislio na tebe.
items.en: Suddenly I thought of you.
items.note: pomisliti = to think of (a single, momentary thought). po- gives that one-off, sudden flash. pomisliti na + accusative.

items.hr: Tko je izmislio ovako glupo pravilo?
items.en: Who invented such a stupid rule?
items.note: izmisliti = to invent, make up (from nothing). iz- = 'out of', so the idea comes 'out' of your head.

items.hr: Moram dobro razmisliti o tvojoj ponudi.
items.en: I have to think your offer over carefully.
items.note: razmisliti o + locative = to think over, consider. raz- turns the thought around in every direction.

items.hr: Zajedno smo smislili odličan plan.
items.en: Together we came up with an excellent plan.
items.note: smisliti = to come up with, devise (a workable idea). Contrast izmisliti (fabricate) vs smisliti (work out).

items.hr: Htio sam ostati, ali sam se predomislio.
items.en: I wanted to stay, but I changed my mind.
items.note: predomisliti se = to change one's mind (reflexive). The prefix pre- signals 'over/re-', a thought done again.

items.hr: Prije odluke dobro promisli o svemu.
items.en: Before deciding, think everything through well.
items.note: promisliti = to think through (thoroughly). pro- = 'through'. Very close to razmisliti; both mean careful reflection.

items.hr: Što misliš o toj ideji?
items.en: What do you think of that idea?
items.note: misliti o + locative = to have an opinion about. Your everyday opinion question.

items.hr: prefiksi: po- (nagli), iz- (izmisliti), raz-/pro- (temeljito), s- (smisliti)
items.en: prefixes: po- (sudden), iz- (make up), raz-/pro- (thoroughly), s- (devise)
items.note: The meaning lives in the prefix. Once you feel these four shades, the whole family decodes itself.

items.hr: razmisliti vs smisliti: razmislim O planu, a onda ga smislim.
items.en: think over vs devise: I think ABOUT the plan, and then I come up with it.
items.note: razmisliti = weigh an existing thing (+ o + locative); smisliti = produce a new thing (+ accusative). The classic exam trap.

items.hr: Daj mi malo vremena da razmislim o tome.
items.en: Give me a little time to think it over.
items.note: Your polite delaying line: da razmislim o tome = so that I can think it over. Learn it as one chunk.

activities.title: Which thinking verb?
activities.intro: The whole family in nine questions, with the razmisliti / smisliti / izmisliti near-twins in the crosshairs. Let the prefix decide.

questions.prompt: What does 'izmisliti' mean?
questions.explanation: izmisliti = to invent, fabricate (out of nothing). iz- = 'out of'.

questions.prompt: You want time to consider an offer. You say: Moram dobro ___.
questions.explanation: razmisliti = to think over, consider an existing matter. izmisliti would mean 'fabricate' it.

questions.prompt: Odjednom sam ___ na tebe. (pomisliti, perfekt, ja, m.)
questions.explanation: pomisliti = a single, sudden thought. Masculine perfekt: pomislio sam.

questions.prompt: Moram razmisliti ___ tvojoj ponudi. (preposition)
questions.explanation: razmisliti o + locative: o tvojoj ponudi. The o-phrase is what marks razmisliti apart from smisliti.

questions.prompt: Zajedno smo ___ odličan plan. (came up with, devised)
questions.explanation: smisliti = to come up with a workable idea (+ accusative: plan). izmisliti would suggest they fabricated it.

questions.prompt: Htio sam ostati, ali sam se ___. (changed my mind)
questions.explanation: predomisliti se = to change one's mind (reflexive, with se). The other verbs aren't reflexive here.

questions.prompt: Ask for time: Give me a little time to think it over.
questions.answer: Daj mi malo vremena da razmislim o tome
questions.explanation: Imperative Daj + dative mi, malo vremena (genitive after malo), then da razmislim o tome = to think it over.

questions.prompt: Prije odluke dobro ___ o svemu. (promisliti, imperativ, ti)
questions.explanation: promisliti = to think through. The 'ti' imperative: promisli (o svemu = about everything).

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Prvo smislim o problemu, a onda razmislim rješenje.
Prvo razmislim problem, a onda smislim o rješenju.
Prvo razmislim o problemu, a onda smislim rješenje.
Prvo izmislim o problemu, a onda razmislim rješenje.
questions.answer: Prvo razmislim o problemu, a onda smislim rješenje.
questions.explanation: razmisliti O + locative (a matter to weigh), smisliti + accusative (a solution to produce). The distractors swap the two patterns.

days.title: Glagolski pridjev trpni, the passive participle
days.objective: Form the passive participle: -n / -en / -t (napisan, viđen, otvoren).
days.paretoFocus: The passive participle is everywhere in written Croatian, news, signs, official texts. ASOO lists it as core B1 grammar.
drills: Learn the three endings: napisati > napisan, vidjeti > viđen, otvoriti > otvoren, uzeti > uzet.
Form the participle of 10 verbs you know.
Note agreement: napisan/napisana/napisano.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Form the trpni of: pozvati, kupiti, zatvoriti.
Review the misliti family.

activities.title: Glagolski pridjev trpni, forming the passive participle
activities.intro: The passive participle is everywhere in written Croatian: napisan, otvoren, viđen, uzet. It turns a verb into an adjective meaning 'having been X-ed'. Three endings cover it (-n, -en, -t) chosen by the infinitive class. Learn the pattern, then the agreement.

items.hr: napisati to napisan; pročitati to pročitan; prodati to prodan
items.en: to write to written; to read to read (through); to sell to sold
items.note: Class -ati takes -an: drop -ti, add -n. The commonest and easiest group. pisati to pisan, dati to dan.

items.hr: Pismo je napisano i poslano.
items.en: The letter has been written and sent.
items.note: The passive = biti + participle. Neuter subject pismo to neuter participle napisano, poslano.

items.hr: otvoriti to otvoren; zatvoriti to zatvoren; naučiti to naučen
items.en: to open to opened; to close to closed; to learn to learnt
items.note: Class -iti takes -en, often with iotation: kupiti to kupljen, platiti to plaćen, baciti to bačen.

items.hr: vidjeti to viđen; donijeti to donesen; peći to pečen
items.en: to see to seen; to bring to brought; to bake to baked
items.note: -jeti and consonant stems also take -en, with sound changes: d to đ (viđen), s stays (donesen), k to č (pečen).

items.hr: uzeti to uzet; početi to počet; čuti to čut
items.en: to take to taken; to begin to begun; to hear to heard
items.note: A small -eti/-uti group takes -t: uzeti to uzet, zauzeti to zauzet, čuti to čut. Short and clean.

items.hr: kupiti to kupljen: iotacija p to plj.
items.en: to buy to bought: iotation p to plj.
items.note: Labial consonants (p, b, m, v) insert -lj- before -en: kupiti to kupljen, ostaviti to ostavljen, snimiti to snimljen.

items.hr: graditi to građen; sagraditi to sagrađen; roditi to rođen
items.en: to build to built; to build (up) to built; to give birth to born
items.note: d to đ, t to ć before -en: sagrađen, plaćen. rođen ('born') is the participle you already know from birthdays.

items.hr: Agreement: pečen kruh, pečena riba, pečeno meso.
items.en: Agreement: baked bread, baked fish, baked meat.
items.note: The participle behaves like any adjective: -an/-en/-t (m.), -a (f.), -o (n.), and plurals -i/-e/-a.

items.hr: Trgovina je zatvorena, a sve je već prodano.
items.en: The shop is closed, and everything is already sold.
items.note: Feminine trgovina to zatvorena; neuter sve to prodano. Read the endings off the subject, every time.

items.hr: Stol je rezerviran na moje ime.
items.en: The table is reserved in my name.
items.note: rezervirati to rezerviran (an -ati/-irati verb, so -an). A phrase you'll hear in every restaurant.

items.hr: Mjesto je, nažalost, zauzeto.
items.en: The seat is, unfortunately, taken.
items.note: zauzeti to zauzet (the -t group); neuter mjesto to zauzeto. The opposite of slobodno (free).

items.hr: Vrata su zatvorena, prozori su otvoreni.
items.en: The door is closed, the windows are open.
items.note: Plural agreement: neuter plural vrata to zatvorena; masculine plural prozori to otvoreni.

activities.title: Form the participle, mind the ending
activities.intro: Pick the ending from the infinitive class, apply the sound change, then make it agree. From the easy -ati verbs up to the iotation traps.

questions.prompt: The passive participle of 'napisati' is:
questions.explanation: Class -ati takes -an: napisati to napisan. napisao is the active (l-)participle, a different form.

questions.prompt: Which ending do -eti/-uti verbs like 'uzeti' and 'čuti' take?
questions.explanation: The small -t group: uzeti to uzet, početi to počet, čuti to čut.

questions.prompt: Trgovina je ___. (zatvoriti, trpni, f.)
questions.explanation: zatvoriti to zatvoren, agreeing with feminine trgovina: zatvorena.

questions.prompt: Sve je već ___. (prodati, trpni, n.)
questions.explanation: prodati to prodan (-ati to -an); neuter subject sve to prodano.

questions.prompt: The passive participle of 'kupiti' is:
questions.explanation: Labial p inserts -lj- before -en: kupiti to kupljen. Same pattern: ostaviti to ostavljen.

questions.prompt: Which participle is correctly formed?
questions.explanation: -jeti to -en with d to đ iotation: vidjeti to viđen. The others skip the sound change.

questions.prompt: Stol je ___ na moje ime. (rezervirati, trpni, m.)
questions.explanation: rezervirati is an -ati verb, so -an: rezerviran, agreeing with masculine stol.

questions.prompt: State a fact: The door is closed and the windows are open.
questions.answer: Vrata su zatvorena a prozori su otvoreni
questions.explanation: Neuter plural vrata to zatvorena; masculine plural prozori to otvoreni. Both take su.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has fully correct agreement?
options: Riba je pečen, kruh je pečena, a meso je pečeni.
Riba je pečeno, kruh je pečena, a meso je pečen.
Riba je pečena, kruh je pečeno, a meso je pečena.
Riba je pečena, kruh je pečen, a meso je pečeno.
questions.answer: Riba je pečena, kruh je pečen, a meso je pečeno.
questions.explanation: The participle agrees like an adjective: feminine riba to pečena, masculine kruh to pečen, neuter meso to pečeno.

days.title: Passive participle in use
days.objective: Use the passive participle in real sentences: 'Roman je napisan 1936.'
days.paretoFocus: Passive sentences let you talk about results without naming the doer, essential for describing art, news and history.
drills: Turn 6 active sentences passive: 'Netko je otvorio muzej' > 'Muzej je otvoren'.
Describe 3 objects around you with participles (kupljen, napravljen, poklonjen…).

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'The museum was built in 1880' (Muzej je sagrađen 1880.).
Re-form yesterday's 10 participles cold.

activities.title: Passiv u akciji, the participle at work
activities.intro: Now you use the participle to talk about results without naming the doer, the language of news, history and art. Turn active sentences passive, describe objects around you, and read the passives that fill every HRT headline.

items.hr: Netko je otvorio muzej. to Muzej je otvoren.
items.en: Someone opened the museum. to The museum is/was opened.
items.note: The passive drops the doer: subject to object, verb to biti + participle. Muzej (m.) to otvoren.

items.hr: Ovaj roman je napisan 1936. godine.
items.en: This novel was written in 1936.
items.note: The dating pattern: participle + year in the genitive (1936. godine). Perfect for talking about books and buildings.

items.hr: Muzej je sagrađen 1880. godine.
items.en: The museum was built in 1880.
items.note: sagraditi to sagrađen (d to đ). Historical dates almost always come out passive: sagrađen, osnovan, otvoren.

items.hr: Zagrebačko sveučilište osnovano je 1669. godine.
items.en: The University of Zagreb was founded in 1669.
items.note: osnovati to osnovan (an -ovati verb, so -an); neuter sveučilište to osnovano. Word order: participle can lead.

items.hr: Ova torba je kupljena u Italiji.
items.en: This bag was bought in Italy.
items.note: Describing an object's origin: kupljen (from kupiti). Feminine torba to kupljena.

items.hr: Stol je napravljen od hrastovine.
items.en: The table is made of oak.
items.note: napraviti to napravljen (v to vlj); od + genitive for the material. Your object-description frame.

items.hr: Ovaj sat mi je poklonjen za rođendan.
items.en: This watch was given to me for my birthday.
items.note: pokloniti to poklonjen (n to nj) = 'given as a gift'. Dative mi = to me; za + accusative for the occasion.

items.hr: Problem je konačno riješen.
items.en: The problem is finally solved.
items.note: riješiti to riješen. A newsroom favourite, alongside dogovoren (agreed) and potpisan (signed).

items.hr: Zakon je izglasan u saboru.
items.en: The law was passed in parliament.
items.note: izglasati to izglasan; sabor = the Croatian parliament. Political news lives in the passive: izglasan, usvojen, predložen.

items.hr: Utakmica je odgođena zbog kiše.
items.en: The match was postponed because of rain.
items.note: odgoditi to odgođen (d to đ). zbog + genitive = because of. Sports and weather news in one line.

items.hr: Sve je već dogovoreno i potpisano.
items.en: Everything is already agreed and signed.
items.note: Two participles for one neuter subject (sve): dogovoreno, potpisano. The tidy 'it's all done' sentence.

activities.title: Turn it passive, catch it in the news
activities.intro: Transform active into passive, describe an object, and pull the passives out of two HRT-style items. Watch the agreement and the genitive dates.

questions.prompt: 'Netko je otvorio muzej.' Made passive, this becomes:
questions.explanation: Drop the doer, promote the object: Muzej je otvoren (biti + participle otvoren).

questions.prompt: Ovaj roman je ___ 1936. godine. (napisati, trpni)
questions.explanation: Masculine roman to napisan. napisao is the active participle; -a/-o would be the wrong gender.

questions.prompt: Ova torba je ___ u Italiji. (kupiti, trpni, f.)
questions.explanation: kupiti to kupljen (p to plj), agreeing with feminine torba: kupljena.

questions.prompt: Muzej je ___ 1880. godine. (sagraditi, trpni, m.)
questions.explanation: sagraditi to sagrađen (d to đ), masculine to agree with muzej.

questions.prompt: Listen to the news item: what happened with the bridge in Osijek?
options: A bridge was closed for repairs
questions.explanation: U Osijeku je otvoren novi most, a new bridge was opened. The passive participle otvoren carries the news.
questions.audioText: Dobra večer. U Osijeku je danas otvoren novi most preko Drave. Most je sagrađen za samo dvije godine, a izgradnja je stajala trideset milijuna eura.

questions.prompt: Listen to the second item: why was the match postponed?
questions.explanation: Utakmica je odgođena zbog jake kiše, the match was postponed because of heavy rain. A new date will be announced (bit će objavljen).
questions.audioText: U sportu: sinoćnja utakmica između Dinama i Hajduka odgođena je zbog jake kiše. Novi termin bit će objavljen sutra.

questions.prompt: Report it: The law was passed in parliament.
questions.explanation: Subject Zakon, clitic je in second position, participle izglasan, then u + locative (saboru).

questions.prompt: Problem je konačno ___. (riješiti, trpni, m.)
questions.explanation: riješiti to riješen, masculine to agree with problem. A newsroom staple.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Sveučilište je osnovano 1669. godine i i danas je otvoreno za studente.
Sveučilište je osnovan 1669. godine i i danas je otvoren za studente.
Sveučilište je osnovana 1669. godine i i danas je otvorena za studente.
Sveučilište je osnovalo 1669. godine i i danas je otvorilo za studente.
questions.answer: Sveučilište je osnovano 1669. godine i i danas je otvoreno za studente.
questions.explanation: Neuter sveučilište to neuter participles osnovano, otvoreno. The date sits in the genitive: 1669. godine.

days.title: Umjetnost, art vocabulary + interview reading
days.objective: Talk about art and read an interview (exam reading genre).
days.paretoFocus: Interviews with stances are an exam reading genre; art gives you rich, opinionated material.
drills: Learn 12 art words: slikarstvo, kiparstvo, izložba, umjetnik, djelo, glazba…
Read a short interview with an artist or musician on a Croatian portal.
Note the interviewee's stance on 2 questions.
Meet Croatian artists: Ivan Meštrović, Vlaho Bukovac, one sentence each.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Describe your favourite artwork in 3 sentences.
Review passive participles.

activities.title: Umjetnost, the language of art
activities.intro: Art gives you rich, opinionated material, and the interview is an exam reading genre. Thirteen words to talk about painting, sculpture and music, plus two Croatian masters to name-drop. Read each line aloud and picture the work.

items.hr: Volim umjetnost, osobito slikarstvo i glazbu.
items.en: I love art, especially painting and music.
items.note: umjetnost = art; slikarstvo = painting (the art form); glazba = music. osobito = especially.

items.hr: Kiparstvo me oduvijek fasciniralo.
items.en: Sculpture has always fascinated me.
items.note: kiparstvo = sculpture (the art); kip = a statue; kipar = a sculptor. oduvijek = always, from the start.

items.hr: U galeriji je otvorena nova izložba.
items.en: A new exhibition has opened in the gallery.
items.note: galerija = gallery; izložba = exhibition (from izložiti, to exhibit). Note the passive otvorena, yesterday's grammar.

items.hr: Ovo je najpoznatije djelo tog umjetnika.
items.en: This is that artist's most famous work.
items.note: djelo = a work (of art); umjetnik / umjetnica = artist (m./f.). Genitive tog umjetnika = 'of that artist'.

items.hr: Na slici je prikazan zimski krajolik.
items.en: A winter landscape is depicted in the painting.
items.note: slika = a picture, painting; prikazati to prikazan = to depict; krajolik = landscape. Na slici je prikazan… is your describe-a-painting frame.

items.hr: Ovaj kip je izradio poznati kipar.
items.en: A famous sculptor made this statue.
items.note: izraditi = to make, craft. kip = statue. Compare izrađen (made) as the passive participle.

items.hr: Skladatelj je napisao glazbu za film.
items.en: The composer wrote the music for the film.
items.note: skladatelj = composer; skladati = to compose. glazba za film = film score.

items.hr: Publika je nakon izvedbe dugo pljeskala.
items.en: The audience applauded for a long time after the performance.
items.note: publika = audience; izvedba = performance; pljeskati = to applaud. Concert and theatre vocabulary in one line.

items.hr: Ivan Meštrović je najveći hrvatski kipar.
items.en: Ivan Meštrović is the greatest Croatian sculptor.
items.note: Meštrović (1883-1962): world-famous sculptor; his works stand in Split, Zagreb and beyond.

items.hr: Vlaho Bukovac bio je istaknuti hrvatski slikar.
items.en: Vlaho Bukovac was a prominent Croatian painter.
items.note: Bukovac (1855-1922), from Cavtat: a leading painter of Croatian modernism. istaknut = prominent.

items.hr: Ova me slika duboko dirnula.
items.en: This painting moved me deeply.
items.note: dirnuti = to move, touch (emotionally); duboko = deeply. Your reaction phrase for a powerful work.

items.hr: Umjetnik je u intervjuu rekao da ga inspirira priroda.
items.en: In the interview the artist said that nature inspires him.
items.note: intervju = interview; inspirirati = to inspire. The reported-speech frame you'll meet in the reading.

items.hr: Po mojem ukusu, moderna umjetnost je previše apstraktna.
items.en: To my taste, modern art is too abstract.
items.note: po mojem ukusu = to my taste; apstraktan = abstract. A ready-made opinion for the interview discussion.

activities.title: Art words + read the interview
activities.intro: Vocabulary first, then a short interview to mine for the artist's stance, exactly the exam reading task. Note who says what, and why.

questions.prompt: What does 'izložba' mean?
questions.explanation: izložba = exhibition. Gallery is galerija, a work is djelo, a performance is izvedba.

questions.prompt: A person who makes statues is a:
questions.explanation: kipar = sculptor (kiparstvo = sculpture). slikar = painter, skladatelj = composer, pisac = writer.

questions.prompt: Ovo je najpoznatije ___ tog umjetnika. (a work of art)
questions.explanation: djelo = a work (of art), neuter. najpoznatije djelo = the most famous work.

questions.prompt: Read: 'U galeriji Klovićevi dvori otvorena je izložba Vlahe Bukovca. Posjetiti se može do kraja svibnja.', Until when is the exhibition open?
questions.explanation: do kraja svibnja = until the end of May. svibanj = May in the Croatian month names.

questions.prompt: Na slici je ___ zimski krajolik. (prikazati, trpni, m.)
questions.explanation: prikazati to prikazan, masculine to agree with krajolik: 'a winter landscape is depicted'.

questions.prompt: Read the interview: 'Novinar: Što vas inspirira? Umjetnica: Priroda i tišina. Slikam najbolje kad sam sama.', When does she paint best?
questions.explanation: Slikam najbolje kad sam sama = I paint best when I'm alone. Her inspiration is nature and silence (priroda i tišina).

questions.prompt: Give your reaction: This painting moved me deeply.
questions.answer: Ova slika me je duboko dirnula
questions.explanation: Subject Ova slika, then the clitics me je, the adverb duboko, and the participle dirnula agreeing with slika.

questions.prompt: Ivan Meštrović was a famous Croatian:
questions.explanation: Meštrović = Croatia's most celebrated kipar (sculptor). Bukovac was the slikar (painter).

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: U galeriji je otvoren izložba djela poznatog slikara.
U galeriji je otvorena izložba djela poznatom slikaru.
U galeriji je otvorena izložba djela poznatog slikara.
U galeriji je otvorena izložbu djela poznati slikar.
questions.answer: U galeriji je otvorena izložba djela poznatog slikara.
questions.explanation: Feminine izložba to otvorena; djela + genitive poznatog slikara ('the works of a famous painter').

days.title: Retelling a film or book
days.objective: Retell a plot with your reactions, a named B1 exam skill.
days.paretoFocus: 'Retell a book/film and give reactions' is a literal B1 descriptor. Practise it on something you love.
drills: Speaking session: retell a film's plot to your tutor or Croatian spouse (5 min).
Use past tense + 2 passive participles (film je snimljen…, knjiga je napisana…).
Give your reaction: svidjelo mi se jer…, razočarao me kraj…

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the same plot in half the time.
Recall art vocabulary.

activities.title: Prepričavanje, retelling a film with your reactions
activities.intro: 'Retell a book or film and give your reactions' is a literal B1 exam descriptor. You need the plot verbs, the past tense, a couple of passive participles and (crucially) your honest reaction. Twelve lines to build tonight's five-minute retelling.

items.hr: Nedavno sam pogledao odličan film.
items.en: I recently watched an excellent film.
items.note: Your opener. pogledati film = to watch a film (perfective). nedavno = recently.

items.hr: Radnja se događa u malom gradu na obali.
items.en: The plot takes place in a small coastal town.
items.note: radnja = the plot, the action; događati se = to take place. Set the scene in the present, then narrate in the past.

items.hr: Glavni lik je mladi liječnik koji traži smisao.
items.en: The main character is a young doctor searching for meaning.
items.note: glavni lik = main character; lik = character. Introduce your protagonist right after the setting.

items.hr: Film je snimljen prošle godine u Hrvatskoj.
items.en: The film was shot last year in Croatia.
items.note: snimiti to snimljen = to shoot/record; the passive participle you need for films. prošle godine = last year (genitive).

items.hr: Temelji se na romanu koji je napisan 1970-ih.
items.en: It's based on a novel that was written in the 1970s.
items.note: temeljiti se na + locative = to be based on; napisan = written (participle number two). A rich B1 sentence.

items.hr: Na početku sve izgleda mirno, ali onda počnu problemi.
items.en: At the start everything looks calm, but then the problems begin.
items.note: na početku = at the start; počnu = perfective present of početi. The narrative turn every plot needs.

items.hr: Na kraju glavni lik donese tešku odluku.
items.en: In the end the main character makes a difficult decision.
items.note: na kraju = in the end; donijeti odluku = to make a decision (present donese). Wrap the plot before your reaction.

items.hr: Svidjelo mi se jer je priča bila vrlo dirljiva.
items.en: I liked it because the story was very moving.
items.note: svidjelo mi se = I liked it (dative experiencer, neuter to match 'it'). dirljiv = moving, touching. jer = because.

items.hr: Najviše me se dojmila gluma.
items.en: The acting impressed me the most.
items.note: dojmiti se + genitive/accusative = to impress; gluma = acting. Point to one concrete thing you admired.

items.hr: Razočarao me kraj, bio je previše nagao.
items.en: The ending disappointed me, it was too abrupt.
items.note: razočarati = to disappoint; nagao = abrupt, sudden. A balanced retelling names one weakness too.

items.hr: Toplo ga preporučujem svima.
items.en: I warmly recommend it to everyone.
items.note: preporučiti / preporučivati = to recommend; toplo = warmly. Your closing verdict, dative svima = to everyone.

items.hr: Ukratko, film koji te natjera na razmišljanje.
items.en: In short, a film that makes you think.
items.note: natjerati na + accusative = to force/make (someone) do; razmišljanje = thinking (verbal noun). A punchy final line.

activities.title: Plot, past tense and your verdict
activities.intro: The retelling toolkit under pressure: set the scene, narrate the turn, deliver a balanced reaction. Watch the passive participles and the dative of the experiencer.

questions.prompt: What does 'radnja' mean here?
questions.explanation: radnja = the plot, the action of a story. Main character is glavni lik, ending is kraj, acting is gluma.

questions.prompt: The film was shot last year. Film je ___ prošle godine.
questions.explanation: snimiti to snimljen (m to mlj), the passive participle. snimio is the active participle.

questions.prompt: ___ mi se film jer je bio dirljiv. (svidjeti se, perfekt, n.)
questions.explanation: svidjeti se with a masculine subject (film): Svidio mi se film. (With 'it/that' as neuter, it's svidjelo mi se.)

questions.prompt: Film se temelji ___ romanu. (preposition)
questions.explanation: temeljiti se na + locative = to be based on: na romanu.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses a DISAPPOINTMENT?
options: Iznenadio me kraj filma na dobar način.
questions.explanation: razočarati = to disappoint. oduševiti = to delight, nasmijati = to make laugh, iznenaditi na dobar način = pleasantly surprise.

questions.prompt: Na kraju glavni lik ___ tešku odluku. (donijeti, prezent)
questions.explanation: Perfective present of donijeti is donese; donijeti odluku = to make a decision. donosi is the imperfective.

questions.prompt: Recommend it: I warmly recommend it to everyone.
questions.explanation: Adverb Toplo, accusative clitic ga, the verb, then the dative svima = to everyone.

questions.prompt: Najviše me se ___ gluma. (dojmiti se, perfekt, f.)
questions.explanation: dojmiti se, with feminine subject gluma: dojmila se. The reflexive se + genitive/accusative object (me).

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Film je snimljen prošle godine i temelji se na istinitu priču.
Film je snimio prošle godine i temelji se na istinitoj priči.
Film je snimljena prošle godine i temelji se na istinitoj priči.
Film je snimljen prošle godine i temelji se na istinitoj priči.
questions.answer: Film je snimljen prošle godine i temelji se na istinitoj priči.
questions.explanation: Masculine film to snimljen; temeljiti se na + locative: na istinitoj priči (from istinita priča).

activities.title: Speaking night: retell a film
activities.intro: Your partner hasn't seen the film you loved, so you retell it, plot, then feelings. She interrupts with questions, as any real listener would. Act it out, then retell a book you both know.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sinoć si gledao film bez mene. Ispričaj mi o čemu se radi.
lines.en: Last night you watched a film without me. Tell me what it's about.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Radnja se događa u malom mjestu na obali. Glavni lik je mladi liječnik koji se vraća kući nakon mnogo godina.
lines.en: The plot takes place in a small coastal town. The main character is a young doctor who returns home after many years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zvuči poznato. Je li snimljen kod nas?
lines.en: Sounds familiar. Was it shot here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jest, snimljen je prošle godine u Dalmaciji, a temelji se na romanu koji je napisan sedamdesetih.
lines.en: It was, it was shot last year in Dalmatia, and it's based on a novel that was written in the seventies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I što se dogodi kad se vrati?
lines.en: And what happens when he returns?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na početku sve izgleda mirno, ali onda otkrije tajnu o svojoj obitelji. Na kraju donese vrlo tešku odluku.
lines.en: At the start everything looks calm, but then he uncovers a secret about his family. In the end he makes a very difficult decision.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nemoj mi otkriti kraj! A je li ti se svidio?
lines.en: Don't give away the ending! And did you like it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jako. Svidio mi se jer je priča bila dirljiva, a najviše me se dojmila gluma.
lines.en: A lot. I liked it because the story was moving, and the acting impressed me the most.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A ima li išta što ti se nije svidjelo?
lines.en: And is there anything you didn't like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Iskreno, razočarao me kraj, bio je previše nagao. Ali svejedno ga toplo preporučujem.
lines.en: Honestly, the ending disappointed me, it was too abrupt. But I still warmly recommend it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro, gledamo ga zajedno u nedjelju. Ovaj put bez tvojih 'spojlera'.
lines.en: Alright, we'll watch it together on Sunday. This time without your 'spoilers'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dogovoreno. Ukratko: film koji te natjera na razmišljanje.
lines.en: Deal. In short: a film that makes you think.

days.title: Writing: describe a picture
days.objective: First run of the exam writing task: describe a picture in 7-8 sentences.
days.paretoFocus: Exam task IV.1 is exactly this: people, actions, appearance, setting, what they discuss. Learn the checklist now.
drills: Pick any photo with people; write 7-8 sentences: who, where, what they're doing, how they look, what they might be discussing.
Use present tense + 2 opinion guesses (vjerojatno, čini se da…).
Check every diacritic, the exam is strict.
Get feedback from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite the picture-description checklist from memory.
Fix your 2 biggest errors and re-write those sentences.

activities.title: Opis slike, describing a picture
activities.intro: Exam writing task IV.1 is exactly this: describe a photo with people in seven or eight sentences. There's a checklist (who, where, what they're doing, how they look, what they might be discussing) and a frame for each item. Learn the frame; the exam rewards the structure.

items.hr: Na slici se vidi obitelj u parku.
items.en: In the picture you can see a family in a park.
items.note: Na slici se vidi… = 'in the picture one sees…', your opening frame. Also: Na slici se nalazi… (there is…).

items.hr: U prvom planu su dvije žene i jedno dijete.
items.en: In the foreground there are two women and a child.
items.note: u prvom planu = in the foreground; u pozadini = in the background. The two zones every description needs.

items.hr: U pozadini se vide drveće i klupa.
items.en: In the background you can see trees and a bench.
items.note: u pozadini = in the background. drveće = trees (a collective noun, neuter singular in form).

items.hr: S lijeve strane sjedi stariji muškarac.
items.en: On the left side an older man is sitting.
items.note: s lijeve strane / s desne strane = on the left / right. Position your people so the reader can 'see' the frame.

items.hr: Žena u sredini drži knjigu i smije se.
items.en: The woman in the middle is holding a book and smiling.
items.note: u sredini = in the middle. Describe ACTION in the present: drži, smije se, čita, gleda.

items.hr: Djeca se igraju i izgledaju sretno.
items.en: The children are playing and look happy.
items.note: izgledati + adverb = to look (a certain way): izgledaju sretno. Cover how the people look and feel.

items.hr: Muškarac nosi plavu košulju i sunčane naočale.
items.en: The man is wearing a blue shirt and sunglasses.
items.note: nositi = to wear; appearance details anchor the description: colours, clothes, objekti (sunčane naočale = sunglasses).

items.hr: Čini se da je lijep, sunčan dan.
items.en: It seems to be a nice, sunny day.
items.note: čini se da = it seems that, for guesses about the setting. Never state as fact what you can only infer.

items.hr: Vjerojatno su na obiteljskom izletu.
items.en: They are probably on a family outing.
items.note: vjerojatno = probably, the exam wants at least one educated guess. izlet = outing, trip.

items.hr: Izgleda kao da razgovaraju o planovima za ljeto.
items.en: It looks as if they are talking about plans for the summer.
items.note: izgleda kao da = it looks as though; razgovarati o + locative. Guess what the people might be discussing.

items.hr: Atmosfera je vesela i opuštena.
items.en: The atmosphere is cheerful and relaxed.
items.note: atmosfera = atmosphere, mood; veseo = cheerful, opušten = relaxed. A tidy closing sentence for the mood.

items.hr: Slika kod mene budi osjećaj mira.
items.en: The picture evokes a feeling of peace in me.
items.note: buditi osjećaj = to evoke a feeling; mir = peace. An optional personal-reaction line to finish strong.

activities.title: Run the checklist, foreground to feeling
activities.intro: Every question is one checklist item: location, position, action, appearance, guess, mood. Build the frame here, then write your seven-to-eight sentences. Every diacritic counts, the exam is strict.

questions.prompt: Which phrase opens a picture description?
questions.explanation: Na slici se vidi… = 'in the picture you can see…', the standard opener for task IV.1.

questions.prompt: 'u prvom planu' means:
questions.explanation: u prvom planu = in the foreground; u pozadini = in the background.

questions.prompt: S ___ strane sjedi stariji muškarac. (lijev, agree with strane)
questions.explanation: s + genitive: s lijeve strane. lijeva to lijeve, strana to strane.

questions.prompt: You want to GUESS what's happening. Which word do you use?
questions.explanation: vjerojatno = probably, for an educated guess. sigurno = certainly states it as fact, wrong for a guess.

questions.prompt: Djeca se igraju i ___ sretno. (izgledati, prezent, oni)
questions.explanation: izgledati + adverb (sretno) = to look happy. Third person plural: izgledaju.

questions.prompt: Čini ___ da je lijep, sunčan dan. (it seems)
questions.explanation: čini se da = it seems that. The impersonal se is fixed here.

questions.prompt: Guess the topic: It looks as if they are talking about plans.
questions.answer: Izgleda kao da razgovaraju o planovima
questions.explanation: izgleda kao da introduces the guess; razgovarati o + locative: o planovima (from planovi).

questions.prompt: Muškarac ___ plavu košulju i sunčane naočale. (nositi, prezent, on)
questions.explanation: nositi = to wear; third person singular nosi, with accusative objects plavu košulju, sunčane naočale.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: U prvom planu su dvije žene koje razgovaraju, a u pozadini se vidi more.
U prvom planu su dvije žene koje razgovaraju, a u pozadini se vidi mora.
U prvom plan su dvije žene koje razgovaraju, a u pozadini se vidi more.
U prvom planu su dva žene koje razgovaraju, a u pozadini se vide more.
questions.answer: U prvom planu su dvije žene koje razgovaraju, a u pozadini se vidi more.
questions.explanation: Locative u prvom planu; dvije žene (feminine paucal); singular se vidi more (the sea, one thing).

days.title: Week 24 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate participles and art; hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: The passive participle only sticks if you produce it live, make it appear in today's conversation.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): art, films, books, with retellings.
Record 2 minutes describing a picture aloud; listen back.
Cold-test the misliti family and 10 passive participles.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Form the trpni of 5 random verbs.

activities.title: Warm-up: art, films and the passive participle
activities.intro: Week 24 closes with the two things that must survive contact with real conversation: the passive participle and the language of art. These twelve lines let you say when a work was made, describe it, and react to it, exactly what tonight's long conversation about films and books needs.

items.hr: umjetničko djelo
items.en: work of art
items.note: djelo declines like selo: G djela, pl. djela. Umjetničko djelo, a work of art.

items.hr: Slika je naslikana 1910. godine.
items.en: The painting was painted in 1910.
items.note: naslikati → naslikan; feminine subject slika → naslikana. Passive with biti.

items.hr: Roman je napisan na hrvatskom.
items.en: The novel was written in Croatian.
items.note: napisati → napisan; the pisati family meets the passive.

items.hr: Film je snimljen prošle godine.
items.en: The film was shot last year.
items.note: snimiti → snimljen: the -iti class takes -en with iotation m → mlj.

items.hr: Izložba je otvorena do kraja mjeseca.
items.en: The exhibition is open until the end of the month.
items.note: otvoriti → otvoren; do + genitive: do kraja mjeseca.

items.hr: Kip je izrađen od bronce.
items.en: The statue is made of bronze.
items.note: izraditi → izrađen (d → đ). od + genitive for material: od bronce, od kamena.

items.hr: Radnja je smještena u Dubrovniku.
items.en: The plot is set in Dubrovnik.
items.note: smjestiti → smješten (st → št); radnja = plot/action, u + locative.

items.hr: Glavni lik je jako dobro opisan.
items.en: The main character is very well described.
items.note: lik = character; opisati → opisan, another pisati-family participle.

items.hr: Moram razmisliti o toj predstavi.
items.en: I have to think over that play.
items.note: misliti family: razmisliti = think over; predstava = (theatre) play, o + locative.

items.hr: Tko je smislio ovakav kraj?
items.en: Who came up with an ending like this?
items.note: smisliti = to come up with; smislio (m. sg. participle). Contrast izmisliti = invent.

items.hr: Svidjelo mi se, ali razočarao me kraj.
items.en: I liked it, but the ending disappointed me.
items.note: sviđati se + dative; razočarati koga = to disappoint someone. Your two go-to reactions.

items.hr: Toplo ti preporučujem tu knjigu.
items.en: I warmly recommend that book to you.
items.note: preporučivati → preporučujem; dative ti = to you. The line that ends every good retelling.

activities.title: Participles and reactions in context
activities.intro: Form the participle, make it agree, and react like a native: from easy vocabulary up to the tricky iotation in izrađen and viđen.

questions.prompt: What is an 'izložba'?
questions.explanation: izložba = exhibition (from izložiti, to display). Otvorena je izložba = the exhibition has opened.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive: Knjiga je ______ na hrvatskom. (napisati)
questions.explanation: napisati → napisan; the subject knjiga is feminine, so the participle agrees: napisana.

questions.prompt: Complete: Film je ______ prošle godine.
questions.explanation: snimiti → snimljen (-iti class, -en with iotation m → mlj). Subject film is masculine → snimljen.

questions.prompt: Complete: Slika je ______ 1910. godine. (naslikati)
questions.explanation: naslikati → naslikan; feminine slika → naslikana.

questions.prompt: Listen to your wife's recommendation. What does she suggest you watch, and why?
options: a film set in Dubrovnik, because the scenery is beautiful
a play about artists, because it is funny
a documentary about Tesla, because it is educational
a film shot in Zagreb, because it is short
questions.answer: a film set in Dubrovnik, because the scenery is beautiful
questions.explanation: Radnja je smještena u Dubrovniku = the plot is set in Dubrovnik; krajolik je prekrasan = the scenery is beautiful.
questions.audioText: Večeras ti predlažem jedan hrvatski film. Radnja je smještena u Dubrovniku, a krajolik je stvarno prekrasan. Snimljen je prošle godine i svima se svidio.

questions.prompt: Choose the misliti-family verb: Moram još ______ o tvojem prijedlogu.
questions.explanation: razmisliti (o čemu) = to think something over. izmisliti = invent; smisliti = come up with; pomisliti = think of suddenly.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'The novel was written a hundred years ago.'
questions.answer: Roman je napisan prije sto godina
questions.explanation: Passive biti + participle: Roman je napisan; prije + genitive of time: prije sto godina.

questions.prompt: Which participle is correct: Kip je ______ od bronce.
questions.explanation: izraditi → izrađen: the -iti class shows iotation d → đ (never plain d, never the perfekt izradio here).

questions.prompt: Complete: Ta je predstava ______ već tri puta ove sezone. (izvesti → passive, feminine)
questions.explanation: izvesti → izveden (consonant stem, -en); feminine predstava → izvedena = has been performed.

activities.title: Razgovor o filmu koji smo gledali
activities.intro: The long conversation that closes the week: you retell last night's film to your wife and give your reactions. Read your lines ('Me') aloud, every one uses a passive participle or a reaction phrase from today's list.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jučer smo gledali onaj hrvatski film. Kako ti se svidio?
lines.en: Yesterday we watched that Croatian film. How did you like it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jako mi se svidio! Radnja je smještena u Dubrovniku, a sve je prekrasno snimljeno.
lines.en: I liked it a lot! The plot is set in Dubrovnik, and everything is beautifully shot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znaš li po čemu je film nastao? Snimljen je prema poznatom romanu.
lines.en: Do you know what the film is based on? It was made from a famous novel.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nisam znao. Roman je vjerojatno napisan davno, zar ne?
lines.en: I didn't know that. The novel was probably written long ago, right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da, napisan je prije skoro sto godina. Glavni lik je jako dobro opisan i u knjizi.
lines.en: Yes, it was written almost a hundred years ago. The main character is very well described in the book too.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To me ne čudi. Ali moram priznati, razočarao me kraj.
lines.en: That doesn't surprise me. But I have to admit, the ending disappointed me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zašto? Meni je kraj bio najljepši dio.
lines.en: Why? For me the ending was the loveliest part.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Predvidiv je. Da sam ja pisao scenarij, smislio bih nešto drukčije.
lines.en: It's predictable. If I had written the script, I would have come up with something different.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha! Onda idemo na kazališnu predstavu, ista priča je i izvedena na pozornici.
lines.en: Ha! Then let's go to the theatre, the same story has also been performed on stage.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rado. A ti mi onda preporuči i roman da ga pročitam.
lines.en: Gladly. And then you recommend the novel too, so I can read it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dogovoreno. Toplo ti ga preporučujem, bolji je od filma.
lines.en: Deal. I warmly recommend it, it's better than the film.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Onda ću ga pročitati pa ćemo razmisliti o kraju zajedno.
lines.en: Then I'll read it and we'll think about the ending together.

days.title: Verb family: raditi
days.objective: Master raditi: uraditi, zaraditi, odraditi, preraditi, obraditi.
days.paretoFocus: za- (earn), od- (get done), pre- (process/rework), the work-verbs family, perfect fuel for the job-market theme.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack, the raditi family.
One sentence per member: 'Zaradio sam…', 'Odradila je smjenu…'
Contrast raditi vs uraditi in two sentences.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
What does zaraditi vs odraditi mean?
Review passive participles briefly.

activities.title: The raditi family: earn it, get it done, rework it
activities.intro: One tiny root, raditi (to work / do), plus a handful of prefixes gives you the whole vocabulary of the working week. za- earns, od- gets a shift done, pre- reworks, ob- processes a topic, iz- produces. Learn the prefix, decode the verb, and fuel the job-market theme that runs through this week.

items.hr: raditi → radim, radiš, radi
items.en: to work / to do (impf.) → I work…
items.note: The imperfective base: the process. Radim u uredu. Što radiš?

items.hr: uraditi
items.en: to get done, to do (pf.)
items.note: The completed counterpart of raditi: Jesi li uradio zadaću? = Have you done your homework?

items.hr: zaraditi
items.en: to earn
items.note: za- = gain/acquire. Zaradio sam prvu plaću. = I earned my first salary.

items.hr: odraditi
items.en: to put in, to get through (a shift, a task)
items.note: od- = complete and be done with. Odradila je noćnu smjenu. = She worked a night shift.

items.hr: preraditi
items.en: to rework, to process
items.note: pre- = re- / over. Moram preraditi izvještaj. And of materials: Nafta se prerađuje. = Oil is processed.

items.hr: obraditi
items.en: to process, to cover (a topic), to till
items.note: Na sastanku smo obradili sve točke. = At the meeting we covered every point. Also: obraditi zemlju = to till the soil.

items.hr: izraditi
items.en: to make, to produce, to draw up
items.note: iz- = out into being. Stolar je izradio stol; izraditi plan = to draw up a plan.

items.hr: Koliko zarađuješ mjesečno?
items.en: How much do you earn a month?
items.note: zarađivati → zarađujem is the imperfective (habitual). mjesečno = per month.

items.hr: Odradio sam pripravnički staž u banci.
items.en: I did my internship at a bank.
items.note: pripravnički staž = internship / traineeship; u banci = locative.

items.hr: Ovaj se materijal reciklira i prerađuje.
items.en: This material is recycled and processed.
items.note: A se-construction: the material is the subject; 3rd person + se = passive-like meaning.

items.hr: Radim od devet do pet, a katkad radim i prekovremeno.
items.en: I work nine to five, and sometimes I work overtime too.
items.note: od… do… + genitive; prekovremeno = overtime (adverb).

items.hr: raditi = process, uraditi = complete
items.en: raditi (impf.) vs uraditi (pf.)
items.note: Radim na projektu = I'm working on it (ongoing). Uradio sam projekt = I finished it (done).

activities.title: raditi and its prefixes
activities.intro: Pick the right prefix for the meaning, agree the past participle, and separate the process (raditi) from the result (uraditi).

questions.prompt: What does 'zaraditi' mean?
questions.explanation: za- adds the sense of gaining: zaraditi = to earn (money). Zaradio sam plaću.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to cover / deal with a topic'? Na sastanku smo ______ sve točke.
questions.explanation: obraditi temu / točke = to cover a topic / points. iz- makes, za- earns, od- gets through.

questions.prompt: Complete the past (male speaker): Ja sam sve ______ na vrijeme. (uraditi)
questions.explanation: uraditi → uradio (m. sg.): a completed action, so the perfective.

questions.prompt: 'She worked a night shift.', Odradila je ______ smjenu.
questions.explanation: Direct object → accusative; the adjective agrees with feminine smjenu: noćnu smjenu.

questions.prompt: Complete: Ovaj se materijal reciklira i ______. (preraditi → impf., 3rd sg.)
questions.explanation: The imperfective of preraditi is prerađivati → prerađuje (d → đ). A se-construction: the material is processed.

questions.prompt: Pick the aspect: Trenutačno ______ na novom projektu.
questions.explanation: 'Currently … on' = an ongoing process → imperfective raditi: Radim na projektu. Uradim would mean a single completed act.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I did my internship at a bank.'
questions.answer: Odradio sam pripravnički staž u banci
questions.explanation: Perfekt Odradio sam, then object pripravnički staž, then u + locative: u banci.

questions.prompt: Which prefix means 're- / over-' (rework, redo)?
questions.explanation: pre- carries the 'across / over / re-' sense: preraditi = rework, prepisati = copy over, prevesti = translate.

questions.prompt: Habitual question: Koliko ______ mjesečno? (zarađivati, ti)
questions.explanation: The habitual sense needs the imperfective zarađivati → zarađuješ (d → đ), not the perfective zaradiš.

days.title: Glagolske imenice, verbal nouns
days.objective: Form and use verbal nouns: čitanje, pisanje, zapošljavanje.
days.paretoFocus: Formal Croatian (news, job ads, contracts) runs on -nje nouns. Recognizing them unlocks written registers.
drills: Form verbal nouns from 10 verbs: učiti > učenje, putovati > putovanje…
Write 4 sentences: 'Učenje hrvatskog je…', 'Zapošljavanje u Hrvatskoj…'
Note the pattern: verbal nouns are neuter.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Form the verbal noun of: plivati, čitati, stanovati.
Review the raditi family.

activities.title: Verbal nouns: how formal Croatian names an action
activities.intro: News, job ads and contracts run on -nje nouns: čitanje, pisanje, zapošljavanje. They name the action itself, they are always neuter, and they turn any verb into a noun. Recognize them and a whole written register opens up, including tomorrow's job ads.

items.hr: čitati → čitanje
items.en: to read → reading
items.note: Formed from the participle stem + -nje. Neuter: deklinira se kao more (G čitanja, u čitanju).

items.hr: pisati → pisanje
items.en: to write → writing
items.note: Pisanje ispita = sitting the exam. Same -nje pattern.

items.hr: učiti → učenje
items.en: to study → learning, studying
items.note: Učenje jezika traži strpljenje. = Learning a language requires patience.

items.hr: putovati → putovanje
items.en: to travel → travelling, a trip
items.note: Note the full -ova- stem is kept: putovanje, not *putanje.

items.hr: zapošljavati → zapošljavanje
items.en: to employ / hire → employment, hiring
items.note: The theme word of the week: zapošljavanje mladih = employment of young people (l → lj in the stem).

items.hr: obrazovati → obrazovanje
items.en: to educate → education
items.note: Ima visoko obrazovanje. = He has higher education. A noun you will meet on every CV.

items.hr: natjecati se → natjecanje
items.en: to compete → competition, contest
items.note: The reflexive se drops in the noun: natjecanje, javno natjecanje = public competition (a job vacancy).

items.hr: parkirati → parkiranje
items.en: to park → parking
items.note: Parkiranje je zabranjeno. the classic sign, verbal noun + passive participle in one.

items.hr: znati → znanje
items.en: to know → knowledge
items.note: A short irregular one: znati → znanje. Znanje jezika = knowledge of the language + genitive.

items.hr: Verbal nouns are neuter and decline regularly.
items.en: bez učenja, u putovanju, sa znanjem
items.note: G -nja, L -nju, I -njem: bez znanja, u pisanju, putovanjem.

items.hr: Bez znanja hrvatskoga teško je naći posao.
items.en: Without knowledge of Croatian it is hard to find a job.
items.note: bez + genitive (bez znanja); genitive object znanja hrvatskoga (jezika).

items.hr: odlučivati → odlučivanje vs odluka
items.en: decision-making (process) vs a decision (result)
items.note: The -nje noun names the process; a separate short noun (odluka) names the result.

activities.title: Building and using -nje nouns
activities.intro: Turn verbs into verbal nouns, decline them after prepositions, and tell the process (-nje) apart from the result.

questions.prompt: What gender are verbal nouns in -nje?
questions.explanation: All -nje verbal nouns are neuter and decline like more/polje: čitanje, G čitanja.

questions.prompt: Form the verbal noun: putovati → ______
questions.explanation: putovati keeps its -ova- stem: putovanje = travelling / a trip.

questions.prompt: Form the verbal noun: učiti → ______
questions.explanation: učiti → učenje (i → e in the stem): learning, studying.

questions.prompt: Complete after 'bez': Bez ______ jezika teško je naći posao.
questions.explanation: bez + genitive; znanje → znanja = without knowledge (of the language).

questions.prompt: Which is the verbal noun of 'zapošljavati'?
questions.explanation: zapošljavati → zapošljavanje = the process of hiring. zaposlenik = employee, zaposlen = employed (participle).

questions.prompt: Complete in the locative: Uživam u ______ po Hrvatskoj. (putovanje)
questions.explanation: uživati u + locative; putovanje → putovanju.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Learning a language requires patience.'
questions.explanation: Subject Učenje + genitive object jezika, then verb traži + accusative strpljenje.

questions.prompt: Which names the PROCESS rather than the result?
questions.explanation: The -nje noun odlučivanje = decision-making (the process); odluka = the decision reached (the result).

questions.prompt: Complete: Sudjelovao sam na ______ mladih pjevača. (natjecanje, locative)
questions.explanation: na + locative; natjecanje → natjecanju. The reflexive se disappears in the verbal noun.

days.title: Tržište rada, job ads
days.objective: Read job ads and respond to one (exam skill: responding to ads).
days.paretoFocus: ASOO B1 covers the Croatian job market; the exam expects you to answer ads in writing.
drills: Learn 15 job words: natječaj, poslodavac, plaća, radno iskustvo, uvjeti, prijava…
Read 2 real job ads on a Croatian job portal.
Draft a 5-sentence reply to one ad.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 10 job-market words.
Say what you do for a living in 3 sentences, formally.

activities.title: Tržište rada: the language of job ads
activities.intro: The Croatian job market has its own vocabulary, and the exam expects you to read an ad and answer it. These fourteen words and phrases cover a whole ad, from the natječaj at the top to S poštovanjem at the bottom of your reply.

items.hr: natječaj
items.en: job vacancy, open competition
items.note: Raspisan je natječaj za novo radno mjesto. = A vacancy has been announced (raspisati → raspisan).

items.hr: oglas za posao
items.en: job advertisement
items.note: Javljam se na Vaš oglas. = I'm responding to your ad. javiti se na + accusative.

items.hr: poslodavac
items.en: employer
items.note: Fleeting a in the oblique cases: G poslodavca, D poslodavcu.

items.hr: zaposlenik / posloprimac
items.en: employee
items.note: zaposlenik is the everyday word; posloprimac is the legal term, opposite of poslodavac.

items.hr: plaća
items.en: salary, wage
items.note: bruto plaća (gross) vs neto plaća (net). Kolika je plaća? = How much is the salary?

items.hr: radno iskustvo
items.en: work experience
items.note: Traži se tri godine radnog iskustva. = Three years of experience required (genitive after the number).

items.hr: uvjeti
items.en: conditions, requirements
items.note: Ispunjavam sve uvjete natječaja. = I meet all the requirements of the vacancy.

items.hr: prijaviti se na natječaj
items.en: to apply for a vacancy
items.note: Prijavio sam se na natječaj. prijaviti se na + accusative.

items.hr: životopis i zamolba
items.en: CV and cover letter
items.note: zamolba = letter of application (also motivacijsko pismo). You send both with a prijava.

items.hr: radno mjesto
items.en: position, post, workplace
items.note: slobodno radno mjesto = a job opening; na radnom mjestu = at the workplace.

items.hr: puno / nepuno radno vrijeme
items.en: full-time / part-time
items.note: raditi na puno radno vrijeme = to work full-time. radno vrijeme = working hours.

items.hr: ugovor na neodređeno / na određeno
items.en: permanent / fixed-term contract
items.note: na neodređeno (vrijeme) = open-ended; na određeno = for a fixed term.

items.hr: Poštovani, javljam se na Vaš oglas za posao.
items.en: Dear Sir/Madam, I am responding to your job advertisement.
items.note: The standard formal opener of a written reply. Poštovani + capital Vaš (polite).

items.hr: Smatram da ispunjavam sve uvjete. S poštovanjem,
items.en: I believe I meet all the requirements. Yours sincerely,
items.note: smatrati da = to believe that; S poštovanjem = the formal sign-off.

activities.title: Reading and answering an ad
activities.intro: Work through a real ad: the vocabulary, the genitive after numbers, the case behind prijaviti se, and the formal phrases that frame a written reply.

questions.prompt: Who is the 'poslodavac'?
questions.explanation: poslodavac (one who gives work) = employer; the employee is the zaposlenik / posloprimac.

questions.prompt: What is a 'zamolba'?
questions.explanation: zamolba = letter of application / cover letter, sent together with the životopis (CV).

questions.prompt: Complete: Prijavio sam se ______ natječaj. ('for/to the vacancy')
questions.explanation: prijaviti se na + accusative: prijaviti se na natječaj = to apply for a vacancy.

questions.prompt: Complete: Traži se tri godine radnog ______.
questions.explanation: The number phrase 'tri godine' + noun takes the genitive: radnog iskustva = of work experience.

questions.prompt: A permanent contract is an ugovor ______.
questions.explanation: na neodređeno (vrijeme) = open-ended / permanent; na određeno = fixed-term.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive: ______ je natječaj za dva radna mjesta. (raspisati)
questions.explanation: raspisati → raspisan; masculine subject natječaj → Raspisan je natječaj = a vacancy has been announced.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I am responding to your job advertisement.'
questions.explanation: javiti se na + accusative; the reflexive se sits in second position: Javljam se na Vaš oglas.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct formal sign-off for a reply to an ad?
questions.explanation: S poštovanjem (Yours sincerely) is the formal close. The others are casual and wrong for a job application.

questions.prompt: Complete: Smatram da ______ sve uvjete. (ispunjavati, ja)
questions.explanation: ispunjavati → ispunjavam = I meet / fulfil; Smatram da ispunjavam sve uvjete = I believe I meet all the requirements.

days.title: Writing: your životopis (CV)
days.objective: Write your own CV, exam writing task IV.2, 7-8 sentences.
days.paretoFocus: The exam literally asks for your životopis. Write it once well now; you'll refine it under time pressure later.
drills: Write your biography in 7-8 sentences: birth, education, work, family, languages, hobbies.
Use exact dates and place names in correct cases (u Zagrebu, 1990. godine).
Use 2 raditi-family verbs and 1 verbal noun.
Have your partner check it.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your CV aloud from memory.
Fix the corrections and note recurring errors.

activities.title: Your životopis: ready-made frames
activities.intro: The exam literally asks for your životopis (CV) in 7-8 sentences. These twelve frames build it: who you are, where and when you were born, your education, your work, your languages and interests. Say each one aloud with your own details, then assemble the whole biography.

items.hr: Rođen sam 1988. godine u Zagrebu.
items.en: I was born in 1988 in Zagreb.
items.note: Male speaker: rođen sam (a woman: rođena sam). Place → u + locative: u Zagrebu.

items.hr: Završio sam srednju školu i fakultet.
items.en: I finished secondary school and university.
items.note: završiti → završio (m. sg.). srednja škola = secondary school; fakultet = university faculty.

items.hr: Diplomirao sam ekonomiju na Sveučilištu u Zagrebu.
items.en: I graduated in economics at the University of Zagreb.
items.note: diplomirati + accusative of the field; na Sveučilištu = at the university (locative).

items.hr: Nakon studija radio sam tri godine u banci.
items.en: After my studies I worked three years at a bank.
items.note: nakon + genitive (nakon studija); tri godine = accusative of duration.

items.hr: Trenutačno radim kao programer.
items.en: Currently I work as a programmer.
items.note: raditi kao + nominative of the profession: kao programer, kao učitelj, kao inženjer.

items.hr: Imam pet godina radnog iskustva.
items.en: I have five years of work experience.
items.note: pet godina + genitive (radnog iskustva), the week's job vocabulary in your own CV.

items.hr: Govorim engleski i njemački, a učim hrvatski.
items.en: I speak English and German, and I'm learning Croatian.
items.note: a here = 'and (by contrast)'. Naming languages is a required CV point.

items.hr: Služim se programima za obradu podataka.
items.en: I am proficient with data-processing software.
items.note: služiti se + instrumental (programima); obrada podataka reuses the raditi/obraditi family.

items.hr: Oženjen sam i imam dvoje djece.
items.en: I am married and have two children.
items.note: Oženjen sam (a man); a woman says Udana sam. dvoje djece = two children (collective djeca, genitive).

items.hr: U slobodno vrijeme bavim se sportom i čitam.
items.en: In my free time I do sports and read.
items.note: baviti se + instrumental (sportom); slobodno vrijeme = free time.

items.hr: Tražim posao u kojem mogu raditi s ljudima.
items.en: I am looking for a job in which I can work with people.
items.note: A relative clause: posao u kojem (locative), impresses on any CV.

items.hr: U prilogu šaljem životopis i zamolbu.
items.en: I am attaching my CV and cover letter.
items.note: u prilogu = attached; slati → šaljem. The line that closes a written application.

activities.title: Assemble your CV
activities.intro: Test the CV frames before you write: gender on rođen/rođena, the locative of places, nakon + genitive, and the instrumental after baviti se and služiti se.

questions.prompt: A male speaker says 'I was born in 1990'. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: The passive participle agrees with the (male) speaker: Rođen sam. A woman would say Rođena sam.

questions.prompt: Complete the place: Rođen sam u ______. (Split)
questions.explanation: Place of birth → u + locative: Split → u Splitu.

questions.prompt: Complete: Radim ______ programer.
questions.explanation: raditi kao + profession in the nominative: raditi kao programer = to work as a programmer.

questions.prompt: Complete: ______ studija radio sam u banci.
questions.explanation: nakon + genitive (nakon studija) = after my studies; it needs a noun, unlike prije nego (+ clause).

questions.prompt: Complete the instrumental: U slobodno vrijeme bavim se ______. (sport)
questions.explanation: baviti se + instrumental: sport → sportom.

questions.prompt: 'I have two children.', Imam ______ djece.
questions.explanation: For a mixed/collective group of children Croatian uses the collective numeral dvoje + genitive djece: dvoje djece.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I graduated in economics in Zagreb.'
questions.explanation: Perfekt Diplomirao sam + accusative field ekonomiju + u + locative Zagrebu.

questions.prompt: Complete: Služim se ______ za obradu podataka.
questions.explanation: služiti se + instrumental; plural programi → instrumental programima.

questions.prompt: Complete: Tražim posao u ______ mogu raditi s ljudima. (koji, locative m.)
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun agrees with posao (m.) and takes the locative after u: u kojem (also u kojemu).

days.title: Speaking: mock job interview
days.objective: Survive a job interview in Croatian.
days.paretoFocus: Interviews force formal register, biography retelling and spontaneous answers, three exam skills in one roleplay.
drills: Roleplay a job interview with your tutor or Croatian spouse (they interview you).
Answer: iskustvo, prednosti/mane, zašto želite ovaj posao?
Ask 2 questions back about the job.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-answer 'Zašto želite ovaj posao?' more smoothly.
Review your CV text.

activities.title: The job interview: questions and answers
activities.intro: An interview forces three exam skills at once: formal register, retelling your biography, and answering on the spot. These twelve lines give you both sides, the questions an examiner or employer asks, and confident, natural answers you can adapt.

items.hr: Predstavite se, molim vas.
items.en: Introduce yourself, please.
items.note: Formal imperative (vi): predstavite se, from predstaviti se, to introduce oneself.

items.hr: Koje su Vaše prednosti i mane?
items.en: What are your strengths and weaknesses?
items.note: prednost = advantage/strength; mana = flaw/weakness. Polite capital Vaše.

items.hr: Zašto želite raditi kod nas?
items.en: Why do you want to work for us?
items.note: raditi kod + genitive (kod nas) = to work at our place / for us.

items.hr: Kakvo radno iskustvo imate?
items.en: What work experience do you have?
items.note: kakav = what kind of; reuses the job vocabulary of lesson 174.

items.hr: Kako se nosite sa stresom?
items.en: How do you handle stress?
items.note: nositi se s + instrumental = to cope with; sa stresom (sa before s-).

items.hr: Gdje se vidite za pet godina?
items.en: Where do you see yourself in five years?
items.note: za + accusative for future point in time: za pet godina = in five years.

items.hr: Spreman sam učiti i preuzeti odgovornost.
items.en: I am ready to learn and take on responsibility.
items.note: Male: spreman sam (a woman: spremna sam). preuzeti odgovornost = to take responsibility.

items.hr: Smatram da sam vrijedan i pouzdan.
items.en: I consider myself hardworking and reliable.
items.note: vrijedan = hardworking, pouzdan = reliable, the two adjectives every interviewer wants.

items.hr: Kolika bi bila plaća?
items.en: What would the salary be?
items.note: A polite conditional question: bi bila (kondicional of biti). kolika agrees with plaća (f.).

items.hr: Mogao bih početi već sljedeći tjedan.
items.en: I could start as early as next week.
items.note: Male: mogao bih (a woman: mogla bih). The conditional keeps it polite.

items.hr: Imate li kakvih pitanja za mene?
items.en: Do you have any questions for me?
items.note: kakvih pitanja = genitive plural after the sense of 'any'; turn it around at the end.

items.hr: Javit ćemo Vam se do kraja tjedna.
items.en: We will get back to you by the end of the week.
items.note: javiti se + dative (Vam); the line that closes the interview.

activities.title: Interview warm-up
activities.intro: Prime your ears and your answers: a listening pass, the formal vi-forms, the conditional in polite questions, and the cases behind nositi se s and raditi kod.

questions.prompt: Listen to the interviewer. What is the very first thing they ask you to do?
options: explain why you left your last job
questions.explanation: Recite nam ukratko nešto o sebi = tell us a bit about yourself, the classic opening request.
