questions.audioText: Dobar dan i dobro došli. Za početak, recite nam ukratko nešto o sebi i o svojem dosadašnjem radnom iskustvu.

questions.prompt: What does 'mana' mean in 'Koje su Vaše mane?'
questions.explanation: mana = flaw / weakness, the opposite of prednost (strength). A standard interview question.

questions.prompt: Complete: Kako se nosite ______ stresom? ('with')
questions.explanation: nositi se s/sa + instrumental; sa is used before words starting with s/z/š/ž: sa stresom.

questions.prompt: 'Why do you want to work for us?', Zašto želite raditi ______ nas?
questions.explanation: raditi kod + genitive (kod nas) = to work at someone's place / for someone.

questions.prompt: A male candidate says he is ready. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: The adjective agrees with the male speaker: spreman sam. A woman would say spremna sam.

questions.prompt: Complete the conditional (male speaker): Kada ______ mogao početi? ('could I')
questions.explanation: 1st person conditional is bih: Kada bih mogao početi? The participle mogao is masculine for a male speaker.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Where do you see yourself in five years?'
questions.answer: Gdje se vidite za pet godina
questions.explanation: The reflexive se sits second; za + accusative of future time: za pet godina (with genitive plural godina).

questions.prompt: Turn the interview around: which line asks the employer a question?
options: Imate li kakvih pitanja za mene?
questions.answer: Imate li kakvih pitanja za mene?
questions.explanation: Trick: three of these are things said TO the candidate or BY the candidate as a statement; only 'Imate li kakvih pitanja…' is a question the candidate can ask back. (In the real interview you invert it: Imam nekoliko pitanja za Vas.)

questions.prompt: Complete the polite reply: Javit ćemo ______ se do kraja tjedna. ('to you', polite)
questions.explanation: javiti se + dative; the polite 'to you' is Vam: Javit ćemo Vam se = we will get back to you.

activities.title: Proba: razgovor za posao
activities.intro: Your wife role-plays the employer and puts you through a mock interview. Read your lines ('Me') aloud in a formal register, masculine participles, conditional questions, the answers from today's list deployed live.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar dan, izvolite sjesti. Za početak, recite nam nešto o sebi.
lines.en: Good afternoon, please have a seat. To begin, tell us something about yourself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Diplomirao sam ekonomiju i imam pet godina radnog iskustva u struci.
lines.en: Good afternoon. I graduated in economics and I have five years of work experience in the field.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zašto želite raditi baš kod nas?
lines.en: Why do you want to work specifically for us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jer cijenim vašu tvrtku i spreman sam učiti i preuzeti odgovornost.
lines.en: Because I value your company and I am ready to learn and take on responsibility.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Koje su Vaše prednosti, a koje mane?
lines.en: What are your strengths, and what are your weaknesses?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vrijedan sam i pouzdan. Mana mi je što sam ponekad previše kritičan prema sebi.
lines.en: I am hardworking and reliable. My flaw is that I am sometimes too critical of myself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako se nosite sa stresom i rokovima?
lines.en: How do you handle stress and deadlines?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro planiram i ostanem miran. Zato uvijek odradim posao na vrijeme.
lines.en: I plan well and stay calm. That's why I always get the work done on time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično. Imate li Vi kakvih pitanja za nas?
lines.en: Excellent. Do you have any questions for us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Imam dva. Kolika bi bila plaća i kada bih mogao početi?
lines.en: I have two. What would the salary be, and when could I start?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O plaći ćemo razgovarati poslije, a početak je moguć odmah. Javit ćemo Vam se do kraja tjedna.
lines.en: We'll discuss the salary later, and you could start straight away. We'll get back to you by the end of the week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala Vam na razgovoru. Radujem se Vašem odgovoru.
lines.en: Thank you for the interview. I look forward to your reply.

days.title: Instrumental of riječ-type nouns + dob/doba
days.objective: Use riječju/noću forms and distinguish dob (f) from doba (n).
days.paretoFocus: Small ASOO items that show up in exam gap-fills: feminine consonant nouns in instrumental, and the dob/doba trap.
drills: Learn: riječ > riječju, noć > noću, ljubav > ljubavlju, sol > solju.
Write 4 sentences with these instrumentals.
Contrast: 'u mojoj dobi' (age) vs 'godišnje doba' (season).

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
What is the instrumental of ljubav?
Review job vocabulary.

activities.title: Instrumental in -ju, and the dob / doba trap
activities.intro: Two small but exam-favourite items. Consonant-final feminine nouns fuse the instrumental into -ju, triggering sound changes (sol → solju, ljubav → ljubavlju, radost → radošću). And two look-alikes must never be confused: dob (age) is feminine, doba (period, season) is neuter and never changes in the singular.

items.hr: riječ → riječju / riječi
items.en: word → with a word
items.note: i-declension feminine. The fused -ju form: reci to jednom riječju = say it in a single word.

items.hr: noć → noću / noći
items.en: night → at / by night
items.note: Radim noću, a spavam danju. noću works as an adverb: 'at night'.

items.hr: ljubav → ljubavlju
items.en: love → with love
items.note: Sound change v → vlj at the boundary: Kuha s ljubavlju = She cooks with love.

items.hr: sol → solju
items.en: salt → with salt
items.note: l + j: Posoli jelo solju = Season the dish with salt.

items.hr: krv → krvlju
items.en: blood → with blood
items.note: Same v → vlj: povezani krvlju = bound by blood.

items.hr: radost → radošću
items.en: joy → with joy
items.note: st → šć: Primili su nas s radošću = They welcomed us with joy.

items.hr: misao → mišlju
items.en: thought → with a thought
items.note: sao → šlj: Bori se s crnom mišlju. (mixed with an adjective, -i is also possible: s tom mišlju/misli.)

items.hr: -ju vs -i: adjective decides
items.en: form depends on a modifier
items.note: Alone, -ju is preferred (s ljubavlju); with an adjective, -i is common (s velikom ljubavi).

items.hr: dob (ž. r.)
items.en: age (feminine, i-declension)
items.note: u mojoj dobi = at my age (locative dobi); srednja dob = middle age. Do NOT confuse with doba.

items.hr: doba (sr. r., nepromjenjivo)
items.en: period, era, season (neuter, unchanged in sg.)
items.note: godišnje doba = season; u to doba = at that time. Same form in every singular case.

items.hr: Djeca te dobi vole crtiće.
items.en: Children of that age love cartoons.
items.note: dob genitive dobi (te dobi = of that age); the collective djeca takes a plural verb (vole).

items.hr: Proljeće je moje najdraže godišnje doba.
items.en: Spring is my favourite season.
items.note: godišnje doba stays put; only the adjective najdraže changes to agree.

activities.title: Fused instrumentals and dob / doba
activities.intro: Build the -ju instrumentals with their sound changes, then keep dob (age) and doba (season) firmly apart, a classic exam gap-fill trap.

questions.prompt: Which noun means 'age'?
questions.explanation: dob (f.) = age (u mojoj dobi). doba (n.) = a period/season (godišnje doba).

questions.prompt: Instrumental with sound change: Posoli jelo ______. (sol)
questions.explanation: sol → solju (l + j) = with salt. The fused -ju instrumental.

questions.prompt: Instrumental: Kuha s ______. (ljubav)
questions.explanation: ljubav → ljubavlju (v → vlj) = with love.

questions.prompt: Complete: Primili su nas s ______.
questions.explanation: radost → radošću (st → šć). Standing without an adjective, the fused -ju (→ šću) form is preferred.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'doba' correctly?
options: U mojoj dobi ima puno posla.
questions.explanation: doba is neuter and invariable: godišnje doba (season). 'U mojoj dobi' would need dob (age), not doba.

questions.prompt: Complete the locative: U mojoj ______ ljudi obično već rade. (dob)
questions.explanation: dob (f., i-declension) → locative dobi: u mojoj dobi = at my age.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I work at night and sleep during the day.'
questions.explanation: noću (instrumental of noć) and danju work as adverbs 'at night' / 'by day'; a = 'and (by contrast)'.

questions.prompt: Complete: Bori se s crnom ______. (misao)
questions.explanation: misao → mišlju (sao → šlj). With the adjective 'crnom', the plain -i (crnom misli) is also acceptable, but mišlju is offered here.

questions.prompt: Complete: Djeca te ______ vole crtiće. (dob, genitive)
questions.explanation: 'children of that age' = djeca te dobi; dob → genitive dobi (i-declension, like the locative).

days.title: Week 25 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate the work theme; hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: You now own the exam's CV genre. Keep it warm while the review locks in verbal nouns.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): work, careers, the Croatian job market.
Record 2 minutes: your work history, from memory; listen back.
Cold-test the raditi family and 5 verbal nouns.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Form riječju-type instrumentals for 3 nouns.

activities.title: The world of work: week 25 consolidation
activities.intro: Everything from this week in one place, ready for tonight's long conversation: the raditi family, verbal nouns, job-ad vocabulary and CV frames, all worked into sentences you can actually say about careers and the Croatian job market.

items.hr: Zapošljavanje mladih velik je problem.
items.en: Youth employment is a big problem.
items.note: The verbal noun zapošljavanje as the subject; mladih = genitive plural (of young people).

items.hr: Zaradio sam dovoljno za godišnji odmor.
items.en: I earned enough for a holiday.
items.note: raditi family: zaraditi; za + accusative (za godišnji odmor).

items.hr: Prijavio sam se na tri natječaja.
items.en: I applied to three vacancies.
items.note: prijaviti se na + accusative; tri natječaja = genitive singular after 'three'.

items.hr: Radim na neodređeno i zadovoljan sam.
items.en: I have a permanent contract and I'm satisfied.
items.note: raditi na neodređeno = to work on a permanent contract; zadovoljan (m.) = satisfied.

items.hr: Za to se radno mjesto traži znanje jezika.
items.en: That position requires knowledge of a language.
items.note: A se-construction (traži se); znanje = verbal noun + genitive jezika.

items.hr: Obrazovanje i iskustvo njegove su prednosti.
items.en: Education and experience are his strengths.
items.note: obrazovanje = verbal noun; prednost = strength (interview vocabulary of lesson 176).

items.hr: Nakon diplome počeo sam raditi u struci.
items.en: After graduation I started working in my field.
items.note: nakon + genitive (nakon diplome); struka = one's profession/field.

items.hr: Šef mi je ponudio napredovanje.
items.en: The boss offered me a promotion.
items.note: dative mi; napredovanje = advancement/promotion (another -nje noun).

items.hr: Razmišljam o promjeni posla.
items.en: I'm thinking about changing jobs.
items.note: razmišljati o + locative (o promjeni); promjena posla = a job change.

items.hr: Poslodavac nudi fleksibilno radno vrijeme.
items.en: The employer offers flexible working hours.
items.note: poslodavac (lesson 174); radno vrijeme = working hours.

items.hr: U slobodno se vrijeme bavim volontiranjem.
items.en: In my free time I do volunteering.
items.note: baviti se + instrumental; volontiranje = volunteering, one more verbal noun.

items.hr: Da imam bolju plaću, ostao bih na ovom poslu.
items.en: If I had a better salary, I'd stay in this job.
items.note: A conditional preview of week 28: da + present, then ostao bih.

activities.title: Work theme: mixed review
activities.intro: Sweep the week: verbal nouns, the raditi family, job vocabulary and the cases that hold them together, with one listening pass from your wife.

questions.prompt: What does 'napredovanje' mean?
questions.explanation: napredovati → napredovanje = advancement / promotion at work.

questions.prompt: Form the verbal noun: zapošljavati → ______
questions.explanation: zapošljavati → zapošljavanje = the process of hiring / employment.

questions.prompt: Complete the raditi family: ______ sam dovoljno za odmor.
questions.explanation: za- = earn: Zaradio sam = I earned. odraditi = get a shift done, preraditi = rework, obraditi = process.

questions.prompt: Listen to your wife. What news does she share about her work?
options: her boss offered her a promotion
questions.answer: her boss offered her a promotion
questions.explanation: Šef mi je danas ponudio napredovanje = my boss offered me a promotion today.
questions.audioText: Imam dobru vijest! Šef mi je danas ponudio napredovanje. Ako pristanem, radit ću na neodređeno i imat ću veću plaću.

questions.prompt: Complete: Prijavio sam se ______ tri natječaja. ('to')
questions.explanation: prijaviti se na + accusative: na tri natječaja = to three vacancies.

questions.prompt: Complete the se-construction: Za to radno mjesto ______ znanje jezika.
questions.explanation: Impersonal se-construction: traži se znanje = knowledge is required. The subject is znanje (3rd sg.).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'After graduation I started working in my field.'
questions.answer: Nakon diplome počeo sam raditi u struci
questions.explanation: nakon + genitive (Nakon diplome); počeo sam raditi; u + locative struci.

questions.prompt: Complete: Razmišljam ______ promjeni posla.
questions.explanation: razmišljati o + locative: o promjeni posla = about a job change.

questions.prompt: Complete the conditional (male speaker): Da imam bolju plaću, ______ na ovom poslu. (ostati)
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: da + present, then kondicional ostao bih (m. sg.) = I would stay.

activities.title: Večernji razgovor: posao i karijera
activities.intro: Tonight's long conversation is about work, careers and the Croatian job market. Read your lines ('Me') aloud, masculine participles, the week's vocabulary put to real use.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako je bilo danas na poslu? Izgledaš zamišljeno.
lines.en: How was work today? You look pensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro, ali razmišljam o promjeni posla. Prijavio sam se na dva natječaja.
lines.en: Good, but I'm thinking about changing jobs. I applied to two vacancies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Stvarno? A što ti se ne sviđa na ovom poslu?
lines.en: Really? And what don't you like about this job?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sve je u redu, ali nema napredovanja. Radim isto već tri godine.
lines.en: Everything's fine, but there's no advancement. I've been doing the same thing for three years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Razumijem. Kakvi su uvjeti u tim novim oglasima?
lines.en: I understand. What are the conditions in those new ads?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jedan nudi ugovor na neodređeno i veću plaću. Traži se znanje jezika, a to imam.
lines.en: One offers a permanent contract and a higher salary. Knowledge of a language is required, and I have that.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To zvuči odlično! A zapošljavanje je danas teško, pa dobro razmisli.
lines.en: That sounds great! But hiring is hard these days, so think it over carefully.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam. Zato sam već napisao novi životopis i zamolbu.
lines.en: I know. That's why I've already written a new CV and cover letter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da imaš intervju, kako bi objasnio zašto odlaziš?
lines.en: If you had an interview, how would you explain why you're leaving?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rekao bih da tražim posao u kojem mogu napredovati i učiti nešto novo.
lines.en: I'd say I'm looking for a job in which I can advance and learn something new.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Savršen odgovor. Podržavam te, javi im se sutra.
lines.en: A perfect answer. I support you, get in touch with them tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hoću. A večeras slavimo tvoje napredovanje, ne moje brige!
lines.en: I will. And tonight we celebrate your promotion, not my worries!

days.title: Verb family: zvati + mock-exam briefing
days.objective: Master zvati (nazvati, pozvati, odazvati se, izazvati) and learn tomorrow's exam format.
days.paretoFocus: Tomorrow you sit your first full mock. Knowing the 5-section format (NN 100/2021) is half the battle.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack, the zvati family.
One sentence per member: nazvati (phone), pozvati (invite), izazvati (provoke).
Read the mock-exam format in the Quiz tab: 5 sections, ≥60% on listening/reading/grammar, pass/fail writing & speaking.
Early night, treat tomorrow like the real thing.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name the exam's 5 sections from memory.
Contrast pozvati vs nazvati.

activities.title: The zvati family: call, invite, provoke
activities.intro: One last verb family before the mock. zvati (to call) takes na- to phone, po- to invite, iz- to provoke or cause, od- to respond. Learn the prefix, decode the verb, and never again mix up nazvati (phone) with pozvati (invite).

items.hr: zvati → zovem, zoveš, zove
items.en: to call → I call…
items.note: The base, with the present stem zov-: Kako se zoveš? = What's your name?

items.hr: nazvati → nazovem
items.en: to phone, to call up
items.note: na- = reach by phone. Nazvat ću te sutra. = I'll call you tomorrow.

items.hr: pozvati → pozovem
items.en: to invite
items.note: po- here = invite. Pozvali su nas na večeru. = They invited us to dinner.

items.hr: odazvati se → odazovem se
items.en: to respond (to a call / invitation)
items.note: od- = answer back; + dative: Odazvao sam se pozivu. = I responded to the invitation.

items.hr: izazvati → izazovem
items.en: to provoke, to cause
items.note: iz- = bring out. Oluja je izazvala veliku štetu. = The storm caused great damage.

items.hr: sazvati → sazovem
items.en: to convene, to call together
items.note: sa- = together. Sazvali su hitan sastanak. = They called an emergency meeting.

items.hr: poziv
items.en: call; invitation (noun)
items.note: The noun behind the family: propušten poziv = missed call; poslati poziv = to send an invitation.

items.hr: Nazvao sam ga, ali se nije javio.
items.en: I called him, but he didn't answer.
items.note: nazvati koga (accusative); javiti se = to answer / pick up. Two everyday verbs together.

items.hr: pozvati ≠ nazvati
items.en: invite vs phone
items.note: Pozvao sam prijatelje = I invited friends. Nazvao sam prijatelje = I phoned friends. Different prefix, different act.

items.hr: Izazov je velik, ali prihvaćam ga.
items.en: The challenge is big, but I accept it.
items.note: izazov = challenge (from izazvati), the noun you'll want when you talk about the exam.

activities.title: Exam briefing: the five sections
activities.intro: Tomorrow you sit your first full mock. Knowing the shape of the exam is half the battle. These are the five sections in official order, the scoring rule, and the conditions you should simulate, straight from the format the app's B1 mock follows.

items.hr: I. Razumijevanje slušanoga teksta
items.en: I. Listening comprehension
items.note: Each recording plays twice; the text stays hidden. Read the questions first, then listen.

items.hr: II. Razumijevanje čitanoga teksta
items.en: II. Reading comprehension
items.note: Genres: a biography, an ad, an interview, plus a word-bank gap-fill. The answer is always in the text.

items.hr: III. Poznavanje gramatičkih struktura
items.en: III. Grammar structures
items.note: Choose the correct form. Dijakritici se broje, č, ć, š, ž, đ count as errors if wrong.

items.hr: IV. Pisano sporazumijevanje
items.en: IV. Writing
items.note: Two tasks of 7-8 sentences, e.g. describe a picture and write your životopis (CV).

items.hr: V. Usmeno sporazumijevanje
items.en: V. Speaking
items.note: A conversation with the examiner: introduce yourself, retell an event, give opinions.

items.hr: Prolazna granica: najmanje 60%
items.en: Pass mark: at least 60%
items.note: You need ≥60% in each of listening, reading and grammar.

items.hr: Pisanje i govorenje: prolaz ili pad
items.en: Writing and speaking: pass or fail
items.note: These two are judged pass/fail against a rubric; every section must be passed.

items.hr: Bez rječnika i bez pauziranja.
items.en: No dictionary and no pausing.
items.note: Simulate real conditions: audio plays twice, but you don't stop it or look words up.

items.hr: Svaki tekst čuješ dvaput.
items.en: You hear each text twice.
items.note: Use the first pass for the gist, the second to confirm details, a key listening tactic.

items.hr: izazov
items.en: challenge
items.note: Treat tomorrow as an izazov, not a verdict: it maps what stands between you and B1.

activities.title: zvati family + exam format
activities.intro: Separate the zvati prefixes, then lock down the facts of tomorrow's exam so nothing about the format can surprise you.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to phone someone'?
questions.explanation: na- + zvati = to reach by phone: Nazvat ću te. pozvati = invite, izazvati = provoke, sazvati = convene.

questions.prompt: 'They invited us to dinner.', ______ su nas na večeru.
questions.explanation: pozvati = to invite: Pozvali su nas na večeru. Nazvali would mean 'they phoned us'.

questions.prompt: Complete the present: Ja te ______ svaki dan. (zvati)
questions.explanation: zvati has the present stem zov-: ja zovem, ti zoveš, on zove.

questions.prompt: 'The storm caused great damage.', Oluja je ______ veliku štetu.
questions.explanation: izazvati = to cause / provoke: izazvati štetu = to cause damage.

questions.prompt: How many of the five sections require at least 60% to pass?
questions.explanation: ≥60% is required in listening, reading and grammar; writing and speaking are pass/fail.

questions.prompt: Complete: Odazvao sam se ______. (poziv, dative)
questions.explanation: odazvati se + dative: odazvati se pozivu = to respond to the invitation. poziv → pozivu.

questions.prompt: In the listening section, how many times do you hear each text?
options: as many times as you like
questions.explanation: Each recording plays exactly twice, like in the real exam, first for the gist, then for details.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I called him, but he didn't answer.'
questions.answer: Nazvao sam ga ali se nije javio
questions.explanation: Nazvao sam ga (perfekt + clitic ga), then the negative javiti se: nije se javio → ali se nije javio.

questions.prompt: Which is a real exam condition to simulate tomorrow?
options: no dictionary and no pausing the audio
you may use a dictionary for grammar
you translate everything into English first
questions.answer: no dictionary and no pausing the audio
questions.explanation: Simulate the real thing: bez rječnika, bez pauziranja; the audio plays twice and you don't stop it.

days.title: MILESTONE: half-way mock exam
days.objective: Take a full mock exam attempt in the app, all 5 sections.
days.paretoFocus: Half-way check. You won't pass everything yet, the point is a precise map of what stands between you and B1.
drills: Take the full mock exam in the Quiz tab: listening, reading, grammar, writing, speaking.
Simulate conditions: no dictionary, no pausing audio (each text plays twice, like the real exam).
Self-grade writing against the model answers honestly.
Note your % per section, the ≥60% rule applies to each of the first three.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write down your score per section.
Pick your weakest section, it gets extra love this month.

activities.title: Before the mock: strategy for all five sections
activities.intro: Milestone day. This is your half-way check, a precise map of what stands between you and B1, not a final verdict. Read these ten tactics, then take the ten-question mixed mock below, and finally sit the full five-section exam in the Quiz tab.

items.hr: Pročitaj sva pitanja prije slušanja.
items.en: Read all the questions before listening.
items.note: You'll know what to listen for on the very first pass.

items.hr: Slušanje: prvi put uhvati smisao, drugi put provjeri detalje.
items.en: Listening: first pass for the gist, second for details.
items.note: Each text plays twice, use the two passes differently.

items.hr: Čitanje: prvo pitanja, pa tekst; odgovor je u tekstu.
items.en: Reading: questions first, then the text; the answer is in the text.
items.note: Don't guess from outside knowledge, locate the exact sentence.

items.hr: Gramatika: pazi na dijakritike, č, ć, š, ž, đ se broje.
items.en: Grammar: mind the diacritics, č, ć, š, ž, đ count.
items.note: A missing dijakritik is a wrong answer. Slow down on spelling.

items.hr: Pisanje: drži se zadatka, 7-8 rečenica, provjeri padeže.
items.en: Writing: stick to the task, 7-8 sentences, check the cases.
items.note: Cover every point asked; re-read once for case and diacritic errors.

items.hr: Govorenje: govori povezano, ne stani na pola.
items.en: Speaking: speak in connected sentences, don't stop mid-way.
items.note: Use connectors (najprije, zatim, zato); self-correct rather than freeze.

items.hr: Ne prevodi doslovno s engleskog.
items.en: Don't translate literally from English.
items.note: Trust Croatian patterns: sviđa mi se, not 'ja volim to' word-for-word.

items.hr: Ostani miran, ovo je polovišna provjera.
items.en: Stay calm, this is a half-way check.
items.note: You won't pass everything yet, and that is exactly the point.

items.hr: Puni ispit uzmi u kartici Kvizovi (B1 mock).
items.en: Take the full exam in the Quiz tab (B1 mock).
items.note: The complete five-section mock lives there, this LEARN + exercise only warms you up.

items.hr: Zabilježi postotak po svakom dijelu.
items.en: Note your percentage for each section.
items.note: Your weakest section gets extra attention this month; the ≥60% rule applies to each of the first three.

activities.title: Half-way mock: 10 mixed B1 questions
activities.intro: A compact sweep of everything B1-core so far (aspect in context, verb families, the passive, cases and the conditional) across the official section types: two listening items, a reading text, grammar gap-fills and one longer reorder. Then sit the full mock in the Quiz tab.

questions.prompt: [Listening] Koje radno vrijeme ima knjižnica radnim danom?
questions.explanation: The announcement says the library is open radnim danom od osam do dvadeset sati = weekdays 8:00-20:00.
questions.audioText: Poštovani korisnici, gradska knjižnica radi radnim danom od osam do dvadeset sati, a subotom od osam do trinaest sati. Nedjeljom ne radimo.

questions.prompt: [Listening] TOČNO ili NETOČNO: Knjižnica radi nedjeljom.
questions.explanation: The recording ends with 'Nedjeljom ne radimo' = we are closed on Sundays. So the statement is false.
questions.audioText: Poštovani korisnici, gradska knjižnica radi radnim danom od osam do dvadeset sati, a subotom od osam do trinaest sati. Nedjeljom ne radimo.

questions.prompt: Choose the verb-family verb: Liječnik me pažljivo ______ i objasnio mi je nalaze.
questions.explanation: pregledati = to examine (a patient); pogledati = take a look, ugledati = catch sight of, razgledati = sightsee.

questions.prompt: [Aspect] Jučer sam napokon ______ cijeli izvještaj. (napisati, male speaker)
questions.explanation: A completed whole → perfective napisati → napisao. 'pisao sam' would mean 'I was writing' (unfinished).

questions.prompt: [Reading] Pročitaj: 'Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić rođena je 1874. u Ogulinu. Pisala je priče za djecu i danas je najpoznatija hrvatska dječja književnica. Njezino najpoznatije djelo je Priče iz davnine.', Gdje je rođena Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić?
questions.explanation: The text states 'rođena je 1874. u Ogulinu', she was born in Ogulin. The answer is directly in the passage.

questions.prompt: [Case] Živim u ______ već pet godina. (Zagreb → locative)
questions.explanation: Static location after u → locative: Zagreb → u Zagrebu.

questions.prompt: [Passive] Complete: Taj je most ______ 1926. godine.
questions.explanation: sagraditi → sagrađen (d → đ); masculine most → sagrađen je = was built. (Never plain 'sagraden' or the perfekt 'sagradio' here.)

questions.prompt: [Conditional] Da imam više vremena, ______ ti. (pomoći, male speaker)
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: da + present, then kondicional pomogao bih (m. sg.) = I would help you.

questions.prompt: [Grammar] I mi ______ rado došli na proslavu!
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo (never colloquial 'bi' for all persons): I mi bismo rado došli.

questions.prompt: [Longer reorder] Put the words in order: 'If I had more time, I would read every day.'
questions.answer: Kad bih imao više vremena čitao bih svaki dan
questions.explanation: Potential condition: kad + kondicional in both clauses, Kad bih imao … čitao bih …; više vremena = genitive; svaki dan = accusative of time.

days.title: Mock-exam error review
days.objective: Turn every mock-exam mistake into a targeted fix.
days.paretoFocus: An error log is the highest-ROI study artifact that exists. Every miss gets a category and a cure.
drills: Go through every wrong answer; classify: vocabulary / case form / aspect / listening speed / diacritics.
Add every missed word to the Words tab.
Re-do the 5 hardest questions from scratch.
Ask your partner about anything you still don't understand.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your top 3 error categories.
Re-answer 2 of yesterday's missed questions cold.

activities.title: Od pogreške do pravila, tipične B1 greške
activities.intro: A mock exam is only worth what you do with the misses. Every wrong answer belongs to a category (vocabulary, case form, aspect, a fixed pattern, or diacritics) and every category has a cure. Read each contrast: the shape that fails, the shape that works, and the rule that separates them.

items.hr: Sviđa mi se ovaj grad. (NE: Sviđam ovaj grad.)
items.en: I like this city.
items.note: Category: fixed pattern. sviđati se puts the liked thing in the nominative and you in the dative: the city 'pleases to-me'. Never translate 'I like' word for word.

items.hr: U travnju idem u Split. (NE: u travanj)
items.en: In April I'm going to Split.
items.note: Category: case form. Months answering 'when' take the locative: u travnju, u srpnju, u prosincu. The bare form travanj is only the nominative.

items.hr: Jučer sam pročitao cijelu knjigu. (NE: Jučer sam čitao cijelu knjigu.)
items.en: Yesterday I read the whole book.
items.note: Category: aspect. cijelu = a finished whole → perfective pročitati. The imperfective čitao would describe only the process, with no guarantee it ended.

items.hr: Nemam vremena. (NE: Nemam vrijeme.)
items.en: I don't have time.
items.note: Category: case form. A negated direct object goes to the genitive: nemam vremena, nemam novca, nema kruha.

items.hr: Bio sam kod liječnika. (NE: kod liječnik)
items.en: I was at the doctor's.
items.note: Category: case form. kod + genitive: kod liječnika, kod prijatelja, kod bake. It always answers 'at whose place'.

items.hr: Idem na posao autobusom. (NE: sa autobusom)
items.en: I go to work by bus.
items.note: Category: case form. Means of transport is the bare instrumental, with no s/sa: autobusom, vlakom, avionom, biciklom.

items.hr: Ovo je žena koju poznaješ. (NE: koji poznaješ)
items.en: This is the woman (whom) you know.
items.note: Category: agreement. The relative koji matches its antecedent in gender and number but takes the case of its own clause, here accusative feminine: koju.

items.hr: Došao sam da ti pomognem. (NE: da pomoći)
items.en: I came to help you.
items.note: Category: fixed pattern. Purpose is expressed with da + present tense, never with the infinitive: da ti pomognem, da vidim, da kupim.

items.hr: Dvije žene, dva muškarca, dvoje djece.
items.en: Two women, two men, two children.
items.note: Category: numbers. dva (m./n.) vs dvije (f.); a mixed group or children take the collective numeral dvoje + genitive.

items.hr: Žao mi je što kasnim. (NE: Ja sam žao.)
items.en: I'm sorry I'm late.
items.note: Category: fixed pattern. Žao takes a dative experiencer (mi) and a što-clause. The same family: drago mi je, muka mi je.

items.hr: Kad sam ušao, svi su ustali. (NE: Kad sam ulazio, svi su ustajali.)
items.en: When I came in, everyone stood up.
items.note: Category: aspect. One event triggering another → both perfective. kad + perfective marks a single completed moment.

items.hr: čaša, žlica, đak, ćevapi, kruška
items.en: glass, spoon, pupil, ćevapi, pear
items.note: Category: diacritics. A missing č, ć, š, ž or đ is a spelling mistake that costs points. Proofread every single word before you hand it in.

activities.title: Ispravi grešku
activities.intro: Each item reproduces a classic B1 slip. Fix it by category, pattern, case, aspect, agreement, number, or diacritic. The difficulty climbs from an easy pattern to the trickiest number form.

questions.prompt: 'I like this song.' Which is correct Croatian?
questions.explanation: sviđati se: the song is the nominative subject, you are the dative experiencer (mi se). 'I like' can never be translated word for word.

questions.prompt: Month as 'when': Idem na more u ______. (travanj → correct case)
questions.explanation: Months answering 'when' take the locative: u travnju. The 'a' in travanj drops out in the oblique cases.

questions.prompt: Negated object: 'I don't have time.', Nemam ______.
questions.explanation: A negated direct object goes to the genitive: nemam vremena, nemam novca. The nominative vrijeme would be wrong here.

questions.prompt: Aspect, a finished whole: Jučer sam ______ cijelu knjigu.
questions.explanation: cijelu knjigu = a completed whole → perfective pročitati → pročitao. The imperfective čitao would name only the process.

questions.prompt: kod + genitive: Bio sam kod ______ jučer. (liječnik)
questions.explanation: kod always takes the genitive: kod liječnika = at the doctor's. The bare liječnik is only the nominative.

questions.prompt: Relative pronoun: Ovo je žena ______ poznaješ.
questions.explanation: koji agrees in gender/number with žena (feminine) but takes the case of its own clause, a direct object → accusative koju.

questions.prompt: Build: 'I came to help you.'
questions.explanation: Purpose is da + present (pomognem), never the infinitive. The clitic sam stands second; ti sits inside the da-clause.

questions.prompt: Means of transport: 'I travel to work by train.', Putujem na posao ______.
questions.explanation: Transport is the bare instrumental with no preposition: vlakom, autobusom, avionom. Adding s/sa is a common English-driven error.

questions.prompt: Counting a mixed group: 'two children',
questions.explanation: Children and mixed-gender groups take the collective numeral dvoje + genitive: dvoje djece. dva/dvije are for uniform masculine/feminine sets.

days.title: Eko-Hrvatska, environment vocabulary
days.objective: Talk about nature, waste and Croatia's national parks.
days.paretoFocus: A 20-hour ASOO theme block. Environment texts are frequent in exam reading and listening material.
drills: Learn 15 eco words: okoliš, otpad, recikliranje, zagađenje, priroda, zaštita…
Name 3 Croatian national parks and say one thing about each (Plitvice, Krka, Mljet).
Give a 1-minute opinion: 'Recikliram li dovoljno?'

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 10 eco words.
Review 3 fixes from your error log.

activities.title: Okoliš i priroda, ekološki rječnik
activities.intro: Environment is one of ASOO's big B1 theme blocks and it turns up in exam reading and listening constantly. Learn these words inside real phrases, the collocations matter as much as the nouns.

items.hr: okoliš
items.en: the environment
items.note: zaštita okoliša = environmental protection; u okolišu (L). Not to be confused with okolica = surroundings of a place.

items.hr: priroda
items.en: nature
items.note: u prirodi = out in nature; prirodan, prirodna = natural. čuvati prirodu = to protect nature.

items.hr: otpad
items.en: waste, rubbish
items.note: odvajanje otpada = waste separation; biootpad = organic waste; G otpada.

items.hr: recikliranje / reciklirati
items.en: recycling / to recycle
items.note: reciklirati papir, staklo i plastiku. reciklaža is the everyday noun too.

items.hr: zagađenje / onečišćenje
items.en: pollution
items.note: zagađenje zraka = air pollution; onečišćenje mora = sea pollution. Verb: zagaditi / onečistiti.

items.hr: zaštita / zaštititi
items.en: protection / to protect
items.note: zaštita prirode; zaštićeno područje = protected area (passive participle zaštićen).

items.hr: klimatske promjene
items.en: climate change
items.note: Almost always plural in Croatian. borba protiv klimatskih promjena = the fight against climate change.

items.hr: obnovljivi izvori energije
items.en: renewable energy sources
items.note: sunce, vjetar i voda. Opposite: fosilna goriva = fossil fuels.

items.hr: smeće
items.en: rubbish, trash
items.note: baciti smeće = to throw out rubbish; kanta / kontejner za smeće = bin / container. Neuter singular collective.

items.hr: odvajati otpad
items.en: to separate waste
items.note: papir, plastika, staklo i biootpad idu u različite spremnike. A daily eco-habit sentence.

items.hr: održivi razvoj
items.en: sustainable development
items.note: održiv, održiva = sustainable; održivost = sustainability.

items.hr: zaštićeno područje
items.en: protected area
items.note: Nacionalni park je zaštićeno područje. Also park prirode = nature park (a lower category).

items.hr: divlje životinje i biljke
items.en: wild animals and plants
items.note: fauna i flora; ugrožene vrste = endangered species.

items.hr: zagađivač
items.en: polluter
items.note: Tvornice su veliki zagađivači. From zagaditi + -ač 'the one who does it'.

activities.title: Hrvatski nacionalni parkovi
activities.intro: Croatia has eight national parks, and the exam loves them as reading and speaking topics. Learn one solid fact about each so you always have something to say.

items.hr: Plitvička jezera
items.en: Plitvice Lakes
items.note: Šesnaest povezanih jezera i brojni slapovi; najstariji i najveći hrvatski nacionalni park, pod zaštitom UNESCO-a.

items.hr: Krka
items.en: Krka
items.note: Slapovi na rijeci Krki; najpoznatiji je Skradinski buk. Blizu Šibenika.

items.hr: Mljet
items.en: Mljet
items.note: Otok s dva slana jezera (Velikim i Malim jezerom) te benediktinskim samostanom na otočiću.

items.hr: Kornati
items.en: Kornati
items.note: Arhipelag od gotovo devedeset otoka i otočića; poznat kao nautički raj.

items.hr: Paklenica
items.en: Paklenica
items.note: U Velebitu, poznata po penjanju i klancima Velika i Mala Paklenica.

items.hr: Risnjak
items.en: Risnjak
items.note: Planinski park u Gorskom kotaru; ime nosi po risu (lynx).

items.hr: Sjeverni Velebit
items.en: Northern Velebit
items.note: Najmlađi hrvatski nacionalni park, proglašen 1999. godine.

items.hr: Brijuni
items.en: Brijuni
items.note: Skupina otoka u Istri; poznati po prirodi, safari-parku i bogatoj povijesti.

activities.title: Ekologija i parkovi
activities.intro: Use the eco vocabulary in real case forms and check your national-park facts. Difficulty rises from a single word to a park detail and an opinion opener.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'waste, rubbish'?
questions.explanation: otpad = waste; odvajanje otpada = waste separation. priroda = nature, zaštita = protection, izvor = source/spring.

questions.prompt: 'environmental protection', zaštita ______. (okoliš → G)
questions.explanation: zaštita governs the genitive: zaštita okoliša. okoliš → okoliša.

questions.prompt: Koji je park poznat po šesnaest jezera i slapovima?
questions.explanation: Plitvice's sixteen linked lakes and waterfalls are its signature; it is the oldest and largest Croatian national park.

questions.prompt: Object of reciklirati: 'We need to recycle paper.', Trebamo reciklirati ______.
questions.explanation: reciklirati takes a direct object in the accusative; papir is an inanimate masculine noun, so accusative = nominative: papir.

questions.prompt: 'air pollution', zagađenje ______. (zrak → G)
questions.explanation: zagađenje + genitive of what is polluted: zagađenje zraka. zrak → zraka.

questions.prompt: 'The sun and wind are renewable energy sources.', Sunce i vjetar su ______ izvori energije.
questions.explanation: The adjective modifies izvori (masculine nominative plural): obnovljivi izvori. The other endings are wrong number/case.

questions.prompt: Build: 'At home we separate waste.'
questions.explanation: kod kuće = at home (kod + genitive); odvajati takes the accusative object otpad.

questions.prompt: Nacionalni park Mljet poznat je po...
questions.explanation: Mljet's landmark is its two saltwater lakes (Veliko i Malo jezero). Canyon climbing points to Paklenica.

questions.prompt: Opinion opener: '______ da moramo bolje čuvati prirodu.' (I think…)
questions.explanation: Mislim da… / Smatram da… = I think / consider that…, the standard B1 opinion opener.

days.title: Besubjektne rečenice & se-constructions
days.objective: Use subjectless sentences: 'Zabranjeno je…', 'Ovdje se ne puši.'
days.paretoFocus: Signs, rules and general truths use se-constructions and subjectless patterns, ASOO B1 grammar you see on every Croatian wall.
drills: Learn the patterns: Zabranjeno je + inf., Treba + inf., Ovdje se + 3rd person.
Write 6 eco-rules: 'Otpad se odvaja', 'Ne smije se bacati smeće u more'…
Translate 4 public signs you remember seeing.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'Smoking is forbidden here' two ways.
Review eco vocabulary.

activities.title: Bez subjekta, Zabranjeno je, Treba, Ovdje se…
activities.intro: Signs, rules and general truths in Croatian almost never name a subject. Instead they use a neuter participle (Zabranjeno je…), an infinitive of necessity (Treba…), or the reflexive se with a 3rd-person verb (Ovdje se ne puši). Learn the three patterns and you can read every wall in Croatia.

items.hr: Zabranjeno je pušiti.
items.en: Smoking is forbidden.
items.note: Zabranjeno je + infinitive. zabranjeno is a passive participle in the neuter, no subject at all.

items.hr: Ovdje se ne parkira.
items.en: No parking here.
items.note: se + 3rd sg = the general 'one'. Same meaning as above, but the sign-and-speech version.

items.hr: Treba čuvati prirodu.
items.en: One must protect nature.
items.note: treba + infinitive = necessity with no subject. Past: Trebalo je čuvati…

items.hr: Ne smije se bacati smeće u more.
items.en: Rubbish must not be thrown into the sea.
items.note: ne smije se + infinitive = prohibition. smjeti = to be allowed.

items.hr: Otpad se odvaja.
items.en: Waste is separated.
items.note: Reflexive-passive: the subject otpad is 3rd sg, the verb agrees with it (odvaja se).

items.hr: Boce se vraćaju u trgovinu.
items.en: Bottles are returned to the shop.
items.note: Plural subject boce → plural verb vraćaju se. The se-passive agrees in number.

items.hr: Ovdje se ne smije parkirati.
items.en: No parking here.
items.note: A very common street sign. ne smije se + infinitive parkirati.

items.hr: U parku se ne pali vatra.
items.en: No lighting fires in the park.
items.note: paliti vatru → reflexive-passive: vatra se ne pali (subject vatra, 3rd sg).

items.hr: Kako se to kaže na hrvatskom?
items.en: How do you say that in Croatian?
items.note: se = the general 'you/one'. A subjectless question you will use daily.

items.hr: Zabranjen je ulaz.
items.en: Entry is forbidden.
items.note: The participle agrees with the noun: zabranjen ulaz (m.), zabranjena vožnja (f.), zabranjeno pušenje (n.).

items.hr: Molimo da ne gazite travu.
items.en: Please do not walk on the grass.
items.note: The polite sign variant: molimo da + present. gaziti = to tread on.

items.hr: U prirodi se ne ostavlja smeće.
items.en: One does not leave rubbish in nature.
items.note: An eco-rule in se-form: ostavljati → ostavlja se. Impersonal, general truth.

activities.title: Znakovi i pravila
activities.intro: Turn each rule into correct Croatian. Watch the three engines: the neuter participle, treba/smjeti + infinitive, and the reflexive-passive that agrees with its subject.

questions.prompt: 'No smoking here.', Ovdje se ne ______.
questions.explanation: se + 3rd sg present: Ovdje se ne puši. The infinitive pušiti would need Zabranjeno je pušiti.

questions.prompt: 'Smoking is forbidden.', ______ je pušiti. (zabranjen → neuter)
questions.explanation: With an infinitive the participle stands in the neuter: Zabranjeno je pušiti. No subject is present.

questions.prompt: Reflexive-passive, singular subject: 'Paper is recycled.', Papir se ______.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the subject papir (3rd sg): papir se reciklira. Plural boce would give recikliraju se.

questions.prompt: Plural subject: 'Bottles are returned to the shop.', Boce se ______ u trgovinu.
questions.explanation: The se-passive agrees in number: plural subject boce → vraćaju se.

questions.prompt: Necessity, one word: '______ čuvati prirodu.' (One must protect nature.)
questions.explanation: treba + infinitive = impersonal necessity: Treba čuvati prirodu. No subject, 3rd sg.

questions.prompt: Prohibition: 'Rubbish must not be thrown into the sea.', Ne ______ se bacati smeće u more.
questions.explanation: Impersonal ne smije se + infinitive = must not. The 3rd sg smije carries the whole subjectless clause.

questions.prompt: Build the rule: 'Waste is separated at home.'
questions.explanation: Reflexive-passive: subject Otpad, clitic se second, verb odvaja agreeing in the 3rd sg; kod kuće = at home.

questions.prompt: 'How do you say that in Croatian?', Kako se to ______ na hrvatskom? (kazati/reći → say)
questions.explanation: Impersonal se + 3rd sg: Kako se to kaže? = How is that said / how do you say that?

questions.prompt: Participle agreement: 'Entry is forbidden.', ______ je ulaz.
questions.explanation: Here the participle agrees with a real noun, masculine ulaz: Zabranjen je ulaz. The neuter Zabranjeno is only for the infinitive pattern.

days.title: Listening: half a news bulletin
days.objective: Step up to half an HRT news bulletin in one sitting.
days.paretoFocus: Your listening endurance must grow before exam prep. Half a bulletin today, a full one within a month.
drills: Write one sentence per story you understood.
Speaking session: discuss an environmental topic with your tutor or spouse.
Re-listen to the hardest story once more.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell 2 news stories from today.
Review se-constructions.

activities.title: Kako je složen dnevnik, jezik vijesti
activities.intro: Before your listening endurance can grow, you need the shape of a news bulletin in your head: how it is announced, how items are ordered, and the reporting phrases that glue it together. Learn these and half a bulletin stops being a wall of sound.

items.hr: informativna emisija / vijesti
items.en: news programme / the news
items.note: Središnji dnevnik = the main evening bulletin on HRT. Slušaš vijesti = you listen to the news.

items.hr: voditelj / voditeljica
items.en: anchor, host
items.note: Voditelj najavljuje priloge = the anchor introduces the reports.

items.hr: prilog
items.en: a news item / report
items.note: Svaki prilog obrađuje jednu temu. Beware: prilog also means 'side dish' and 'attachment', context decides.

items.hr: izvještaj / izvještavati
items.en: report / to report
items.note: Dopisnik izvještava s terena = the correspondent reports from the field.

items.hr: najava
items.en: the opening headlines
items.note: U najavi voditelj kaže o čemu će biti riječi = in the intro the anchor previews what's coming.

items.hr: Prema riječima…
items.en: According to…
items.note: Prema riječima ministra… = according to the minister. A core reporting frame + genitive.

items.hr: kako doznajemo / kako javljaju
items.en: as we learn / as they report
items.note: Connectors that introduce a fact from a source: Kako doznajemo, istraga je u tijeku.

items.hr: u tijeku je
items.en: is under way
items.note: U tijeku je istraga = an investigation is under way. u tijeku (L) = in progress.

items.hr: očekuje se
items.en: it is expected
items.note: Reflexive-passive: Očekuje se poboljšanje vremena = an improvement in the weather is expected.

items.hr: gospodarstvo, politika, sport, vrijeme
items.en: economy, politics, sport, weather
items.note: The usual running order of sections; the weather (vrijeme) closes the bulletin.

items.hr: vremenska prognoza
items.en: the weather forecast
items.note: Sutra se očekuje kiša / sunčano vrijeme. prognoza = forecast.

items.hr: Za kraj… / I na kraju…
items.en: And finally…
items.note: Introduces the closing, usually lighter item of the bulletin.

activities.title: Slušanje: pola dnevnika
activities.intro: Three items are read aloud with the text hidden, catch the fact the way you must on the exam. The rest check the reporting vocabulary you just learned.

questions.prompt: Poslušaj vijest. Što je Vlada danas učinila?
options: Usvojila novi zakon o zaštiti okoliša.
questions.answer: Usvojila novi zakon o zaštiti okoliša.
questions.explanation: The item says the government adopted a new environmental-protection law ('usvojila novi zakon o zaštiti okoliša').
questions.audioText: Dobra večer. Vlada je danas usvojila novi zakon o zaštiti okoliša kojim se građanima olakšava odvajanje otpada. Zakon stupa na snagu prvog siječnja.

questions.prompt: Poslušaj prognozu. Kakvo se vrijeme sutra očekuje na Jadranu?
questions.explanation: The forecast promises sunny weather on the Adriatic, with clouds and possible rain only inland.
questions.audioText: A sada vremenska prognoza. Sutra se na Jadranu očekuje sunčano i toplo vrijeme, dok će u unutrašnjosti biti oblačno uz mogućnost kiše u poslijepodnevnim satima.

questions.prompt: Reporting frame: Prema ______ ministra, gradnja počinje na proljeće. (riječ → D pl.)
questions.explanation: Prema takes the dative: prema riječima ministra = according to the minister's words. riječ → riječima.

questions.prompt: Poslušaj vijest. Koliko je radnih mjesta ponuđeno na sajmu?
questions.explanation: The report states employers offered more than fifteen hundred jobs ('više od tisuću i petsto radnih mjesta'). Listening for numbers is a core exam skill.
questions.audioText: Na jučerašnjem sajmu poslova u Zagrebu poslodavci su ponudili više od tisuću i petsto radnih mjesta. Najviše se tražilo radnika u turizmu i graditeljstvu.

questions.prompt: U informativnoj emisiji, što znači 'prilog'?
questions.explanation: In a news context prilog = a single report/item. It also means a side dish or an attachment elsewhere, context decides.

questions.prompt: Build: 'A weather improvement is expected tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: Reflexive-passive očekuje se; the clitic se is second; poboljšanje vremena = an improvement in the weather (genitive vremena).

questions.prompt: Što uvodi izraz 'Kako doznajemo…'?
questions.explanation: Kako doznajemo / kako javljaju flags a piece of information coming from a source, not the anchor's opinion.

questions.prompt: 'An investigation is under way.', U ______ je istraga. (tijek → L)
questions.explanation: u tijeku (locative) = under way / in progress: U tijeku je istraga. tijek → tijeku.

days.title: Week 26 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate the eco theme and mock-exam lessons.
days.paretoFocus: This week produced your error log, the document that steers everything until lesson 240.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): nature, Croatia's coast and parks, ekologija.
Record 2 minutes: 'Što bih promijenio/promijenila u zaštiti okoliša'; listen back.
Cold-test the zvati family and 5 se-constructions.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-read your error log; check 2 items as fixed.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.

activities.title: Pregled 26. tjedna, okoliš, se-konstrukcije, ispravci
activities.intro: Week 26 on one page. Reread these consolidated sentences aloud: the eco vocabulary, the subjectless and se-constructions, and the error fixes from your mock exam, everything you'll reach for in today's long conversation.

items.hr: U nacionalnom parku se ne smije bacati smeće.
items.en: In the national park you must not throw rubbish.
items.note: Subjectless ne smije se + infinitive, joined to the eco theme.

items.hr: Otpad se odvaja: papir, plastika i staklo.
items.en: Waste is separated: paper, plastic and glass.
items.note: Reflexive-passive odvaja se, subject otpad.

items.hr: Sviđa mi se hrvatska obala jer je čista i divlja.
items.en: I like the Croatian coast because it's clean and wild.
items.note: Dative experiencer sviđa mi se + an eco opinion.

items.hr: Trebamo čuvati prirodu za buduće naraštaje.
items.en: We need to protect nature for future generations.
items.note: trebati + infinitive; naraštaj = generation.

items.hr: Plitvička jezera zaštićena su i pod zaštitom UNESCO-a.
items.en: The Plitvice Lakes are protected and under UNESCO protection.
items.note: Passive participle agreeing with neuter plural jezera: zaštićena su.

items.hr: Da imam više vremena, češće bih išao u planine.
items.en: If I had more time, I'd go to the mountains more often.
items.note: Unreal conditional: da + present in the if-clause, kondicional bih išao in the main clause.

items.hr: Nemam vremena za sve, ali reciklirati stignem.
items.en: I don't have time for everything, but I manage to recycle.
items.note: Negated object in the genitive: nemam vremena.

items.hr: U travnju smo posjetili Krku i vidjeli slapove.
items.en: In April we visited Krka and saw the waterfalls.
items.note: Locative month u travnju; perfective posjetili / vidjeli for completed events.

items.hr: Zabranjeno je paliti vatru u šumi.
items.en: It is forbidden to light a fire in the forest.
items.note: Neuter participle Zabranjeno je + infinitive.

items.hr: Klimatske promjene sve su vidljivije svake godine.
items.en: Climate change is more and more visible every year.
items.note: sve + comparative = more and more; vidljiv → vidljiviji.

activities.title: Miješani test 26. tjedna
activities.intro: One pass across the whole week: dative experiencer, months, se-constructions, negated genitive, conditionals and passive participles. Read each sentence fully before you answer.

questions.prompt: 'I like the Croatian coast.', ______ mi se hrvatska obala.
questions.explanation: The subject obala is singular, so the verb is 3rd sg: Sviđa mi se hrvatska obala.

questions.prompt: 'In April we visited Krka.', U ______ smo posjetili Krku. (travanj)
questions.explanation: Month as 'when' → locative u travnju.

questions.prompt: Reflexive-passive: 'Waste is separated at home.', Otpad se ______ kod kuće.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the singular subject otpad: odvaja se.

questions.prompt: 'It's forbidden to light a fire in the forest.', ______ je paliti vatru u šumi. (zabranjen → n.)
questions.explanation: Neuter participle with an infinitive: Zabranjeno je paliti.

questions.prompt: Negated object: 'I don't have time.', Nemam ______.
questions.explanation: A negated direct object takes the genitive: nemam vremena.

questions.prompt: Build: 'We need to protect nature for future generations.'
questions.answer: Trebamo čuvati prirodu za buduće naraštaje
questions.explanation: trebati + infinitive čuvati + accusative prirodu; za + accusative buduće naraštaje.

questions.prompt: Participle agreement: 'The Plitvice Lakes are protected.', Plitvička jezera su ______.
questions.explanation: jezera is neuter plural, so the passive participle is zaštićena: Plitvička jezera su zaštićena.

questions.prompt: Necessity, one word: '______ čuvati more.' (One must protect the sea.)
questions.explanation: Impersonal treba + infinitive: Treba čuvati more.

activities.title: Duži razgovor: priroda, obala i parkovi
activities.intro: Your speaking session for the week: you and your wife plan a weekend in nature and range over the coast, the parks and ecology. She's a native, so she praises a good form and confirms your case. Read your lines ('Me') aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Za vikend bih negdje otputovala. Kamo bi ti najradije?
lines.en: I'd like to travel somewhere this weekend. Where would you most like to go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sviđa mi se ideja da odemo u nacionalni park. Dugo nismo bili u prirodi.
lines.en: I like the idea of going to a national park. We haven't been out in nature for a long time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažem se. Plitvice ili Krka? Oboje je prekrasno u ovo doba godine.
lines.en: I agree. Plitvice or Krka? Both are gorgeous at this time of year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja bih Krku, želim opet vidjeti slapove i prošetati uz rijeku.
lines.en: I'd go for Krka, I want to see the waterfalls again and take a walk along the river.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Može. Spakirat ću ruksak. Imaš li ti danas vremena za pripremu?
lines.en: Works for me. I'll pack the backpack. Do you have time today to get ready?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nemam puno vremena, ali stignem. Samo da ne zaboravimo vrećice za smeće.
lines.en: I don't have much time, but I'll manage. Let's just not forget the rubbish bags.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bravo, 'nemam vremena', genitiv nakon niječnog glagola. Baš točno!
lines.en: Well done, 'nemam vremena', genitive after the negated verb. Spot on!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: U parku se moramo lijepo ponašati, ne smije se bacati smeće ni paliti vatra.
lines.en: In the park we have to behave well, you mustn't throw rubbish or light fires.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tako je. Priroda je zaštićena, a mi smo ondje samo gosti.
lines.en: That's right. Nature is protected, and we're only guests there.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da imam više slobodnog vremena, svaki bih vikend išao u planine.
lines.en: If I had more free time, I'd go to the mountains every weekend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I ja. Klimatske promjene su tu, ali barem je kod nas more još čisto.
lines.en: Me too. Climate change is here, but at least our sea is still clean.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato ga i trebamo čuvati. Idem po kartu i krećemo u avanturu.
lines.en: That's exactly why we need to protect it. I'll grab the map and off we go on an adventure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znaš što? O ekologiji govoriš bolje nego neki naši političari!
lines.en: You know what? You talk about ecology better than some of our politicians!

days.title: Verb family: stati
days.objective: Master stati: postati, ostati, prestati, pristati, ustati.
days.paretoFocus: po- (become), o- (stay), pre- (stop), pri- (agree), u- (get up), five essential meanings from one tiny root.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack, the stati family.
One sentence per member: 'Prestao sam pušiti', 'Postala je liječnica'…
Note the presents: postanem, ostanem, prestanem.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Contrast prestati vs ostati.
Say 'I get up at 7' (Ustajem u 7).

activities.title: Obitelj glagola: stati
activities.intro: One tiny root, stati, and a fistful of prefixes give you five essential everyday verbs, plus a few more. Each prefix carries a predictable meaning: po- (become), o- (stay), pre- (stop), pri- (agree), u- (get up). Learn the family and note the present forms, which all follow the -stanem pattern.

items.hr: stati
items.en: to stop; to stand; to fit
items.note: Present: stanem. Autobus je stao na stanici. / Sve stane u torbu (it all fits).

items.hr: postati
items.en: to become, po-
items.note: Postala je liječnica. Present: postanem. Takes a nominative complement: postati liječnik.

items.hr: ostati
items.en: to stay, remain, o-
items.note: Ostao sam kod kuće cijeli dan. Present: ostanem. Impf ostajati/ostajem.

items.hr: prestati
items.en: to stop (doing), pre-
items.note: Prestao sam pušiti. Takes an imperfective infinitive: prestati + inf. Present: prestanem.

items.hr: pristati
items.en: to agree, consent; (of a ship) to dock, pri-
items.note: Pristao je na dogovor (pristati na + A). Brod je pristao u luku. Present: pristanem.

items.hr: ustati
items.en: to get up, stand up, u-
items.note: Ustao sam u sedam. Present: ustanem. Habitual impf ustajati/ustajem: Ustajem u sedam.

items.hr: nastati
items.en: to arise, come into being, na-
items.note: Nastao je problem. Nastala je tišina. Present: nastanem (rare); mostly 3rd person.

items.hr: sastati se
items.en: to meet up, s(a)-
items.note: Sastajemo se u petak. pf sastati se, present sastanem se; impf sastajati se.

items.hr: rastati se
items.en: to part, split up, raz-
items.note: Rastali su se nakon deset godina braka. rastati se s + instrumental.

items.hr: zastati
items.en: to pause, halt briefly, za-
items.note: Zastao je na trenutak i onda nastavio. za- adds a short, momentary stop.

items.hr: postajati / ostajati / prestajati
items.en: the secondary imperfectives
items.note: For habits and processes: postajem sve bolji, ostajem doma petkom, kiša prestaje.

items.hr: stajati (stojim)
items.en: to stand; to cost, a different verb!
items.note: Careful: stajati/stojim = to be standing, and Koliko stoji? = How much does it cost? Not the same as stati/stanem.

activities.title: Prefiksi obitelji stati
activities.intro: Drop the right member of the stati family into each sentence, and mind the present forms of the -stanem class. The final questions test the trap between stati and stajati.

questions.prompt: 'She became a doctor.', ______ je liječnica.
questions.explanation: po- marks a change of state: postati = to become → Postala je liječnica.

questions.prompt: 'I stopped smoking.', ______ sam pušiti. (prestati → male)
questions.explanation: pre- = cease; prestati + imperfective infinitive: Prestao sam pušiti.

questions.prompt: 'I stayed home.', ______ sam kod kuće.
questions.explanation: o- = remain: ostati → Ostao sam kod kuće. Postao = became, Ustao = got up, Nastao = arose.

questions.prompt: Which member is followed by an infinitive (e.g. '…pušiti')?
questions.explanation: prestati takes an imperfective infinitive: prestati pušiti, prestati raditi. The others don't pattern that way.

questions.prompt: Habit: 'I get up at seven.', ______ u sedam. (ustajati → 1st sg.)
questions.explanation: A daily habit → imperfective ustajati → Ustajem. The perfective ustanem would need a single occasion.

questions.prompt: 'He agreed to the deal.', Pristao je ______ dogovor.
questions.explanation: pristati na + accusative: pristati na dogovor / na prijedlog = to agree to something.

questions.prompt: Build: 'A problem arose and silence fell.'
questions.answer: Nastao je problem i nastala je tišina
questions.explanation: nastati = to arise/come into being; the participle agrees: nastao problem (m.), nastala tišina (f.). The clitic je stands second in each clause.

questions.prompt: The look-alike verb: 'How much does it cost?', Koliko ______?
questions.explanation: 'to cost' is stajati/stojim, not stati/stanem: Koliko stoji? = How much does it cost / stand at?

questions.prompt: Perfective present in a čim-clause: 'As soon as you get up, let me know.', Čim ______, javi mi se. (ustati → 2nd sg.)
questions.explanation: The perfective present of ustati follows the -stanem class: ustanem, ustaneš… Čim ustaneš = as soon as you get up.

days.title: Dative experiencer, nositelj stanja u dativu
days.objective: Say how you feel the Croatian way: Hladno mi je, Muka mi je, Sviđa mi se.
days.paretoFocus: Croatian puts the feeler in the dative. This one pattern unlocks dozens of everyday feelings-sentences.
drills: Learn the pattern: dative + je: Hladno mi je, Dosadno joj je, Žao nam je.
Learn sviđati se and nedostajati with the dative.
Write 8 sentences about how family members feel today.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'I miss the sea' (Nedostaje mi more).
Review the stati family.

activities.title: Nositelj stanja u dativu, kako se osjećaš
activities.intro: Croatian puts the person who feels something in the dative, while the state or the thing is the grammatical subject. Master this one pattern and dozens of everyday feelings-sentences fall into place. Watch the verb agreement: it follows the nominative subject, not you.

items.hr: Hladno mi je.
items.en: I'm cold.
items.note: adverb (n.) + dative: literally 'cold is to-me'. Never 'Ja sam hladan' for feeling cold.

items.hr: Vruće mi je. / Toplo mi je.
items.en: I'm hot. / I'm warm.
items.note: Same frame. Past: Bilo mi je vruće = I was hot.

items.hr: Dosadno mi je.
items.en: I'm bored.
items.note: Dosadno joj je = she's bored; Dosadno nam je = we're bored.

items.hr: Muka mi je. / Zlo mi je.
items.en: I feel sick.
items.note: Muka mi je od masne hrane = greasy food makes me sick (od + genitive = from).

items.hr: Žao mi je.
items.en: I'm sorry.
items.note: Žao mi je što kasnim = I'm sorry I'm late. Takes a što-clause.

items.hr: Drago mi je.
items.en: Pleased to meet you / I'm glad.
items.note: Drago mi je što si došao = I'm glad you came.

items.hr: Sviđa mi se…
items.en: I like…
items.note: The verb agrees with the subject: Sviđa mi se film (sg.), Sviđaju mi se filmovi (pl.).

items.hr: Nedostaje mi…
items.en: I miss…
items.note: Nedostaje mi more (sg.); Nedostaju mi prijatelji (pl. verb). It agrees with what is missed.

items.hr: Treba mi…
items.en: I need…
items.note: Treba mi pomoć (sg.); Trebaju mi cipele (pl.). The needed thing is the nominative subject.

items.hr: Prija mi…
items.en: … does me good / agrees with me
items.note: Prija mi topli čaj. Teška hrana mi ne prija = heavy food doesn't sit well with me.

items.hr: Ne da mi se…
items.en: I don't feel like…
items.note: Ne da mi se učiti = I don't feel like studying. Very common in speech; + infinitive.

items.hr: Vrti mi se (u glavi).
items.en: I'm dizzy.
items.note: A physical state in the same dative frame: literally 'it spins to-me'.

activities.title: Osjećaji u dativu
activities.intro: Build feelings the Croatian way. The trickiest questions test verb agreement: sviđati se, nedostajati and trebati all agree with the nominative subject, not with you.

questions.prompt: 'I'm cold.', ______ mi je.
questions.explanation: The frame uses the neuter adverb + dative: Hladno mi je. 'Ja sam hladan' would describe you as cold to the touch.

questions.prompt: 'I like this song.', ______ mi se ova pjesma. (sviđati → 3rd sg.)
questions.explanation: The subject pjesma is singular → Sviđa mi se ova pjesma.

questions.prompt: Agreement: 'I like these songs.', ______ mi se ove pjesme.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the plural subject pjesme: Sviđaju mi se ove pjesme.

questions.prompt: 'I miss the sea.', ______ mi more.
questions.explanation: more is singular → Nedostaje mi more. Plural (prijatelji) would give Nedostaju mi.

questions.prompt: 'I need help.', ______ mi pomoć. (trebati → 3rd sg.)
questions.explanation: The needed thing pomoć is the singular subject: Treba mi pomoć. Plural cipele → Trebaju mi.

questions.prompt: 'I feel sick from the greasy food.', Muka mi je ______ masne hrane.
questions.explanation: od + genitive marks the cause: Muka mi je od masne hrane = the greasy food makes me sick.

questions.prompt: Build: 'I'm sorry I'm late.'
questions.explanation: Žao + dative (mi) + je, then a što-clause: Žao mi je što kasnim.

questions.prompt: Agreement: 'I need new shoes.', ______ mi nove cipele.
questions.explanation: cipele is plural, so the verb is plural: Trebaju mi nove cipele.

questions.prompt: 'The warm tea does me good.', Topli čaj mi ______. (prijati → 3rd sg.)
questions.explanation: prijati agrees with the subject čaj: Topli čaj mi prija = the warm tea agrees with me.

activities.title: Kako se danas osjećamo?
activities.intro: A morning check-in at home. Your wife asks how everyone feels and you answer with the dative frame all the way through. She corrects one agreement slip and praises the rest. Read your lines ('Me') aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro jutro! Kako si spavao? Izgledaš pomalo umorno.
lines.en: Good morning! How did you sleep? You look a bit tired.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Iskreno, malo mi je hladno i uopće mi se nije dalo ustati.
lines.en: Honestly, I'm a little cold and I didn't feel like getting up at all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Skuhat ću ti čaj, topli čaj ti uvijek prija. A kako je mala?
lines.en: I'll make you tea, warm tea always does you good. And how's the little one?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Njoj je super. Nije joj hladno i nikad joj nije dosadno.
lines.en: She's great. She's not cold and she's never bored.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Haha, djeca su takva. A tebi? Nedostaju li ti tvoji iz Amerike?
lines.en: Haha, kids are like that. And you? Do you miss your family in America?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, nedostaju mi roditelji. I nedostaje mi ono naše sunce.
lines.en: Yes, I miss my parents. And I miss that sun of ours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lijepo rečeno, 'nedostaju mi roditelji', množina. A i 'nedostaje mi sunce'. Bravo!
lines.en: Nicely put, 'nedostaju mi roditelji', plural. And 'nedostaje mi sunce' too. Well done!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trudim se. Sviđa mi se ovi mali izrazi u dativu, iako mi je isprva bilo teško.
lines.en: I'm trying. I like these little dative expressions, even though it was hard for me at first.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Samo sitnica: 'sviđaju mi se ovi izrazi', množina. Glagol se slaže s onim što ti se sviđa.
lines.en: Just a small thing: 'sviđaju mi se ovi izrazi', plural. The verb agrees with what you like.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Točno, izrazi su u množini pa i glagol mora biti. Sviđaju mi se!
lines.en: Right, the expressions are plural, so the verb must be too. I like them!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Eto, sad savršeno. Treba li ti još što? Aspirin, malo vode?
lines.en: There, perfect now. Do you need anything else? An aspirin, some water?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Samo mi treba tvoja kava i bit ću novi čovjek.
lines.en: I just need your coffee and I'll be a new man.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vidiš, kad ti nije dobro, dativ ti savršeno sjeda!
lines.en: See, when you're not feeling well, the dative fits you perfectly!

days.title: Ono smo što jedemo, food & health reading
days.objective: Read a health/nutrition article, the exam's gap-fill text genre.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Reading B is a ~150-word health-style article with a 12-word bank. Learn the genre's vocabulary now.
drills: Learn 15 food/health words: prehrana, namirnice, zdravlje, pretilost, vitamini, navike…
Read a short nutrition article on a Croatian portal.
Summarize its advice in 3 sentences.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 10 food/health words.
Make 3 dative-experiencer sentences about food (Muka mi je od…).

activities.title: Prehrana i zdravlje, rječnik
activities.intro: Exam Reading B is a short health-and-nutrition article with a word bank, so this genre's vocabulary is worth its weight in points. Learn each word inside a natural collocation, that's how it will appear in the text.

items.hr: prehrana
items.en: diet, nutrition
items.note: zdrava prehrana = healthy diet; prehrambene navike = eating habits.

items.hr: namirnice
items.en: groceries, foodstuffs
items.note: svježe namirnice; kupiti namirnice. Plural noun.

items.hr: zdravlje
items.en: health
items.note: brinuti o zdravlju (o + L); na zdravlje! = cheers / bless you.

items.hr: pretilost
items.en: obesity
items.note: problem pretilosti; pretio, pretila = obese. Feminine i-declension noun.

items.hr: vitamini i minerali
items.en: vitamins and minerals
items.note: Voće je bogato vitaminima (bogat + instrumental).

items.hr: masnoća / mast
items.en: fat
items.note: zasićene masnoće = saturated fats; previše masti šteti zdravlju.

items.hr: šećer
items.en: sugar
items.note: Previše šećera šteti zdravlju. G šećera.

items.hr: vlakna
items.en: fibre
items.note: Cjelovite žitarice sadrže vlakna = wholegrains contain fibre. Neuter plural.

items.hr: probava
items.en: digestion
items.note: dobra probava; probavni sustav = the digestive system.

items.hr: uravnotežena prehrana
items.en: a balanced diet
items.note: ravnoteža = balance; uravnotežen = balanced (a passive participle).

items.hr: obrok / porcija
items.en: meal / portion
items.note: pet obroka dnevno; umjerena porcija = a moderate portion.

items.hr: štetiti / koristiti (+ D)
items.en: to harm / to benefit
items.note: Pušenje šteti zdravlju; voće koristi zdravlju. Both take the dative.

items.hr: unos
items.en: intake
items.note: dnevni unos kalorija / soli = daily intake of calories / salt.

items.hr: umjerenost
items.en: moderation
items.note: Ključ je u umjerenosti = the key is in moderation. umjeren = moderate.

activities.title: Članak: 'Ono smo što jedemo'
activities.intro: A short nutrition article in the exact register of the exam text. Read it line by line; the notes gloss only the harder words and constructions. Try to guess before you look.

items.hr: Sve više stručnjaka upozorava da su naše prehrambene navike sve lošije.
items.en: More and more experts warn that our eating habits are getting worse.
items.note: upozoravati = to warn; sve lošije = worse and worse (sve + comparative).

items.hr: Brza hrana bogata je mastima i šećerom, a siromašna vlaknima.
items.en: Fast food is rich in fats and sugar, but poor in fibre.
items.note: bogat + instrumental / siromašan + instrumental. mastima, šećerom, vlaknima.

items.hr: Zbog toga raste broj ljudi koji imaju problema s pretilošću.
items.en: Because of that, the number of people with obesity problems is rising.
items.note: imati problema (G) s + instrumental (pretilošću, i-declension).

items.hr: Liječnici savjetuju da jedemo više voća i povrća.
items.en: Doctors advise that we eat more fruit and vegetables.
items.note: savjetovati da + present; više + genitive (voća, povrća).

items.hr: Uravnotežena prehrana znači ravnotežu bjelančevina, ugljikohidrata i masti.
items.en: A balanced diet means a balance of proteins, carbohydrates and fats.
items.note: bjelančevine = proteins; ugljikohidrati = carbohydrates.

items.hr: Važno je i piti dovoljno vode tijekom dana.
items.en: It's also important to drink enough water during the day.
items.note: Subjectless Važno je + infinitive; dovoljno + genitive (vode).

items.hr: Nije se potrebno potpuno odreći slatkoga; dovoljna je umjerenost.
items.en: It's not necessary to give up sweets entirely; moderation is enough.
items.note: odreći se + genitive (slatkoga); umjerenost = moderation.

items.hr: Male promjene, poput manje soli, s vremenom donose velike rezultate.
items.en: Small changes, like less salt, bring big results over time.
items.note: poput + genitive = like; s vremenom = over time.

items.hr: Zdravlje se ne kupuje u ljekarni, nego se gradi za stolom.
items.en: Health isn't bought at the pharmacy, but built at the table.
items.note: Reflexive-passive (kupuje se, gradi se); nije… nego = not… but rather.

items.hr: Ono smo što jedemo, stara izreka koja i danas vrijedi.
items.en: We are what we eat, an old saying that still holds today.
items.note: izreka = saying; vrijediti = to hold true, be valid.

activities.title: Razumijevanje i rječnik prehrane
activities.intro: Show you understood the article and can handle the collocations that carry the vocabulary, bogat + instrumental, odreći se + genitive, subjectless Važno je. Difficulty rises to the article's overall message.

questions.prompt: What does 'namirnice' mean?
questions.explanation: namirnice = groceries / foodstuffs: kupiti namirnice = to buy groceries.

questions.prompt: 'a healthy diet', zdrava ______. (prehrana)
questions.explanation: prehrana = diet/nutrition; zdrava prehrana = a healthy diet.

questions.prompt: Prema članku, brza hrana bogata je...
questions.explanation: The text says fast food is 'bogata mastima i šećerom, a siromašna vlaknima', rich in fats and sugar, poor in fibre.

questions.prompt: Što liječnici savjetuju?
options: Da jedemo više voća i povrća.
questions.answer: Da jedemo više voća i povrća.
questions.explanation: 'Liječnici savjetuju da jedemo više voća i povrća.' The article never demands giving up sweets entirely.

questions.prompt: bogat + instrumental: 'Fruit is rich in vitamins.', Voće je bogato ______. (vitamin → instr. pl.)
questions.explanation: bogat governs the instrumental: bogato vitaminima. vitamin → vitaminima.

questions.prompt: odreći se + case: 'It's not easy to give up sweets.', Nije se lako odreći ______.
questions.explanation: odreći se takes the genitive: odreći se slatkoga = to give up sweet things.

questions.prompt: Build: 'It's important to drink enough water.'
questions.explanation: Subjectless Važno je + infinitive piti; dovoljno + genitive vode = enough water.

questions.prompt: Koja je glavna poruka članka?
options: Zdravlje ovisi o skupim dodacima prehrani.
Zdravlje se gradi svakodnevnom, uravnoteženom prehranom.
questions.answer: Zdravlje se gradi svakodnevnom, uravnoteženom prehranom.
questions.explanation: 'Zdravlje se… gradi za stolom.' The message is that health comes from everyday, balanced eating and moderation.

days.title: Word-bank gap-fill training
days.objective: Practise inserting 12 scrambled words into a text in correct case forms.
days.paretoFocus: Gap-fills test vocabulary AND cases at once (travnju, očiju, superlatives). It's the exam's densest section, train it early.
drills: Do a word-bank gap-fill exercise in the Quiz tab (or build one from yesterday's article).
For each gap, name WHY that case form fits.
Watch for traps: locative months (u travnju), genitive plurals (očiju).
Add every miss to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Decline oko/oči through the plural cases.
Review food vocabulary.

activities.title: Popuni prazninu iz banke riječi, padeži u tekstu
activities.intro: The word-bank gap-fill tests vocabulary and case at the same time: you get twelve scrambled words and must slot each into the text in the right form. Study these worked examples, each shows the base word from the bank and the exact inflected form the sentence demands, plus the reason. Watch the classic traps: locative months, irregular genitive plurals, superlatives.

items.hr: (travanj) → U travnju počinje proljetna sezona.
items.en: (April) → The spring season begins in April.
items.note: Trap: a month answering 'when' → locative u travnju, not the nominative travanj.

items.hr: (oko) → Boja njezinih očiju je zelena.
items.en: (eye) → The colour of her eyes is green.
items.note: Trap: irregular genitive plural oko → očiju (not *oka).

items.hr: (dijete) → Igralište je bilo puno djece.
items.en: (child) → The playground was full of children.
items.note: puno + genitive; the plural of dijete is the collective djeca → genitive djece.

items.hr: (dobar) → Ovo je najbolji restoran u gradu.
items.en: (good) → This is the best restaurant in the city.
items.note: Trap: irregular superlative dobar → najbolji (not *najdobriji).

items.hr: (godina) → Prije mnogo godina živjeli smo u Splitu.
items.en: (year) → Many years ago we lived in Split.
items.note: mnogo + genitive plural; godina has a zero ending in the G pl.: godina.

items.hr: (prijatelj) → Došao sam s dvojicom prijatelja.
items.en: (friend) → I came with two friends.
