activities.intro: One pass across the week: prefixed verb families, the dative of feeling, collective numerals, conditionals and the passive participle. Read each sentence fully before choosing.

questions.prompt: 'We mustn't allow discrimination.', Ne smijemo ______ diskriminaciju.
questions.explanation: dopustiti = to allow/permit. propustiti = to miss; spustiti = to lower; pustiti = to let go.

questions.prompt: staviti family: 'The employer set clear rules.', Poslodavac je ______ jasna pravila. (postaviti → m.)
questions.explanation: postaviti = to set/establish; male subject → postavio. postaviti pravila = to lay down rules.

questions.prompt: 'We missed a good opportunity.', ______ smo dobru priliku.
questions.explanation: propustiti priliku = to miss an opportunity. The pro- prefix carries the sense of letting something pass by.

questions.prompt: Dative of feeling: 'I like that things are changing.', ______ mi se što se stvari mijenjaju.
questions.explanation: The subject is the što-clause, so the verb is 3rd sg: Sviđa mi se… .

questions.prompt: dati family: 'Everyone should get a chance.', Svatko treba ______ priliku. (dobiti → infinitive)
questions.explanation: trebati + infinitive; dobiti priliku = to get a chance. dobiti is the perfective 'to receive'.

questions.prompt: Collective numeral: '______ kolega složilo se s prijedlogom.'
questions.explanation: dvoje + genitive plural kolega, with a neuter singular verb složilo se, the standard mixed/general two.

questions.prompt: Build: 'If I had the chance, I would change a lot.'
questions.answer: Da imam priliku promijenio bih mnogo toga
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: Da + present (imam) in the if-clause, kondicional (promijenio bih) in the main clause. mnogo toga = a lot (of it).

questions.prompt: Passive participle: 'Equality is guaranteed by law.', Ravnopravnost je ______ zakonom.
questions.explanation: The participle agrees with ravnopravnost (feminine sg): zajamčena. zakonom = instrumental of means.

questions.prompt: pisati / dati families: 'I wrote a proposal and submitted it to management.', Napisao sam prijedlog i ______ ga upravi.
questions.explanation: predati = to hand over / submit to an authority (predati upravi). prodati = to sell; izdati = to publish/betray; zadati = to assign.

activities.title: Duži razgovor: društvo, poštenje i posao
activities.intro: Your long speaking session for the week. You tell your wife about a proposal you made at work and you range over society, fairness and change, deliberately using the prefixed verb families you've trained. Read your lines ('Me') aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Danas si imao sastanak na poslu, zar ne? Kako je prošlo?
lines.en: You had a meeting at work today, didn't you? How did it go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro. Napisao sam prijedlog o ravnopravnijim uvjetima rada i predao ga upravi.
lines.en: Well. I wrote a proposal about fairer working conditions and submitted it to management.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Svaka čast! O čemu se točno radi?
lines.en: Well done! What exactly is it about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Predložio sam da svi zaposlenici dobiju jednake prilike za napredovanje.
lines.en: I proposed that all employees get equal opportunities for promotion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je važno. Nadam se da te nisu odbili.
lines.en: That's important. I hope they didn't reject you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nisu. Dvoje kolega odmah se složilo sa mnom, a šef je postavio nekoliko pitanja.
lines.en: They didn't. Two colleagues agreed with me right away, and the boss asked a few questions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A što je s onima koji se ne slažu?
lines.en: And what about those who don't agree?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uvijek ima takvih, ali ne smijemo dopustiti da strah zaustavi promjene.
lines.en: There are always such people, but we mustn't let fear stop change.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažem se. Da svi šute, ništa se ne bi promijenilo.
lines.en: I agree. If everyone stayed silent, nothing would change.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Upravo tako. Kad bismo se svi malo više trudili, atmosfera bi bila puno bolja.
lines.en: Exactly. If we all tried a little harder, the atmosphere would be much better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ponosna sam na tebe. I govoriš sve tečnije, koristiš i kondicional i glagolske obitelji!
lines.en: I'm proud of you. And you speak more and more fluently, you use both the conditional and the verb families!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trudim se. Šteta bi bila propustiti sve što sam ovih mjeseci naučio.
lines.en: I'm trying. It would be a shame to waste everything I've learned these months.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nećeš ništa propustiti. Hajde, zaslužio si odmor večeras.
lines.en: You won't waste anything. Come on, you've earned a rest tonight.

days.title: Smijeh je lijek, humor + grammar sweep I
days.objective: Enjoy Croatian humor and start the full B1 grammar sweep.
days.paretoFocus: Understanding jokes is real comprehension. Meanwhile, a systematic sweep finds gaps before exam prep begins.
drills: Read/watch 3 simple Croatian jokes or a comedy clip; explain ONE to your spouse in Croatian.
Grammar sweep part 1: aspect pairs, passive participle, verbal nouns, 5 self-test sentences each.
Note anything shaky in your error log.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell your favourite joke.
Re-drill your shakiest grammar point.

activities.title: Hrvatski humor, vicevi i izrazi
activities.intro: Understanding a joke in Croatian is real comprehension: you have to catch the punchline in real time, often built on a double meaning. Learn the vocabulary of humour so you can follow a comedy clip and explain one joke to your wife.

items.hr: vic
items.en: a joke
items.note: pričati viceve = to tell jokes; G vica, N pl vicevi. Classic characters: Mujo i Haso, plavuša (the blonde).

items.hr: šaliti se
items.en: to joke, to kid
items.note: Šalim se! = I'm joking! šala = a joke/prank; nema šale = no kidding.

items.hr: smiješno
items.en: funny
items.note: Baš je smiješno! = That's really funny! Do not confuse with smiješan (adj., m.) = funny/ridiculous.

items.hr: nasmijati (koga)
items.en: to make (someone) laugh
items.note: Nasmijao me do suza = he made me laugh to tears. Reflexive nasmijati se = to laugh, to burst out laughing.

items.hr: poanta
items.en: the point, the punchline
items.note: Nisam skužio poantu = I didn't get the point. skužiti (colloquial) = to grasp/get it.

items.hr: dosjetka
items.en: a witticism, a one-liner
items.note: A quick, clever remark. dosjetljiv = witty, quick-thinking.

items.hr: ironija / sarkazam
items.en: irony / sarcasm
items.note: Rekao je to s ironijom = he said it ironically. Croatian humour leans heavily on both.

items.hr: pukao sam od smijeha
items.en: I burst out laughing
items.note: Idiom, literally 'I cracked from laughter'. Female: pukla sam od smijeha.

items.hr: zafrkavati / zezati (koga)
items.en: to tease, to mess with (someone)
items.note: Colloquial. Samo te zafrkavam = I'm just teasing you. Reflexive: zezamo se = we're kidding around.

items.hr: imati smisla za humor
items.en: to have a sense of humour
items.note: Ona ima odličan smisao za humor. smisao za + accusative.

activities.title: Gramatički pregled I: vid, trpni pridjev, glagolske imenice
activities.intro: Part one of the big grammar revision. Reread each contrast: the imperfective vs perfective pair, the passive participle that turns a verb into an adjective, and the verbal noun that turns it into a thing. Any of these can be an exam gap.

items.hr: pisati (pišem) / napisati (napišem)
items.en: to write, process vs completed
items.note: Cijelu večer sam pisao (process). Napisao sam pismo (finished result). The na- prefix perfectivises.

items.hr: Čitao sam knjigu. / Pročitao sam cijelu knjigu.
items.en: I was reading the book. / I read the whole book through.
items.note: cijelu (the whole) pushes you to the perfective: the action is completed.

items.hr: napisan, napisana, napisano
items.en: written (passive participle)
items.note: Pismo je napisano (n.). The participle agrees like an adjective: napisan tekst, napisana poruka.

items.hr: otvoren / zatvoren
items.en: open / closed (participles)
items.note: From otvoriti / zatvoriti. Vrata su otvorena (neuter pl vrata → otvorena). Trgovina je zatvorena.

items.hr: pisanje
items.en: writing (verbal noun)
items.note: From pisati; neuter noun in -nje. Pisanje eseja traži vrijeme = writing an essay takes time.

items.hr: čitanje / učenje / putovanje
items.en: reading / learning / travelling
items.note: Verbal nouns are neuter and formed from the passive participle stem + -e: učenje, putovanje.

items.hr: učenje jezika
items.en: the learning of languages
items.note: A verbal noun governs the genitive of its object: učenje jezika, pisanje teksta, čitanje knjige.

items.hr: Nakon dugog putovanja bili smo umorni.
items.en: After the long journey we were tired.
items.note: Verbal noun putovanje declines like any noun: nakon + genitive putovanja.

items.hr: Zadatak je riješen.
items.en: The task has been solved.
items.note: Passive participle riješen (from riješiti). Note the double meaning of the -je verbal noun rješenje = a solution.

activities.title: Humor i gramatika, pregled I
activities.intro: A little humour vocabulary and a solid pass over aspect, the passive participle and verbal nouns. The difficulty climbs from a joke word to a participle-agreement trap.

questions.prompt: 'I'm joking!', ______ se!
questions.explanation: šaliti se → Šalim se = I'm joking. Šaljem is 'I send' (slati); Salim has no diacritic and is wrong.

questions.prompt: 'the punchline of the joke', ______ vica.
questions.explanation: poanta = the point/punchline (nominative). poantu is accusative; the spelling pointa is not standard.

questions.prompt: Aspect, a finished whole: 'Yesterday I read the whole book.', Jučer sam ______ cijelu knjigu.
questions.explanation: cijelu knjigu = a completed whole → perfective pročitati → pročitao. čitao names only the process.

questions.prompt: Verbal noun: 'Learning languages takes time.', ______ jezika traži vrijeme. (učiti → verbal noun)
questions.explanation: The verbal noun of učiti is učenje (neuter). It governs the genitive of its object: učenje jezika.

questions.prompt: Passive participle: 'The letter is written.', Pismo je ______.
questions.explanation: The participle agrees with pismo (neuter sg): napisano. napisan is masculine, napisana feminine.

questions.prompt: Aspect, process: 'I was writing all evening.', Cijelu večer sam ______.
questions.explanation: 'all evening' describes a process with no stated end → imperfective pisati → pisao. napisao would mean the writing was completed.

questions.prompt: Build the idiom: 'I burst out laughing at that joke.'
questions.answer: Pukao sam od smijeha na taj vic
questions.explanation: pukao sam od smijeha = I burst out laughing (od + genitive smijeha); na taj vic = at that joke (na + accusative).

questions.prompt: Verbal noun + genitive object: 'the writing of the text', pisanje ______.
questions.explanation: A verbal noun governs the genitive: pisanje teksta. The other cases don't fit an object here.

questions.prompt: Participle agreement: 'The doors are open.', Vrata su ______.
questions.explanation: vrata is a neuter plural (pluralia tantum), so the participle is otvorena: Vrata su otvorena.

days.title: Phase 4 milestone: B1 core complete
days.objective: Finish the grammar sweep and certify your B1 core. Exam prep starts tomorrow.
days.paretoFocus: 65 days of B1 building are done: 11 verb families, all core grammar, native listening, three writing genres. Now we sharpen for the exam.
drills: Grammar sweep part 2: dative experiencers, conditionals, collectives, se-constructions, 5 self-test sentences each.
Take the B1 grammar quiz in the app.
Record a 3-minute talk: 'Moj put do hrvatskog', compare with your lesson 157 recording.
Long conversation with your tutor or spouse to celebrate.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write your 3 weakest areas, exam prep will target them.
Read the exam-prep phase overview in the app.

activities.title: Gramatički pregled II: dativ nositelja, pogodbe, zbirne imenice, se-konstrukcije
activities.intro: The second half of the grammar sweep certifies your B1 core. Reread each sentence aloud and name the rule: the dative of the experiencer, the three conditional types, the collective nouns, and the se-constructions. If any one feels shaky, that's tomorrow's exam-prep target.

items.hr: Hladno mi je.
items.en: I'm cold.
items.note: Dative experiencer: neuter adverb + dative. Never 'Ja sam hladan' for feeling cold.

items.hr: Nedostaju mi prijatelji.
items.en: I miss my friends.
items.note: The verb agrees with the subject prijatelji (plural): nedostaju. Nedostaje mi more (sg.).

items.hr: Ako budeš vježbao, položit ćeš ispit.
items.en: If you practise, you'll pass the exam.
items.note: Real condition: ako + futur II (budeš vježbao) → futur I (položit ćeš) in the main clause.

items.hr: Kad bih mogao, preselio bih se na more.
items.en: If I could, I'd move to the seaside.
items.note: Potential condition: kad + kondicional (kad bih mogao) → kondicional (preselio bih se).

items.hr: Da imam više vremena, više bih čitao.
items.en: If I had more time, I'd read more.
items.note: Unreal condition: da + present in the if-clause signals the condition is not met.

items.hr: Dvoje djece i troje ljudi.
items.en: Two children and three people.
items.note: Collective numerals + genitive plural: dvoje djece, troje ljudi. Used for mixed or general groups.

items.hr: Ovdje se ne fotografira.
items.en: No photography here.
items.note: Impersonal se + 3rd sg: the general 'one'. A subjectless sign sentence.

items.hr: Kruh se peče svako jutro.
items.en: Bread is baked every morning.
items.note: Reflexive-passive: the subject kruh is 3rd sg, so peče se agrees with it.

items.hr: Lišće već pada.
items.en: The leaves are already falling.
items.note: Collective noun lišće (from list) is neuter singular: lišće pada. Also cvijeće = flowers, granje = branches.

items.hr: Braća su stigla.
items.en: The brothers have arrived.
items.note: The collective braća takes a neuter plural participle: stigla. Similarly gospoda, djeca.

activities.title: Miješani test, pregled II
activities.intro: The certifying pass: the dative of feeling, all three conditionals, collective nouns and se-constructions. Read carefully, the endings and the agreement are where points are won or lost.

questions.prompt: 'I'm cold.', ______ mi je.
questions.explanation: The frame uses the neuter adverb + dative: Hladno mi je. 'Ja sam hladan' would describe you as cold to the touch.

questions.prompt: Agreement: 'I miss my friends.', ______ mi prijatelji.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the plural subject prijatelji: Nedostaju mi prijatelji. Singular: Nedostaje mi more.

questions.prompt: Real condition (futur II): 'If you practise, you'll pass.', Ako ______ vježbao, položit ćeš. (biti → 2nd sg)
questions.explanation: Futur II = budem/budeš… + participle: Ako budeš vježbao… The main clause then takes futur I (položit ćeš).

questions.prompt: Collective numeral: 'three people', ______ ljudi.
questions.explanation: A general/mixed group takes troje + genitive plural: troje ljudi. trojica is 'three men' specifically.

questions.prompt: Reflexive-passive: 'Bread is baked every morning.', Kruh se ______ svako jutro.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the singular subject kruh: peče se. A plural subject (kolači) would give peku se.

questions.prompt: Potential condition: 'If I could, I'd move to the seaside.', Kad bih mogao, ______ se na more.
questions.explanation: A potential condition keeps kondicional in both clauses: Kad bih mogao… preselio bih se.

questions.prompt: Build the unreal condition: 'If I had time, I'd read more.'
questions.answer: Da imam vremena više bih čitao
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: Da + present (imam vremena) → kondicional (više bih čitao). da + indicative signals the condition is not fulfilled.

questions.prompt: Collective noun: 'The leaves are falling.', ______ pada.
questions.explanation: The collective of list is lišće (neuter singular), so the verb is singular: Lišće pada. The spelling 'listje' is wrong.

questions.prompt: Collective agreement: 'The brothers have arrived.', Braća su ______.
questions.explanation: The collective braća takes a neuter plural participle: Braća su stigla. Same pattern as djeca su došla.

activities.title: Slavlje: Moj put do hrvatskoga
activities.intro: The Phase 4 milestone. You and your wife celebrate finishing the B1 core and look back over your journey. Tomorrow the exam-prep phase begins. Read your lines ('Me') aloud, you are the male learner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Danas je poseban dan, završio si cijelu B1 razinu! Kako se osjećaš?
lines.en: Today is a special day, you've finished the whole B1 level! How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ponosno i pomalo nevjerojatno. Prije godinu dana jedva sam sastavljao rečenice.
lines.en: Proud and a little incredulous. A year ago I could barely put sentences together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sjećaš se svoje prve snimke? Da je sad poslušaš, ne bi vjerovao koliko si napredovao.
lines.en: Do you remember your first recording? If you listened to it now, you wouldn't believe how much you've progressed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam. Da imam vremena, danas bih je ponovno preslušao i usporedio s novom.
lines.en: I know. If I had time, I'd listen to it again today and compare it with a new one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što ti je bilo najteže na tom putu?
lines.en: What was the hardest part of the journey for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Padeži, bez sumnje. Dugo mi nije bilo jasno kada dolazi genitiv, a kada lokativ.
lines.en: The cases, no doubt. For a long time it wasn't clear to me when the genitive comes and when the locative.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sad? Zvučiš gotovo kao domaći govornik.
lines.en: And now? You sound almost like a native speaker.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala! Najviše mi je pomoglo to što svaki dan razgovaram s tobom.
lines.en: Thanks! What helped me most is talking with you every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I bit će još bolje. Sutra počinje priprema za ispit, zar ne?
lines.en: And it'll get even better. Tomorrow the exam prep begins, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da. Malo sam nervozan, ali osjećam da sam spreman za sljedeći korak.
lines.en: Yes. I'm a little nervous, but I feel I'm ready for the next step.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bit ćeš odličan. Večeras slavimo, a od sutra učimo format ispita!
lines.en: You'll be great. Tonight we celebrate, and from tomorrow we learn the exam format!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dogovoreno. Bez tebe ništa od ovoga ne bih uspio.
lines.en: Agreed. Without you I wouldn't have managed any of this.

days.title: Namjerne rečenice: da + prezent i kako bi
days.objective: Express purpose in Croatian with 'da' plus the present tense and with 'kako bi' plus the conditional.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every explanation of your plans, reasons and intentions needs a purpose clause, and Croatian never uses the infinitive the way English uses 'to do something' here.
drills: Write five sentences about your week starting with a reason to act, each ending in a 'da' purpose clause.
Rewrite three of those five sentences in the 'kako bi' pattern, changing the conditional auxiliary to match the subject.
Say out loud why you are learning Croatian, giving three different purposes in one breath.

items: Which tense follows 'da' in a purpose clause, and which form follows 'kako bi'?
Give the conditional auxiliary for ja, mi and oni.
Say 'Speak more slowly so everyone understands you' in Croatian.

activities.title: Zašto nešto radimo
activities.intro: Where English says 'to do something', Croatian normally uses 'da' followed by a present-tense verb. The alternative 'kako bi' takes the conditional, which is the auxiliary bih, bi, bi, bismo, biste, bi plus the past participle. Both are correct; 'kako bi' sounds slightly more careful and written.

items.hr: Ustajem ranije da stignem na vlak.
items.en: I get up earlier to catch the train.
items.note: da + prezent, never da + infinitive

items.hr: Zovem te da ti kažem novost.
items.en: I am calling you to tell you the news.
items.note: the present 'kažem' covers English 'to tell'

items.hr: Ponavljam gradivo kako bih bolje razumio pravila.
items.en: I am revising the material so that I understand the rules better.
items.note: kako + bih + razumio (participle agrees with the speaker)

items.hr: Poslali su nam upute kako bismo znali što učiniti.
items.en: They sent us instructions so that we would know what to do.
items.note: mi -> bismo znali

items.hr: Govori sporije da te svi razumiju.
items.en: Speak more slowly so that everyone understands you.
items.note: two different subjects, so 'da' is the only option

activities.title: Vježba namjere
activities.intro: Practise choosing between 'da' with the present and 'kako bi' with the conditional.

questions.prompt: Choose the word that fits: 'Idem u knjižnicu ___ posudim knjigu.'
questions.explanation: A purpose clause with a present-tense verb is introduced by 'da'. 'Kako' would need the conditional 'bih posudio'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the conditional correctly after 'kako', spoken by one person about herself?
options: Vježbam svaki dan kako bih govorila tečnije.
Vježbam svaki dan kako bih govorim tečnije.
Vježbam svaki dan kako bi govorila tečnije.
Vježbam svaki dan kako bih govoriti tečnije.
questions.answer: Vježbam svaki dan kako bih govorila tečnije.
questions.explanation: 'Kako' requires the conditional: the first-person auxiliary 'bih' plus the participle 'govorila'. Neither the present nor the infinitive can follow it.

questions.prompt: Fill in the missing conditional auxiliary for 'mi' (we): 'Rano smo krenuli kako ___ izbjegli gužvu.'
questions.explanation: The conditional auxiliary for 'mi' is 'bismo', and it combines with the participle 'izbjegli'.

questions.prompt: Put the verb 'stići' into the present tense for 'oni' (they): 'Požurite da svi ___ na vrijeme.'
questions.explanation: After 'da' the verb stays in the present tense, and the third-person plural of 'stići' is 'stignu'.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I am saving money to travel to Croatia.'
questions.explanation: The purpose is expressed with 'da' plus the present 'putujem', and 'u' with the accusative 'Hrvatsku' marks movement towards a place.

activities.title: Dogovor oko selidbe
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks why you took a day off, and you explain every step with a purpose clause.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zašto si uzeo slobodan dan?
lines.en: Why did you take a day off?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uzeo sam ga da mogu pomoći bratu oko selidbe.
lines.en: I took it so that I can help my brother with the move.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trebate li kombi?
lines.en: Do you need a van?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, unajmit ćemo ga kako bismo sve prenijeli odjednom.
lines.en: Yes, we will rent one so that we can move everything at once.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Krenite rano da izbjegnete gužvu.
lines.en: Set off early to avoid the traffic.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Upravo zato ustajem u šest da stignemo prije podne.
lines.en: That is exactly why I am getting up at six, so that we arrive before noon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Javi mi ako treba još ruku.
lines.en: Let me know if you need another pair of hands.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nazvat ću te da ne moraš cijeli dan čekati.
lines.en: I will call you so that you do not have to wait all day.

days.title: Vremenske rečenice: kad, dok, čim, nakon što, prije nego što
days.objective: Place events in time using the conjunctions kad, dok, čim, nakon što and prije nego što with the correct tense.
days.paretoFocus: Telling any story or arranging any meeting means saying when something happens, and Croatian uses the present tense after these conjunctions where English uses a future.
drills: Describe your morning in four sentences, each linked with a different time conjunction.
Write three plans for next week using 'kad' plus a present-tense verb and a future main clause.
Contrast one pair of sentences: one with 'dok' plus an imperfective verb, one with 'dok ne' plus a perfective verb.

items: Why is 'Kad ću doći' wrong when you mean 'when I arrive'?
What is the difference in meaning between 'dok' and 'dok ne'?
Say 'Before you leave, close the window' in Croatian.

activities.title: Kada se što događa
activities.intro: Time clauses in Croatian take the present tense when they point to the future, so you never say 'kad ću'. 'Dok' with an imperfective verb means 'while', but 'dok ne' with a perfective verb means 'until'. 'Prije nego što' always takes the present, even when the whole story is in the past.

items.hr: Kad stignem kući, javit ću ti se.
items.en: When I get home, I will let you know.
items.note: present after 'kad', future only in the main clause

items.hr: Dok sam čekao vlak, čitao sam novine.
items.en: While I was waiting for the train, I was reading the newspaper.
items.note: dok + imperfective = while; dok ne + perfective = until

items.hr: Čim završim posao, idemo na kavu.
items.en: As soon as I finish work, we are going for a coffee.
items.note: čim = the moment that, immediate sequence

items.hr: Nakon što smo večerali, prošetali smo gradom.
items.en: After we had had dinner, we took a walk around town.
items.note: nakon što introduces a full clause, unlike 'nakon' plus a noun

items.hr: Prije nego što odeš, zatvori prozor.
items.en: Before you leave, close the window.
items.note: prije nego što + present, whatever the tense of the main clause

activities.title: Vježba vremenskih veznika
activities.intro: Practise picking the right time conjunction and the tense that goes with it.

questions.prompt: Choose the conjunction for: '___ sam kuhao, slušao sam glazbu.' (While I was cooking, I was listening to music.)
questions.explanation: 'Dok' with an imperfective verb marks two actions running in parallel. 'Čim' would mean one action started the moment the other ended.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct Croatian for 'When I come, I will call you'?
options: Kad ću doći, nazvat ću te.
Kad bih došao, nazvat ću te.
Kad dolazit ću, nazvat ću te.
questions.explanation: A time clause about the future uses the present tense of the perfective verb. The future tense stays in the main clause only.

questions.prompt: Fill in the conjunction meaning 'the moment that': '___ dobijem plaću, kupit ću novi bicikl.'
questions.explanation: 'Čim' marks an immediate sequence and is followed by the present tense of a perfective verb.

questions.prompt: Put 'otići' into the correct form for 'mi' (we): 'Prije nego što ___, moramo platiti račun.'
questions.explanation: 'Prije nego što' is always followed by the present tense, and the first-person plural of 'otići' is 'odemo'.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'As soon as I finish work, I am going home.'
questions.explanation: The time clause with 'čim' comes first and keeps the present tense; 'kući' is the fixed adverbial form meaning 'homewards'.

activities.title: Planovi za večer
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are arranging to meet a friend and every reply pins down the timing of your evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kad završavaš danas?
lines.en: When do you finish today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Čim predam izvještaj, slobodan sam.
lines.en: As soon as I hand in the report, I am free.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Možemo li se naći prije nego što krene kiša?
lines.en: Can we meet before the rain starts?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Može, ali dok ne pošaljem poruku šefu, ne mogu nikamo.
lines.en: We can, but until I send the message to my boss, I cannot go anywhere.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro, čekam te u kafiću nakon što završiš.
lines.en: Fine, I will wait for you in the cafe after you finish.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad stignem, naručit ću nam kavu.
lines.en: When I get there, I will order us a coffee.

days.title: Uzročne rečenice: jer, zato što, budući da, zbog
days.objective: Give reasons in Croatian, choosing correctly between the clause conjunctions jer, zato što and budući da and the preposition zbog.
days.paretoFocus: Explaining why you are late, why you changed your mind or why you like something is the single most common thing you do beyond describing facts.
drills: Answer the question 'Zašto učiš hrvatski?' three times, once with jer, once with zato što, once with budući da.
Turn three 'jer' clauses into 'zbog' phrases with a noun in the genitive.
Write two sentences that must begin with 'Budući da' and explain why 'jer' cannot open them.

items: Of today's three cause conjunctions, which one can comfortably start a sentence?
Which case does 'zbog' take, and give two examples.
Say 'We arrived late because of the heavy traffic' in Croatian.

activities.title: Zašto se nešto dogodilo
activities.intro: 'Jer' and 'zato što' introduce a reason clause but stand after the main clause, so a sentence never begins with them. 'Budući da' does the same job and normally opens the sentence, with a comma before the main clause. 'Zbog' is not a conjunction at all: it is a preposition and takes a noun in the genitive.

items.hr: Ne idem van jer pada kiša.
items.en: I am not going out because it is raining.
items.note: jer always follows the main clause

items.hr: Zakasnio sam zato što je vlak kasnio.
items.en: I was late because the train was late.
items.note: zato što = a slightly stronger, more explicit 'because'

items.hr: Budući da nemamo vremena, počet ćemo odmah.
items.en: Since we do not have time, we will start right away.
items.note: budući da opens the sentence, comma before the main clause

items.hr: Zbog gustog prometa stigli smo kasno.
items.en: Because of the heavy traffic we arrived late.
items.note: zbog + genitiv: gust promet -> gustog prometa

items.hr: Zašto si tužan? Zato što sam izgubio ključeve.
items.en: Why are you sad? Because I lost my keys.
items.note: an answer to 'zašto' starts with zato što, never with jer alone

activities.title: Vježba uzroka
activities.intro: Practise choosing between reason clauses and the preposition of cause.

questions.prompt: Which linker can open the sentence: '___ je bilo hladno, ostali smo doma.'
questions.explanation: 'Budući da' is the reason conjunction that normally stands first. 'Jer' and 'zato što' follow the main clause, and 'zbog' needs a noun, not a clause.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form after the preposition of cause: 'Utakmica je otkazana ___.'
questions.explanation: 'Zbog' governs the genitive, so 'kiša' becomes 'kiše'.

questions.prompt: Fill in the one-word conjunction meaning 'because': 'Ostajem kod kuće ___ se ne osjećam dobro.'
questions.explanation: 'Jer' introduces the reason and always stands after the main clause.

questions.prompt: Put the noun 'gužva' into the case required after 'zbog': 'Zbog ___ smo propustili početak filma.'
questions.explanation: 'Zbog' takes the genitive, and the genitive singular of 'gužva' is 'gužve'.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'She studies Croatian because she lives in Zagreb.'
questions.explanation: The reason clause with 'jer' follows the main clause, and 'u' with the locative 'Zagrebu' marks location.

activities.title: Zašto kasnim
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are late for a film and have to justify yourself with a different reason structure each time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gdje si? Film počinje za deset minuta.
lines.en: Where are you? The film starts in ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kasnim jer je tramvaj stao usred ceste.
lines.en: I am late because the tram stopped in the middle of the road.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zašto nisi krenuo ranije?
lines.en: Why did you not set off earlier?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato što sam morao završiti izvještaj za šeficu.
lines.en: Because I had to finish the report for my boss.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Budući da nemamo vremena, kupit ću ti kartu.
lines.en: Since we do not have time, I will buy your ticket.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala, zbog gužve ću stići tek u zadnji tren.
lines.en: Thanks, because of the traffic I will only make it at the last moment.

days.title: Posljedične rečenice: tako da, toliko da
days.objective: State the consequence of a situation with tako da, with toliko plus da, and with takav plus da.
days.paretoFocus: Consequence clauses let you make a point instead of listing facts, which is exactly what moves your speech from A2 description to B1 narration.
drills: Describe three things that happened today and add a 'tako da' consequence to each.
Write three sentences with 'toliko' plus a verb and 'da' for the result.
Turn two of your sentences into the 'takav plus noun plus da' pattern.

items: Which intensifier goes with a verb and which with an adjective?
What follows 'takav' before the 'da' clause?
Say 'The film was so boring that we left' in Croatian.

activities.title: Posljedica situacije
activities.intro: 'Tako da' introduces the result of the whole main clause and simply means 'so, with the result that'. To intensify a single word you put 'toliko' before a verb, 'tako' or 'toliko' before an adjective, and 'takav, takva, takvo' before a noun, and the result then follows with 'da'.

items.hr: Padala je jaka kiša, tako da smo ostali kod kuće.
items.en: It was raining heavily, so we stayed at home.
items.note: tako da = the result of everything just said

items.hr: Bio je toliko umoran da je zaspao u fotelji.
items.en: He was so tired that he fell asleep in the armchair.
items.note: toliko + pridjev + da

items.hr: Film je bio tako dosadan da smo otišli prije kraja.
items.en: The film was so boring that we left before the end.
items.note: tako works before adjectives just like toliko

items.hr: Toliko smo hodali da nas sada bole noge.
items.en: We walked so much that our legs hurt now.
items.note: toliko + glagol, never tako + glagol

items.hr: Bila je takva gužva da nismo mogli ući u tramvaj.
items.en: There was such a crowd that we could not get on the tram.
items.note: takav, takva, takvo agree with the noun

activities.title: Vježba posljedice
activities.intro: Practise linking a situation to the result it produced.

questions.prompt: Choose the linker: 'Zaboravio sam ključeve, ___ sam morao čekati pred vratima.'
questions.explanation: 'Tako da' presents the second clause as the result of the first. The other options mark time, concession and purpose.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'It was such a heat that we could not sleep'?
options: Bila je takva vrućina da nismo mogli spavati.
Bila je tako vrućina da nismo mogli spavati.
Bila je toliko vrućina da nismo mogli spavati.
Bila je takvu vrućinu da nismo mogli spavati.
questions.answer: Bila je takva vrućina da nismo mogli spavati.
questions.explanation: Before a noun you need 'takav' agreeing with it, so the feminine nominative 'takva vrućina'. 'Tako' and 'toliko' modify adjectives and verbs, not nouns.

questions.prompt: Fill in the intensifier that goes with a verb to mean 'so much': 'Djeca su ___ vikala da smo zatvorili prozor.'
questions.explanation: A verb is intensified with 'toliko'; 'tako' is used with adjectives and adverbs.

questions.prompt: Fill in the two-word linker that introduces the result: 'Autobus je kasnio, ___ ___ smo propustili sastanak.'
questions.explanation: 'Tako da' marks the whole second clause as the consequence of the first.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'The film is so boring that we are leaving.'
questions.explanation: The intensifier 'tako' stands directly before the adjective, and the result clause with 'da' comes last.

activities.title: Vrućina u gradu
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a neighbour complain about a heatwave, and each reply names a consequence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li spavao noćas?
lines.en: Did you sleep last night?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bila je takva vrućina da nisam mogao zaspati.
lines.en: It was such a heat that I could not fall asleep.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I meni je bilo grozno, tako da sam ustao u pet.
lines.en: It was awful for me too, so I got up at five.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ventilator je bio toliko bučan da sam ga ugasio.
lines.en: The fan was so noisy that I turned it off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Danas je najavljeno još toplije vrijeme.
lines.en: Even warmer weather has been announced for today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Onda ću raditi od kuće, tako da ne moram u tramvaj.
lines.en: Then I will work from home, so that I do not have to get on the tram.

days.title: Dopusne rečenice: iako, premda, unatoč tome što
days.objective: Concede a point in Croatian with iako, premda, unatoč tome što and unatoč plus the dative.
days.paretoFocus: Conceding something before you disagree is the core move of any real opinion, and it instantly makes your Croatian sound like an adult speaker rather than a beginner.
drills: Write three sentences of the type 'Although X, I still do Y' about your own habits.
Rewrite each of them with 'unatoč tome što' instead of 'iako'.
Shorten one of them to 'unatoč' plus a noun in the dative.

items: Name two one-word conjunctions meaning 'although' and say which is more formal.
Which case follows 'unatoč'?
Say 'Although I do not have much time, I study every day' in Croatian.

activities.title: Suprotno očekivanju
activities.intro: A concessive clause says that the result goes against what you would expect. 'Iako' is the everyday word for 'although' and 'premda' is its more formal twin; both are followed by an ordinary indicative verb. 'Unatoč tome što' introduces a full clause, while plain 'unatoč' takes a noun in the dative.

items.hr: Iako je padala kiša, otišli smo na izlet.
items.en: Although it was raining, we went on the trip.
items.note: iako opens the sentence, comma before the main clause

items.hr: Premda ga slabo poznajem, vjerujem mu.
items.en: Although I hardly know him, I trust him.
items.note: premda = same meaning as iako, more formal register

items.hr: Unatoč tome što smo krenuli rano, zapeli smo u gužvi.
items.en: Despite the fact that we set off early, we got stuck in traffic.
items.note: unatoč tome što + cijela rečenica

items.hr: Unatoč kiši, utakmica je odigrana.
items.en: Despite the rain, the match was played.
items.note: unatoč + dativ: kiša -> kiši

items.hr: Volim taj grad iako je vrlo bučan.
items.en: I love that city although it is very noisy.
items.note: the concessive clause can also stand second

activities.title: Vježba dopuštanja
activities.intro: Practise conceding a point and keeping the right case after the preposition.

questions.prompt: Choose the linker: '___ je bio umoran, pomogao nam je sa selidbom.'
questions.explanation: The second clause goes against expectation, which calls for a concessive conjunction. The others mark reason, time and result.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the preposition of concession correctly?
options: Unatoč lošem vremenu, prošetali smo uz more.
Unatoč lošeg vremena, prošetali smo uz more.
Unatoč loše vrijeme, prošetali smo uz more.
Unatoč lošim vremenom, prošetali smo uz more.
questions.answer: Unatoč lošem vremenu, prošetali smo uz more.
questions.explanation: 'Unatoč' governs the dative, so 'loše vrijeme' becomes 'lošem vremenu'.

questions.prompt: Fill in the more formal one-word conjunction meaning 'although': '___ nije imao iskustva, dobio je posao.'
questions.explanation: 'Premda' is the formal equivalent of 'iako' and introduces a concessive clause with an indicative verb.

questions.prompt: Fill in the three-word linker meaning 'despite the fact that': '___ ___ ___ smo rezervirali stol, morali smo čekati.'
questions.explanation: The preposition needs the pronoun 'tome' plus 'što' before it can introduce a whole clause.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Although I do not have much time, I study every day.'
questions.explanation: The concessive clause with 'iako' comes first, and 'puno' takes the genitive 'vremena'.

activities.title: Izlet unatoč kiši
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants to cancel a hike because of the weather and you argue back by conceding each point.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vani pada kiša. Otkazujemo izlet?
lines.en: It is raining outside. Are we cancelling the trip?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Iako pada, ja bih ipak išao.
lines.en: Although it is raining, I would still go.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Premda volim planinu, danas mi se ne penje.
lines.en: Although I love the mountain, I do not feel like climbing today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Unatoč tome što je mokro, staza je sasvim sigurna.
lines.en: Despite the fact that it is wet, the path is perfectly safe.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro, idem, iako ću vjerojatno pokisnuti.
lines.en: All right, I am coming, although I will probably get soaked.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ponesi jaknu i unatoč kiši bit će nam lijepo.
lines.en: Bring a jacket and despite the rain we will have a good time.

days.title: Neupravni govor: prenošenje izjava
days.objective: Report what someone said with 'da', shifting the pronouns but keeping the original tense.
days.paretoFocus: Passing on messages is daily life, and Croatian is easier than English here because there is no backshift of tenses to remember.
drills: Take three things a friend told you today and report each with 'Rekao je da' or 'Rekla je da'.
Report one statement about the past, one about the present and one about the future, checking that the tense stays the same.
Report a sentence containing 'ja' and 'ti' and write down which pronouns changed.

items: Does the tense change when you report a statement in Croatian?
Report 'Doći ću sutra' said by Ana.
Which words normally have to change when you report someone's words?

activities.title: Što je tko rekao
activities.intro: Reported statements are introduced by 'da'. Unlike English, Croatian keeps the tense of the original words, so a present stays present and a future stays future even after a past reporting verb. What does change is the person: pronouns and verb endings shift to the point of view of the speaker who is reporting.

items.hr: Rekao je da je umoran.
items.en: He said he was tired.
items.note: original: Umoran sam. The present stays present.

items.hr: Marija kaže da nema vremena.
items.en: Marija says she has no time.
items.note: original: Nemam vremena.

items.hr: Rekli su da će doći poslije.
items.en: They said they would come later.
items.note: the future stays future, no conditional needed

items.hr: Objasnila je da je izgubila ključeve.
items.en: She explained that she had lost her keys.
items.note: original past stays a simple past

items.hr: Rekla je da me čeka pred kinom.
items.en: She said she was waiting for me in front of the cinema.
items.note: original: Ja te čekam. te -> me

activities.title: Vježba prenošenja izjava
activities.intro: Practise reporting statements without changing the tense.

questions.prompt: How do you report the words 'Nemam novca.' spoken by a man?
options: Rekao je da nije imao novca.
Rekao je da neće imati novca.
questions.explanation: The original present is kept, and only the person changes from first to third.

questions.prompt: How do you report 'Doći ću sutra.' spoken by Ana?
options: Ana je rekla da će doći sutra.
Ana je rekla da bi došla sutra.
Ana je rekla da ću doći sutra.
Ana je rekla da je došla sutra.
questions.answer: Ana je rekla da će doći sutra.
questions.explanation: The future is kept as a future, with the auxiliary changed from 'ću' to 'će' for the third person.

questions.prompt: Ana said: 'Volim tvoju sestru.' Complete the report: 'Ana je rekla da voli ___ sestru.'
questions.explanation: The possessive shifts to the point of view of the person reporting, so 'tvoju' becomes 'moju'.

questions.prompt: Petar told me: 'Bio sam u Splitu.' Complete the report: 'Petar mi kaže da ___ bio u Splitu.'
questions.explanation: The past tense stays, but the auxiliary changes from the first person 'sam' to the third person form.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'She says that she does not have time.'
questions.explanation: The reported clause is introduced by 'da' and keeps the present tense; 'nemati' governs the genitive 'vremena'.

activities.title: Prenosim poruku
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are relaying what two other people told you about a dinner on Friday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li razgovarao s Anom?
lines.en: Did you talk to Ana?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam. Rekla je da ne može doći u petak.
lines.en: I did. She said she cannot come on Friday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A što je s Markom?
lines.en: And what about Marko?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Marko kaže da će stići oko osam.
lines.en: Marko says he will arrive around eight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je li rekao išta o hrani?
lines.en: Did he say anything about the food?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rekao je da donosi kolače i da mu ne kupujemo ništa.
lines.en: He said he is bringing cakes and that we should not buy him anything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično, javi im da počinjemo u sedam.
lines.en: Great, tell them that we are starting at seven.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Reći ću im da je dogovor u sedam.
lines.en: I will tell them that the arrangement is for seven.

days.title: Neupravni govor: prenošenje pitanja i zapovijedi
days.objective: Report questions with 'li' and question words, and report orders and requests with 'da' or 'neka' plus the present tense.
days.paretoFocus: At work and with officials you constantly pass on what someone asked and what someone told you to do, and both need structures that direct speech does not.
drills: Report three yes and no questions someone asked you, putting 'li' straight after the verb.
Report three questions that begin with gdje, kada or zašto and check that the word order is a statement, not a question.
Turn three imperatives into reported orders with 'da' plus the present tense.

items: Where does the particle 'li' stand in a reported yes and no question?
How do you report the order 'Dođi ranije'?
What is the difference between reporting with 'da' and with 'neka'?

activities.title: Prenosim pitanje i molbu
activities.intro: A reported yes and no question keeps the particle 'li' immediately after the verb, and the clause has the word order of a statement. Questions with gdje, kada, tko, što or zašto simply keep their question word. Orders and requests are reported with 'da' + present, or with 'neka' + present, never with an infinitive.

items.hr: Pitao me imam li vremena.
items.en: He asked me whether I had time.
items.note: original: Imaš li vremena? li stays right after the verb

items.hr: Pitala je gdje stanujem.
items.en: She asked where I live.
items.note: the question word stays, the word order is a statement

items.hr: Zanima me je li vlak već otišao.
items.en: I wonder whether the train has already left.
items.note: with the copula the pair is je li

items.hr: Rekao mi je da zatvorim prozor.
items.en: He told me to close the window.
items.note: original: Zatvori prozor! imperative -> da + prezent

items.hr: Molila nas je neka pričekamo pred zgradom.
items.en: She asked us to wait in front of the building.
items.note: neka + prezent is a softer alternative to da

activities.title: Vježba pitanja i zapovijedi
activities.intro: Practise turning direct questions and imperatives into reported speech.

questions.prompt: How do you report the question 'Gdje živiš?'
questions.explanation: The question word stays, the person shifts to the speaker who is reporting, and no 'da' is added.

questions.prompt: How do you report the order 'Dođi ranije!'
options: Rekao mi je da dođem ranije.
Rekao mi je da dođi ranije.
Rekao mi je da doći ranije.
questions.answer: Rekao mi je da dođem ranije.
questions.explanation: A reported order uses 'da' plus the present tense in the person who has to act, never the imperative or the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Report the yes and no question 'Do you know the address?' by adding the missing particle: 'Pitala me znam ___ adresu.'
questions.explanation: In a reported yes and no question the particle stands immediately after the verb it questions.

questions.prompt: Report the request 'Pričekajte me!': 'Zamolio nas je da ga ___.'
questions.explanation: The imperative becomes 'da' plus the present tense in the first person plural, because it is 'nas' who have to act.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'They are asking whether we have time.'
questions.explanation: The reported question keeps 'li' directly after the verb 'imamo', and 'imati' governs the genitive 'vremena'.

activities.title: Pitanja s posla
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague relays your boss's questions and instructions, and you send answers and a question back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Šefica me nešto pitala za tebe.
lines.en: The boss asked me something about you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Što te pitala?
lines.en: What did she ask you?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pitala je jesi li završio izvještaj.
lines.en: She asked whether you had finished the report.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Reci joj da sam ga poslao jučer.
lines.en: Tell her that I sent it yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pitala je i kada dolaziš u ured.
lines.en: She also asked when you are coming to the office.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Odgovori joj da dolazim u devet i pitaj je trebam li ponijeti mapu.
lines.en: Tell her that I am coming at nine and ask her whether I should bring the folder.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Još je rekla neka nazoveš klijenta.
lines.en: She also said you should call the client.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro, rekla mi je da nazovem i prošli tjedan.
lines.en: All right, she told me to call last week as well.

days.title: Sklonidba brojeva: jedan, dva, tri i četiri
days.objective: Use jedan, dva, tri and četiri with the correct gender, the correct noun form and the genitive forms dvaju and dviju.
days.paretoFocus: Prices, dates, quantities and times all hang on these four numbers, and getting dva against dvije wrong is the single most audible beginner mistake.
drills: Write six shopping items with the numbers two, three and four, choosing dva or dvije by gender.
Say how many brothers, sisters, rooms and windows you have, out loud, twice.
Put 'jedan' into five different cases inside five short sentences.

items: When do you use dva and when dvije?
What form does the noun take after dva, tri and četiri?
Give the genitive of the numeral two with a feminine noun.

activities.title: Brojevi i njihovi oblici
activities.intro: 'Jedan' behaves like an adjective and agrees with its noun in gender, number and case. 'Dva' is used with masculine and neuter nouns and 'dvije' with feminine ones, and after dva, dvije, tri and četiri the noun takes the old dual form, which looks like the genitive singular. In the genitive the numeral itself changes to dvaju, dviju, triju and četiriju.

items.hr: Kupio sam dva kruha i dvije boce vode.
items.en: I bought two loaves of bread and two bottles of water.
items.note: dva + muški rod, dvije + ženski rod

items.hr: Imam tri sestre i četiri brata.
items.en: I have three sisters and four brothers.
items.note: after 2, 3, 4 the noun looks like a genitive singular: sestre, brata

items.hr: Razgovarao sam s jednim kolegom o poslu.
items.en: I talked with one colleague about work.
items.note: jedan declines like an adjective: s jednim kolegom

items.hr: Oba prozora su otvorena, a obje sobe su prazne.
items.en: Both windows are open, and both rooms are empty.
items.note: oba for masculine and neuter, obje for feminine

items.hr: Cijena dviju karata je dvadeset eura.
items.en: The price of two tickets is twenty euros.
items.note: genitiv: dvaju (m, n), dviju (ž)

activities.title: Vježba brojeva
activities.intro: Practise matching the numeral to the gender and case of its noun.

questions.prompt: Choose the numeral meaning 'two': '___ godine živim u Zagrebu.'
questions.explanation: 'Godina' is feminine, so the feminine form of the numeral is required.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form of 'jedan': 'Živim u ___ maloj sobi.'
questions.explanation: 'Jedan' declines like an adjective and here it stands in the feminine locative after 'u', matching 'maloj sobi'.

questions.prompt: Write the numeral 'two' in the form used with feminine nouns: '___ knjige čekaju na stolu.'
questions.explanation: 'Knjiga' is feminine, so the numeral takes its feminine form, and the noun stands in the dual form 'knjige'.

questions.prompt: Put the numeral 'two' into the genitive with a feminine noun: 'Cijena ___ ulaznica je previsoka.'
questions.explanation: In the genitive the feminine numeral becomes 'dviju', and the noun takes the genitive plural 'ulaznica'.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I have two brothers and three sisters.'
questions.explanation: 'Brat' is masculine so it takes 'dva', and both nouns appear in the dual form used after two, three and four.

activities.title: Na blagajni
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are buying tickets and souvenirs, and every line contains a number with the right form.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar dan, koliko karata trebate?
lines.en: Good afternoon, how many tickets do you need?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trebam dvije karte za odrasle i jednu za dijete.
lines.en: I need two adult tickets and one for a child.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cijena dviju karata je dvadeset eura, a dječja je pet.
lines.en: The price of two tickets is twenty euros, and the child one is five.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uzet ću i tri razglednice i četiri magneta.
lines.en: I will also take three postcards and four magnets.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znači, ukupno trideset i jedan euro.
lines.en: So that is thirty one euros in total.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Plaćam karticom, hvala.
lines.en: I am paying by card, thank you.

days.title: Glagolski prilog prošli: prepoznavanje u tekstu
days.objective: Recognise verb forms ending in -vši in written Croatian and rewrite them as a normal 'nakon što' clause.
days.paretoFocus: This form is everywhere in news writing and official texts, and once you can unpack it you stop losing the thread of a paragraph.
drills: Find three sentences in any Croatian text you have and underline every word ending in -vši.
Rewrite each of them as a clause beginning with 'nakon što' or 'kad'.
Build the -vši form of five perfective verbs you already know.

items: Which aspect of verb forms the -vši adverb?
Turn 'Došavši kući, skuhao je večeru' into a sentence with 'nakon što'.
Does the -vši form change for gender or number?

activities.title: Oblici na -vši
activities.intro: The past verbal adverb is made from perfective verbs by adding -vši, as in došavši, pročitavši and saznavši. It never changes for gender, number or case, and it always refers to the same subject as the main verb. It means that its action finished first, so you can always replace it with 'nakon što' or 'kad' plus a past tense.

items.hr: Došavši kući, odmah je skuhao večeru.
items.en: Having got home, he immediately cooked dinner.
items.note: došavši = nakon što je došao

items.hr: Pročitavši pismo, ostala je bez riječi.
items.en: Having read the letter, she was left speechless.
items.note: pročitati -> pročitavši

items.hr: Saznavši istinu, odlučili su reagirati.
items.en: Having found out the truth, they decided to react.
items.note: the form is the same for a plural subject

items.hr: Vidjevši da nema nikoga, tiho je zatvorio vrata.
items.en: Having seen that there was nobody there, he quietly closed the door.
items.note: vidjeti -> vidjevši

items.hr: Otišavši s posla, svratio je u trgovinu.
items.en: Having left work, he stopped by the shop.
items.note: otići -> otišavši, the stem changes like in the past participle

activities.title: Vježba prepoznavanja
activities.intro: Practise reading the -vši form and turning it back into an ordinary clause.

questions.prompt: What does 'Ušavši u sobu, upalila je svjetlo.' mean?
options: After entering the room, she turned on the light.
While entering the room, she turned on the light.
Before entering the room, she turned on the light.
In order to enter the room, she turned on the light.
questions.answer: After entering the room, she turned on the light.
questions.explanation: The -vši form is perfective, so its action is finished before the main verb. A simultaneous action would need the present verbal adverb in -ući.

questions.prompt: Which form is the past verbal adverb of 'napisati'?
questions.explanation: The past verbal adverb ends in -vši. 'Pišući' is the present verbal adverb, 'napisan' is the passive participle and 'napisati' is the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Rewrite the beginning with the past verbal adverb of 'završiti': '___ posao, otišli su na ručak.'
questions.explanation: The perfective verb takes -vši on the infinitive stem, giving a form that does not change for number or gender.

questions.prompt: Turn the adverb into a full clause: 'Stigavši u Zagreb, nazvao je prijatelja.' becomes 'Nakon što je ___ u Zagreb, nazvao je prijatelja.'
questions.explanation: The -vši form unpacks into a past tense with the same subject, so the masculine participle is required.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Having heard the news, they immediately call their parents.'
questions.explanation: The adverbial phrase opens the sentence and is followed by the main clause; 'čuti' gives the form 'čuvši'.

activities.title: Članak o knjižnici
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner quotes a written article and you translate each -vši form into everyday speech.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pročitao sam članak o gradskoj knjižnici.
lines.en: I read an article about the city library.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Što piše?
lines.en: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Piše da je knjižnica, preselivši se u novu zgradu, udvostručila broj članova.
lines.en: It says that the library, having moved into a new building, doubled its number of members.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To znači da se prvo preselila, a onda je broj članova porastao.
lines.en: That means it first moved, and then the number of members grew.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ima i rečenica: Saznavši za nove sadržaje, mnogi su se učlanili.
lines.en: There is also a sentence: Having found out about the new facilities, many people joined.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Razgovorno bih rekao: nakon što su saznali za sadržaje, učlanili su se.
lines.en: In everyday speech I would say: after they found out about the facilities, they joined.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tvoja verzija zvuči puno jednostavnije.
lines.en: Your version sounds much simpler.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Glagolski prilog prošli koristimo uglavnom u pisanom tekstu.
lines.en: We use the past verbal adverb mainly in written texts.

days.title: Red riječi i isticanje u rečenici
days.objective: Order the words of a Croatian sentence naturally, keep the clitics in second position and move a word forward to emphasise it.
days.paretoFocus: Croatian word order is free but not random, and the second position rule for clitics is the one thing that makes a sentence sound native or foreign.
drills: Write one neutral sentence with a past tense and mark which word carries the clitic in second position.
Rewrite the same sentence three times, each time fronting a different word, and note what each version emphasises.
Build three sentences with two clitics in a row and check the order dative before accusative.

items: Which position in the clause do clitics take?
Give the order of the clitics sam, mi and ga in one sentence.
Where does the auxiliary 'je' stand inside a group of clitics?

activities.title: Gdje što stoji u rečenici
activities.intro: The neutral order is subject, verb, object, but Croatian lets you move any word to the front to emphasise it, and the emphasised word is the one you say first. Unstressed short words such as sam, je, ću, se, mi and ga are clitics and must sit in second position in their clause. Inside that group the order is li, then the verbal auxiliary, then se, then the dative, then the accusative, with 'je' always last.

items.hr: Marko je sinoć gledao utakmicu.
items.en: Marko watched the match last night.
items.note: neutral order, the clitic 'je' sits second

items.hr: Utakmicu je sinoć gledao Marko, a ne Ivan.
items.en: It was Marko who watched the match last night, not Ivan.
items.note: fronting the object shifts the emphasis to the subject at the end

items.hr: Dala mu ga je bez riječi.
items.en: She gave it to him without a word.
items.note: order: dative mu, accusative ga, and 'je' last

items.hr: Jesi li ti to stvarno rekao?
items.en: Did you really say that?
items.note: li comes immediately after the first word

items.hr: Samo jednu stvar ne razumijem.
items.en: There is only one thing I do not understand.
items.note: samo emphasises the word straight after it

activities.title: Vježba reda riječi
activities.intro: Practise placing clitics and moving words for emphasis.

questions.prompt: Which word order is correct for 'I have already told you that'?
options: Ja sam ti to već rekao.
Ja ti sam to već rekao.
Ja ti to sam već rekao.
Sam ja ti to već rekao.
questions.answer: Ja sam ti to već rekao.
questions.explanation: The clitic group stands in second position, and inside it the verbal auxiliary 'sam' comes before the dative 'ti'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence puts the auxiliary of the third person in the right place?
questions.explanation: The auxiliary 'je' is the one clitic that goes last in the group, after the dative and the accusative pronouns.

questions.prompt: Fill in the missing clitic so the sentence means 'Tomorrow I will let you know the results': 'Sutra ___ ti javiti rezultate.'
questions.explanation: The future auxiliary is a clitic and takes second position, right after the fronted adverb.

questions.prompt: Fill in the focus word meaning 'only': 'Razumijem ___ jednu riječ.'
questions.explanation: This particle stands directly before the word it restricts, here the object phrase.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Ivan bought a new car yesterday.'
questions.explanation: The neutral order starts with the subject, the clitic 'je' follows it in second position, and the time adverb comes before the participle.

activities.title: Tko je to rekao?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You sort out who told whom about a moved meeting, fronting the key word in each answer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tko ti je javio da je sastanak pomaknut?
lines.en: Who told you that the meeting had been moved?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Javila mi je Ana. Ona mi je to rekla jutros.
lines.en: Ana told me. She told me that this morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni nitko nije rekao ni riječi.
lines.en: Nobody said a word to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tebi je to trebao javiti Marko, a ne ja.
lines.en: Marko was the one who should have told you, not me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znači, njemu se moram žaliti.
lines.en: So he is the one I have to complain to.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Upravo tako. Samo njemu, nikome drugom.
lines.en: Exactly. Only to him, nobody else.

days.title: Exam prep begins: format mastery + listening A
days.objective: Know the exam inside-out and drill short-sentence listening.
days.paretoFocus: 25 days to exam-ready. The format (NN 100/2021): 5 sections, ≥60% each on listening/reading/grammar, pass/fail writing & speaking. No surprises allowed.
drills: Study the mock exam in the Quiz tab: section order, task types, points.
Listening drill I.A: six short sentences (heard twice), open short answers, traffic-report style.
Start a fresh exam-phase error log.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name all 5 exam sections and the pass rule.
Re-answer your 2 missed listening items.

activities.title: Format ispita, pet dijelova i pravilo prolaza
activities.intro: Twenty-five days to exam-ready, and the first rule is: no surprises. The official exam (Ispit iz poznavanja hrvatskoga jezika, NN 100/2021) has five sections in a fixed order and a strict pass rule. Learn the shape by heart so that on the day you spend energy on answers, not on figuring out the format.

items.hr: Prvi dio: Razumijevanje slušanoga teksta
items.en: Part one: Listening comprehension
items.note: Each recording is played twice; the text stays hidden. Short sentences and one or two longer announcements, with open answers, MCQ and DA/NE (true/false) tasks.

items.hr: Drugi dio: Razumijevanje čitanoga teksta
items.en: Part two: Reading comprehension
items.note: One or more texts (often a biography or an advert), with open questions and a word-bank gap-fill. All answers come FROM the text.

items.hr: Treći dio: Poznavanje gramatičkih struktura
items.en: Part three: Knowledge of grammatical structures
items.note: Form-choice tasks: pick the correct grammatical form from four options. Genitive plural, futur I spelling, conditional, prepositions, comparatives.

items.hr: Četvrti dio: Pisano sporazumijevanje
items.en: Part four: Written communication
items.note: Two short writing tasks (e.g. describe your day, write a CV or a message). Marked pass/fail against a rubric.

items.hr: Peti dio: Usmeno sporazumijevanje
items.en: Part five: Oral communication
items.note: A conversation with the examiner: introduce yourself, describe, compare. Marked pass/fail.

items.hr: pravilo prolaza
items.en: the pass rule
items.note: At least 60% in each of listening, reading and grammar, AND a pass in writing and speaking. Every section must be passed separately.

items.hr: Slušni tekst čuje se dvaput.
items.en: The listening text is heard twice.
items.note: Use the first play for the gist and the second to verify details, the exam's built-in strategy.

items.hr: Dijakritički znakovi se boduju.
items.en: Diacritics are scored.
items.note: A missing č, ć, š, ž or đ is a spelling error that costs points in the written and gap-fill tasks. Proofread every word.

activities.title: Slušanje A: kratke rečenice i najave
activities.intro: The first listening task, exactly in the exam format: short sentences and announcements, each heard with the text hidden. Catch the one fact that answers the question, traffic reports, times, numbers and DA/NE announcements. Play each item, then answer.

questions.prompt: Koliko se čeka na graničnom prijelazu?
questions.audioText: Na graničnom prijelazu Bregana putnička vozila na izlazu iz zemlje čekaju oko trideset minuta.
questions.explanation: The recording says vehicles wait 'oko trideset minuta' (about 30 minutes) at the Bregana crossing.

questions.prompt: Zašto je zatvorena autocesta kod Karlovca?
questions.audioText: Zbog prometne nesreće privremeno je zatvorena autocesta A1 kod Karlovca. Vozače molimo za strpljenje.
questions.explanation: 'Zbog prometne nesreće' = because of a traffic accident.

questions.prompt: Koji broj zovete ako vam se pokvari auto? (upišite broj)
questions.audioText: Ako vam se na putu pokvari automobil, nazovite Hrvatski autoklub na broj tisuću devetsto osamdeset sedam.
questions.explanation: 1987 is the Croatian Auto Club (HAK) roadside-assistance number.

questions.prompt: U koliko sati počinje predstava?
questions.audioText: Poštovani posjetitelji, večerašnja kazališna predstava počinje u devetnaest i trideset. Molimo vas da zauzmete svoja mjesta.
questions.explanation: 'u devetnaest i trideset' = at 19:30 (half past seven in the evening).

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Trgovina radi nedjeljom.
questions.audioText: Obavještavamo cijenjene kupce da naša trgovina radi od ponedjeljka do subote, a nedjeljom ne radi.
questions.explanation: The shop works Monday to Saturday, and 'nedjeljom ne radi', it is closed on Sundays.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Let za Frankfurt kasni.
questions.audioText: Poštovani putnici, let za Frankfurt predviđen u petnaest sati kasni zbog lošega vremena. Novo vrijeme polijetanja je šesnaest i trideset.
questions.explanation: The flight 'kasni zbog lošega vremena' and the new departure is 16:30 instead of 15:00, so yes, it is delayed.

questions.prompt: Od koliko do koliko sati radi pošta radnim danom? (npr. 'od 9 do 17')
questions.audioText: Pošta u središtu grada radi radnim danom od osam do devetnaest sati, a subotom do trinaest sati.
questions.explanation: 'radnim danom od osam do devetnaest sati' = on weekdays from 8 to 19.

days.title: Listening B: DA/NE statements
days.objective: Master the longer-text listening task with true/false statements.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Listening B: one longer text (event announcement), heard twice, six DA/NE statements. Strategy: read statements BEFORE the audio starts.
drills: Do the DA/NE listening drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Strategy: first listen = gist, second listen = verify each statement.
Log misses in the error log.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the two-listen strategy.
Retell today's announcement in 3 sentences.

activities.title: Slušanje B: strategija za tvrdnje DA/NE
activities.intro: The second listening task is one longer text (usually an event announcement) heard twice, with six DA/NE (true/false) statements. The single most valuable habit: read all the statements BEFORE the audio starts, so you know what to listen for. Learn the strategy and the announcement vocabulary.

items.hr: Pročitaj sve tvrdnje prije slušanja.
items.en: Read all the statements before listening.
items.note: The statements tell you which facts matter. Underline names, numbers, times and any negation.

items.hr: Prvo slušanje = opći smisao.
items.en: First listen = the gist.
items.note: Don't panic if you miss a detail; get the overall picture of what the announcement is about.

items.hr: Drugo slušanje = provjeri svaku tvrdnju.
items.en: Second listen = verify each statement.
items.note: Now confirm each DA/NE one by one against what you hear.

items.hr: Pazi na negacije: ne, nije, nikad.
items.en: Watch for negations.
items.note: A single 'ne' flips a statement from DA to NE. 'Ulaz nije besplatan' means it is NOT free.

items.hr: održati se
items.en: to take place, to be held
items.note: Koncert će se održati u dvorani = the concert will be held in the hall. Reflexive; often in the future.

items.hr: događanje / priredba
items.en: an event / a happening
items.note: kulturno događanje = a cultural event. Common in announcements of festivals and fairs.

items.hr: ulaz je besplatan / ulaznica
items.en: entry is free / a ticket
items.note: ulaz slobodan = free admission. Watch: is entry free (besplatan) or do you need a ticket (ulaznica)?

items.hr: prijaviti se (unaprijed)
items.en: to register (in advance)
items.note: Molimo da se prijavite na broj… = please register at the number… A frequent 'catch' in the statements.

activities.title: Slušanje B: najava proljetnoga sajma knjige
activities.intro: One longer announcement, exactly in the exam format. Read all six DA/NE statements first, then play the announcement (it plays twice, text hidden) and mark each true or false. The final item checks one detail.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Sajam se održava u gradskoj knjižnici.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici', it is held in the city library.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Ulaz na sajam treba platiti.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje', entry is free, so no, you don't pay.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Sajam traje tri dana.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'od petka do nedjelje' = from Friday to Sunday, three days.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Susret sa spisateljicom održava se u nedjelju.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: The meeting with the writer is 'u subotu u podne', on Saturday at noon, not Sunday.

questions.prompt: DA ili NE: Za radionicu se treba prijaviti unaprijed.
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed', you must register in advance for the workshop.

questions.prompt: U koliko sati počinje sajam?
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'Sajam počinje u deset sati' = the fair starts at 10 o'clock.

questions.prompt: Tko dolazi u subotu u podne? (dovršite: poznata hrvatska ______)
questions.audioText: Dragi sugrađani, pozivamo vas na proljetni sajam knjige koji će se održati u gradskoj knjižnici od petka do nedjelje. Sajam počinje u deset sati, a ulaz je besplatan za sve posjetitelje. U subotu u podne održat će se susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom, a djeca mogu sudjelovati u radionici crtanja. Zbog velikog zanimanja molimo vas da se za radionicu prijavite unaprijed na broj knjižnice.
questions.explanation: 'susret s poznatom hrvatskom spisateljicom', a meeting with a famous Croatian (woman) writer. The base form is spisateljica.

days.title: Reading A: biography + open questions
days.objective: Answer open questions on a ~150-word biography quickly and precisely.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Reading A: biography text, seven open questions. Answers must come FROM the text, no creativity, full sentences, correct forms.
drills: Do the biography reading drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab (Marija Jurić Zagorka style).
Practise locating answers: scan for names, dates, places first.
Write answers as full sentences with correct case endings.
Read one more short biography online for speed.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the biography in 4 sentences.
Review 2 errors from the log.

activities.title: Čitanje A: biografija, Marija Jurić Zagorka
activities.intro:
Exam Reading A is a biography of about 150 words followed by open questions. Every answer must come FROM the text - no invention, full sentences, correct case endings. First read the biography below, then learn the reading strategy and the biography vocabulary.

MARIJA JURIĆ ZAGORKA
Marija Jurić Zagorka rodila se 1873. godine u mjestu Negovec pokraj Vrbovca. Bila je prva profesionalna novinarka u Hrvatskoj i jedna od najčitanijih hrvatskih spisateljica. Radila je u novinama Obzor, iako su se mnogi u to doba protivili tome da žena bude novinarka. Pisala je o pravima žena i borila se protiv nepravde. Najpoznatija je po povijesnim romanima kao što su 'Grička vještica' i 'Republikanci', koje su čitali ljudi iz svih društvenih slojeva. Osim toga, pokrenula je i uređivala prve časopise za žene u Hrvatskoj. Cijeli je život živjela skromno, posvećena pisanju i borbi za ravnopravnost. Umrla je u Zagrebu 1957. godine. Danas jedna ulica u Zagrebu nosi njezino ime, a svake se godine dodjeljuje ugledna novinarska nagrada koja se zove po njoj.

items.hr: Strategija: prvo pronađi imena, datume i mjesta.
items.en: Strategy: first locate names, dates and places.
items.note: Scan the text for capitalised names and numbers before you read the questions. That's where most answers hide.

items.hr: Odgovori dolaze IZ teksta.
items.en: Answers come FROM the text.
items.note: Never add what you happen to know about the person. If it isn't in the text, it isn't the answer.

items.hr: Piši potpune rečenice s točnim padežom.
items.en: Write full sentences with the correct case.
items.note: 'Gdje se rodila?' → 'Rodila se u Negovcu' (locative), not just 'Negovec'.

items.hr: novinarka / spisateljica
items.en: journalist (f.) / writer (f.)
items.note: novinar/novinarka; pisac or spisatelj/spisateljica. Zagorka was the first professional female journalist in Croatia.

items.hr: roditi se / umrijeti
items.en: to be born / to die
items.note: rodila se 1873. godine; umrla je 1957. godine. Years take 'godine' in the genitive.

items.hr: roman / povijesni roman
items.en: novel / historical novel
items.note: 'Grička vještica' is her most famous historical novel. roman → G romana, N pl romani.

items.hr: pokrenuti / uređivati
items.en: to launch / to edit
items.note: pokrenula je časopis = she launched a magazine; uređivati = to edit (a publication).

items.hr: dodjeljivati nagradu
items.en: to award a prize
items.note: Nagrada 'Marija Jurić Zagorka' se dodjeljuje novinarima, the prize is awarded to journalists. dodjeljivati se = to be awarded (se-passive).

activities.title: Pitanja uz biografiju
activities.intro: Answer each question from the Zagorka biography you just read. Locate the fact first, then answer. The difficulty climbs from a place to a two-part fact at the end.

questions.prompt: Gdje se rodila Marija Jurić Zagorka?
questions.explanation: 'rodila se 1873. godine u mjestu Negovec pokraj Vrbovca.' Location → locative: u Negovcu.

questions.prompt: Čime se bavila?
questions.explanation: 'prva profesionalna novinarka u Hrvatskoj i jedna od najčitanijih hrvatskih spisateljica.'

questions.prompt: Koje se godine rodila? (upišite broj)
questions.explanation: 'rodila se 1873. godine.'

questions.prompt: Po čemu je najpoznatija?
