lines.en: I disagree. I'd rather watch a team that plays boldly and loses than one that defends and wins.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To ti govoriš dok tvoja momčad ne ispadne. Tada svi žele bodove.
lines.en: You say that until your team gets knocked out. Then everyone wants the points.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Istina je da boli poraz, ali navijač se vraća zbog osjećaja, a ne zbog tablice.
lines.en: It's true that defeat hurts, but a fan comes back for the feeling, not for the standings.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ipak, klub bez pobjeda gubi navijače i novac. Rezultat plaća sve.
lines.en: Still, a club without wins loses fans and money. The result pays for everything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu imaš pravo, novac je stvaran. No klub koji zaboravi igru brzo izgubi dušu, a onda i navijače.
lines.en: There you're right, money is real. But a club that forgets the game quickly loses its soul, and then its fans too.

days.title: Mediji i program
days.objective: Discuss news, shows and adverts and defend how much you trust what you hear and read.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentees cuti and znaciti alongside the media core vijest, emisija, informacija, gledatelj and reklama.
drills: Name in Croatian one show you watch and one you avoid, with a reason for each.
Report one thing you heard on the radio this week, starting with 'Cuo/Cula sam da'.
Explain in one sentence how you decide whether a piece of news is true.

items: How does the relative pronoun ciji agree with the thing possessed?
Which case does the verb vjerovati govern for the person or thing trusted?
What is the difference in aspect between slusati and cuti?

activities.title: Vijesti, emisije i reklame
activities.intro: Media language is full of two very common verbs: čuti (to hear) and značiti (to mean). Around them sit vijest (news item), emisija (broadcast, show), informacija and reklama (advert). Each line states an opinion.

items.hr: Vijesti gledam svako jutro, ali sve manje im vjerujem jer svaka kuća priča svoju priču.
items.en: I watch the news every morning, but I trust it less and less because every channel tells its own story.
items.note: vijest = news item

items.hr: Čuo sam na radiju da su zbog snijega neke ceste zatvorene, pa sam krenuo ranije.
items.en: I heard on the radio that some roads are closed because of the snow, so I set off earlier.
items.note: čuti = to hear; snijeg = snow

items.hr: Ta emisija traje već deset godina i još uvijek ima najviše gledatelja.
items.en: That show has been running for ten years and still has the most viewers.
items.note: emisija = show; gledatelj = viewer

items.hr: Ne razumijem što ta reklama zapravo znači; ponavljaju je sto puta, a poruka ostaje nejasna.
items.en: I don't understand what that advert actually means; they repeat it a hundred times, yet the message stays unclear.
items.note: reklama = advert; značiti = to mean

items.hr: Danas svatko može objaviti informaciju, pa je teže razlikovati vijest od glasine.
items.en: Today anyone can publish information, so it is harder to tell news from rumour.
items.note: informacija = information

activities.title: Vjerovati vijestima
activities.intro: Practise the verbs and nouns of media and information.

questions.prompt: Na radiju sam jutros ___ da će sutra padati snijeg.
questions.explanation: On the radio you hear, so the past participle of čuti is čuo. The other verbs go with seeing or reading, not the radio.

questions.prompt: To je novinar ___ knjigu upravo čitam.
questions.explanation: A spiral to possession: 'whose book' uses čiji, agreeing with the feminine object knjiga in the accusative, hence čiju.

questions.prompt: Ova ___ o divljim životinjama najdraža je mojoj djeci.
questions.explanation: The show is the feminine subject emisija, agreeing with najdraža.

questions.prompt: Ne vjerujem svakoj ___ koju pročitam na internetu.
questions.explanation: Vjerovati governs the dative, so informacija becomes informaciji.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I heard that the show starts at eight.'
questions.explanation: Here da is the conjunction 'that' after čuo sam; the clause keeps normal order, with emisija as its subject.

activities.title: Vjeruješ li još vijestima?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend has given up on the news entirely; you argue for staying informed with a critical eye.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja vijestima više uopće ne vjerujem. Svi lažu, pa gledam samo sport.
lines.en: I don't trust the news at all anymore. Everyone lies, so I only watch sport.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Razumijem umor, ali ako se svi isključe, laž pobijedi bez borbe.
lines.en: I understand the fatigue, but if everyone tunes out, the lie wins without a fight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako da vjerujem kad ista priča na dva kanala zvuči potpuno drukčije?
lines.en: How am I to believe it when the same story sounds completely different on two channels?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Baš zato usporedim nekoliko izvora i sam izvučem zaključak.
lines.en: That's exactly why I compare a few sources and draw my own conclusion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tko ima vremena za to? Lakše je ne čitati ništa.
lines.en: Who has time for that? It's easier to read nothing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se da je naporno, no biti neobaviješten danas je skuplje nego uložiti pet minuta.
lines.en: I agree it's exhausting, but being uninformed today costs more than investing five minutes.

days.title: Osjećaji u nijansama
days.objective: Name feelings precisely and defend an opinion about whether emotions should be shown or hidden.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentee shvatiti and the emotion core dusa, sreca, strah, ponos, sram and tuga that the deck barely touches.
drills: Name in Croatian three feelings you had this week and what caused each.
Say one sentence with 'Zao mi je' and one with 'Drago mi je'.
Explain aloud the difference between ljutnja and strah in your own words.

items: Which case names the person in the phrase 'zao mi je'?
How does the adjective ponosan change before a masculine subject?
What does the perfective verb shvatiti add compared to the imperfective shvacati?

activities.title: Sreća, strah i ponos
activities.intro: Feelings need exact words. Note shvatiti (to realize, grasp), duša (soul), sreća (happiness), strah (fear), ponos (pride) and sram (shame). The dative names the one who feels: žao mi je.

items.hr: Kad sam shvatio da su svi dobro, osjetio sam veliko olakšanje.
items.en: When I realized everyone was fine, I felt great relief.
items.note: shvatiti = to realize; olakšanje = relief

items.hr: Ponosan sam na sestru jer je sama, bez ičije pomoći, završila fakultet.
items.en: I am proud of my sister because she finished university on her own, without anyone's help.
items.note: ponos = pride; ponosan = proud

items.hr: Iza ljutnje se često krije strah, barem kod mene.
items.en: Behind anger fear often hides, at least in my case.
items.note: ljutnja = anger; strah = fear

items.hr: Sreća nije stalno stanje nego niz malih trenutaka, tako ja to vidim.
items.en: Happiness is not a permanent state but a series of small moments, that's how I see it.
items.note: sreća = happiness, luck

items.hr: Duboko u duši znao je da griješi, ali ga je bilo sram to priznati.
items.en: Deep in his soul he knew he was in the wrong, but he was ashamed to admit it.
items.note: duša = soul; sram = shame

activities.title: Kako se osjećaš?
activities.intro: Practise naming feelings with correct forms.

questions.prompt: Tek kad je otišao, ___ sam koliko mi znači.
questions.explanation: A completed realization takes the perfective past shvatio; shvaćam is the present and shvatiti the infinitive.

questions.prompt: ___ je žao što te nisam nazvao na vrijeme.
questions.explanation: A spiral to the dative experiencer: in 'žao mi je' the person who is sorry stands in the dative, Meni.

questions.prompt: Nije to mržnja, nego obična ___: htio bih i ja ono što ti imaš.
questions.explanation: Wanting what another has is ljubomora (jealousy), the nominative subject after nego.

questions.prompt: Poslije svađe osjećala je samo ___ i prazninu.
questions.explanation: The object of osjećati here is tuga (sadness) in the accusative, tugu.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I am proud of my daughter.'
questions.explanation: Ponosan biti na takes the accusative; kći becomes kćer, and svoju agrees with it.

activities.title: Treba li skrivati osjećaje?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend thinks showing emotion is weakness; you argue that hiding it costs more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muškarac ne bi trebao pokazivati strah ni tugu. To je slabost.
lines.en: A man shouldn't show fear or sadness. That's weakness.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mislim da je obrnuto. Sakriti osjećaj ne znači biti jak nego usamljen.
lines.en: I think it's the opposite. To hide a feeling doesn't mean being strong but being lonely.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali ako svima pokažeš što te boli, iskoristit će to protiv tebe.
lines.en: But if you show everyone what hurts you, they'll use it against you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Neki hoće, priznajem. No pred pravim prijateljem šutnja samo gradi zid.
lines.en: Some will, I admit. But in front of a true friend silence only builds a wall.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni je lakše kad ne pričam o tome. Prođe samo od sebe.
lines.en: It's easier for me when I don't talk about it. It passes on its own.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ponekad prođe, ponekad naraste. Ja radije jednom osjetim sram nego godinama nosim teret.
lines.en: Sometimes it passes, sometimes it grows. I'd rather feel shame once than carry the burden for years.

days.title: Pravila i pravo
days.objective: Talk about laws, rules and rights and defend how strictly rules should be applied.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentee policija and the civic-legal core zakon, sud, pravo, pravilo, dozvola, kazna and prekrsaj.
drills: Name in Croatian two rules you think are fair and one you find pointless.
Describe in two sentences a situation in which you would call the police.
Say one sentence beginning 'Svatko ima pravo' about a right you value.

items: How does the relative pronoun koji take a preposition like o in front of it?
Which case follows the preposition bez?
What is the standard Croatian word for the police?

activities.title: Zakon, sud i pravila
activities.intro: Legal life has its own core: zakon (law), sud (court), pravo (a right, also 'law' in general), pravilo (rule), dozvola (permit), kazna (penalty). The everyday word policija appears constantly.

items.hr: Zakon vrijedi za sve jednako, barem bi tako trebalo biti.
items.en: The law applies to everyone equally, at least that's how it should be.
items.note: zakon = law

items.hr: Ako se ne slažeš s kaznom, imaš pravo žaliti se sudu.
items.en: If you disagree with the penalty, you have the right to appeal to the court.
items.note: kazna = penalty; pravo = right; sud = court

items.hr: Svaka igra ima svoja pravila, a tko ih krši, mora snositi posljedice.
items.en: Every game has its rules, and whoever breaks them has to bear the consequences.
items.note: pravilo = rule

items.hr: Za rad s djecom potrebna je posebna dozvola i čista potvrda.
items.en: Working with children requires a special permit and a clean certificate.
items.note: dozvola = permit

items.hr: Policija je došla za nekoliko minuta, što me iskreno iznenadilo.
items.en: The police arrived within a few minutes, which honestly surprised me.
items.note: policija = police

activities.title: Zakon i kazna
activities.intro: Practise the language of rules, rights and enforcement.

questions.prompt: Ako netko prijeđe cestu na crveno, čini ___.
questions.explanation: Breaking a rule is a prekršaj (offence); the other three are the law, the court and a right.

questions.prompt: Ovo je zakon o ___ svi govore ovih dana.
questions.explanation: A spiral to the relative pronoun: after the preposition o it stands in the locative, o kojem.

questions.prompt: Kad su čuli glasnu buku usred noći, susjedi su odmah pozvali ___.
questions.explanation: The object of pozvati is policija in the accusative, policiju.

questions.prompt: Svatko ima svoja ___, ali i svoje obveze prema drugima.
questions.explanation: Rights are prava, the neuter plural of pravo.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'The law must be the same for everyone.'
questions.explanation: Morati takes the infinitive biti; isti (the same) agrees with the masculine zakon, and za sve gives 'for everyone'.

activities.title: Zakon bez iznimke?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants rules applied to the letter; you argue that a judge must weigh circumstances.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zakon je zakon. Tko pogriješi, mora platiti kaznu, bez iznimke.
lines.en: The law is the law. Whoever makes a mistake must pay the penalty, no exceptions.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se da pravila trebaju vrijediti za sve, ali sudac ipak mora gledati okolnosti.
lines.en: I agree that rules should apply to everyone, but a judge still has to look at the circumstances.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ako počneš gledati okolnosti, svatko će imati ispriku.
lines.en: If you start looking at circumstances, everyone will have an excuse.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nije baš tako. Razlika između greške i namjere postoji i u samom zakonu.
lines.en: It's not quite like that. The difference between a mistake and intent exists in the law itself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni je jednostavnije kad je pravilo jasno: prekršio si, snosiš posljedice.
lines.en: It's simpler for me when the rule is clear: you broke it, you bear the consequences.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jasno jest lakše, no pravda bez razuma brzo postane nepravda.
lines.en: Clear is easier, true, but justice without reason quickly becomes injustice.

days.title: Društvo i zajednica
days.objective: Talk about community and society and defend whether small-town closeness or city anonymity suits you better.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentee isti in fresh contexts plus the social core zajednica, drustvo, skupina, odnos, susjed, clan and povjerenje.
drills: Name in Croatian two good and two hard sides of living in a small community.
Describe your relationship with your neighbours in two sentences.
Say one sentence about a group you belong to and why you joined.

items: How do you stack a negative pronoun with a negated verb, as in nikoga nisam?
Which case does the preposition sa (with) govern?
How does the adjective isti change in the locative feminine?

activities.title: Zajednica i pojedinac
activities.intro: Society vocabulary sits between the abstract and the everyday: zajednica (community), društvo (society, company), skupina (group), odnos (relationship), susjed (neighbour), pojedinac (individual). The word isti (the same) returns here.

items.hr: U maloj zajednici svi se poznaju, što ima i dobre i loše strane.
items.en: In a small community everyone knows everyone, which has both good and bad sides.
items.note: zajednica = community

items.hr: Čovjek je društveno biće; mislim da nitko ne može sasvim sam biti sretan.
items.en: A human is a social being; I think nobody can be entirely happy alone.
items.note: društvo = society; društveno biće = social being

items.hr: Postali smo bliski jer nas je ista skupina prijatelja spojila još na fakultetu.
items.en: We became close because the same group of friends brought us together back at university.
items.note: skupina = group; isti = the same

items.hr: Dobar odnos sa susjedima vrijedi više od visoke ograde.
items.en: A good relationship with the neighbours is worth more than a high fence.
items.note: odnos = relationship; susjed = neighbour

items.hr: Pojedinac malo može, ali skupina ljudi može promijeniti cijelo naselje.
items.en: An individual can do little, but a group of people can change a whole neighbourhood.
items.note: pojedinac = individual

activities.title: Ljudi oko nas
activities.intro: Practise the language of community, groups and relationships.

questions.prompt: Već je godinama aktivan ___ istoga planinarskog društva.
questions.explanation: The subject 'is an active member' takes the nominative član; the other endings are instrumental, genitive and dative.

questions.prompt: U novom kvartu isprva ___ nisam poznavao.
questions.explanation: A spiral to negative pronouns: Croatian stacks negatives, so the negated nisam needs nikoga, not nekoga.

questions.prompt: Bez ___ nema pravog prijateljstva; jednom kad ga izgubiš, teško se vraća.
questions.explanation: The preposition bez takes the genitive, so povjerenje becomes povjerenja.

questions.prompt: Odrasla je u mirnoj ___ malog mjesta, daleko od gradske gužve.
questions.explanation: Where someone grew up takes u plus the locative; sredina (setting, environment) becomes sredini.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'We have a good relationship with the neighbours.'
questions.explanation: Odnos is the accusative object of imamo; 'with the neighbours' is sa plus the instrumental plural susjedima.

activities.title: Malo mjesto ili veliki grad?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend feels smothered by small-town closeness; you defend what that closeness gives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: U malom mjestu se guši. Svi znaju sve o tebi, nema mira.
lines.en: In a small town you suffocate. Everyone knows everything about you, there's no peace.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meni je baš to vrijednost. Kad zatreba pomoć, susjed je tu za pet minuta.
lines.en: For me that's exactly the value. When help is needed, the neighbour is there in five minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dok ti ne počne soliti pamet kako da živiš.
lines.en: Until they start telling you how to live your life.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To se događa, priznajem. No u gradu možeš tjednima ne vidjeti nikoga, pa i to ubija.
lines.en: That happens, I admit. But in the city you can go weeks without seeing anyone, and that kills too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja radije biram svoje ljude nego da ih naslijedim s adresom.
lines.en: I'd rather choose my people than inherit them with an address.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pošteno. Možda je istina negdje u sredini: zajednica koju sam biraš, ali koja te i drži.
lines.en: Fair. Maybe the truth is somewhere in between: a community you choose yourself, but one that also holds you.

days.title: Posao II: plan i projekt
days.objective: Talk about plans, projects and meetings at work and defend planning against improvising.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentees plan and dogoditi se plus the work core projekt, sastanak, uspjeh, organizacija, rok and kolega.
drills: Describe your ideal work day in three sentences, naming one task and one meeting.
Say one sentence about something unexpected that happened at work, using dogoditi se in the past.
Argue in one breath why a plan helps or hinders a team.

items: Which verb form follows morati and other modals in standard Croatian?
After the temporal conjunction kad about the future, which tense do you use?
How does the clitic se sit relative to the verb in 'dogodilo se'?

activities.title: Sastanak, plan i rok
activities.intro: Work talk needs plan (plan), projekt (project), sastanak (meeting), rok (deadline), uspjeh (success) and organizacija (organization). The verb dogoditi se (to happen) reports what came up.

items.hr: Na sastanku smo dogovorili plan za idući mjesec, ali nitko nije htio preuzeti najteži zadatak.
items.en: At the meeting we agreed a plan for next month, but nobody wanted to take on the hardest task.
items.note: sastanak = meeting; plan = plan; zadatak = task

items.hr: Ako projekt ne završimo do roka, izgubit ćemo klijenta.
items.en: If we don't finish the project by the deadline, we'll lose the client.
items.note: projekt = project; rok = deadline

items.hr: Uspjeh tvrtke ne ovisi o jednom čovjeku nego o dobroj organizaciji.
items.en: A company's success doesn't depend on one person but on good organization.
items.note: uspjeh = success; organizacija = organization

items.hr: Danas se na poslu dogodilo nešto neobično: šef nam je zahvalio pred svima.
items.en: Something unusual happened at work today: the boss thanked us in front of everyone.
items.note: dogoditi se = to happen

items.hr: Volim kad kolega kaže što misli otvoreno, makar se ne slagali.
items.en: I like it when a colleague says what they think openly, even if we don't agree.
items.note: kolega = colleague

activities.title: Rokovi i dogovori
activities.intro: Practise the language of tasks, deadlines and meetings.

questions.prompt: Do petka moramo ___ ovaj izvještaj i poslati ga klijentu.
questions.explanation: Croatian modals take the infinitive, so morati is followed by završiti. 'Da završimo' follows the Serbian pattern.

questions.prompt: Kad ___ sve zadatke, otići ćemo na zajedničku večeru.
questions.explanation: A spiral to future time clauses: after kad about the future Croatian uses the perfective present završimo, not the future.

questions.prompt: Cijeli tim radi na istom ___ već tri mjeseca.
questions.explanation: Working on something takes na plus the locative; projekt becomes projektu.

questions.prompt: Na kraju ___ dobili smo nove ideje i jasan raspored.
questions.explanation: 'At the end of' takes the genitive, so sastanak becomes sastanka.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Something strange happened at work today.'
questions.explanation: The clitic se comes early in the sentence; dogodilo agrees with the neuter nešto, and čudno describes it.

activities.title: Plan ili improvizacija?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your colleague prefers to dive in and improvise; you defend planning, but grant the cost of endless meetings.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Previše planiramo. Meni je bolje krenuti odmah pa usput rješavati.
lines.en: We plan too much. I prefer to start right away and solve things as I go.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne bih se složio. Bez jasnog plana projekt se raspadne kod prvog problema.
lines.en: I wouldn't agree. Without a clear plan the project falls apart at the first problem.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali planovi se svejedno ruše čim se nešto nepredviđeno dogodi.
lines.en: But plans collapse anyway as soon as something unexpected happens.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ruše se, da, ali s planom bar znaš što si žrtvovao. Bez njega samo gasiš požare.
lines.en: They collapse, yes, but with a plan you at least know what you sacrificed. Without one you just put out fires.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni previše sastanaka oduzima vrijeme za pravi posao.
lines.en: For me too many meetings take away time for the real work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu se slažem. Plan da, beskrajni sastanci ne. Dogovor od deset minuta vrijedi više od dva sata priče.
lines.en: There I agree. A plan yes, endless meetings no. A ten-minute agreement is worth more than two hours of talk.

days.title: Obrazovanje II: ispit i znanje
days.objective: Talk about exams, grades and skills and defend what matters more, the grade or the knowledge.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentee pokazati plus the education core ispit, znanje, ocjena, vjestina, gradivo, diploma and predavanje.
drills: Describe your last exam in three sentences, naming the subject and how it went.
Say one sentence about a skill you value more than any grade.
Explain aloud why a good grade and real knowledge are not always the same.

items: How do you form the superlative of an adjective like tezak?
What ending does gradivo (neuter) take as a direct object?
What is the difference in aspect between polagati and poloziti an exam?

activities.title: Ispit, ocjena i vještina
activities.intro: School and study bring ispit (exam), znanje (knowledge), ocjena (grade), vještina (skill), diploma and predavanje (lecture). The verb pokazati (to show) appears whenever results are made clear.

items.hr: Položio sam ispit iz prve, iako sam mislio da ću pasti.
items.en: I passed the exam on the first try, even though I thought I'd fail.
items.note: ispit = exam; položiti = to pass

items.hr: Ocjena nije najvažnija; više vrijedi znanje koje ostane.
items.en: The grade isn't the most important thing; the knowledge that stays is worth more.
items.note: ocjena = grade; znanje = knowledge

items.hr: Na predavanju je profesor pokazao kako se teorija koristi u praksi.
items.en: In the lecture the professor showed how the theory is used in practice.
items.note: predavanje = lecture; pokazati = to show

items.hr: Diploma otvara vrata, ali vještine odlučuju hoćeš li ostati u sobi.
items.en: A diploma opens doors, but skills decide whether you stay in the room.
items.note: diploma = diploma; vještina = skill

items.hr: Odlučila sam upisati tečaj hrvatskog jer želim čitati knjige u izvorniku.
items.en: I decided to enrol in a Croatian course because I want to read books in the original.
items.note: upisati = to enrol

activities.title: Učenje i ocjene
activities.intro: Practise the language of exams, grades and skills.

questions.prompt: Cijeli tjedan učim jer u ponedjeljak ___ važan ispit.
questions.explanation: Taking an exam as a scheduled, ongoing process uses the imperfective present polažem; položiti means to pass it.

questions.prompt: Ovaj mi je ispit bio ___ od svih dosad.
questions.explanation: A spiral to comparison: 'the hardest of all so far' calls for the superlative najteži, not the comparative teži.

questions.prompt: Ako dobro ponoviš cijelo ___, nemaš čega se bojati.
questions.explanation: The material you revise is gradivo (neuter), whose accusative equals the nominative.

questions.prompt: Profesorica nam je jasno ___ gdje smo pogriješili.
questions.explanation: The feminine subject profesorica takes the participle pokazala from pokazati.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I passed the exam on the first try.'
questions.explanation: Položiti is perfective; the idiom 'iz prve' means on the first attempt.

activities.title: Ocjena ili znanje?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend blames students for chasing grades; you point the finger at the system that measures them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Djecu danas zanima samo ocjena. Nitko ne uči zbog znanja.
lines.en: Kids today care only about the grade. Nobody studies for the knowledge.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Djelomično si u pravu, ali kriv je i sustav koji nagrađuje samo brojku.
lines.en: You're partly right, but the system that rewards only a number is also to blame.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Svejedno, tko želi znati, naći će način bez obzira na sustav.
lines.en: All the same, whoever wants to know will find a way regardless of the system.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Naći će, ali zašto bismo krivili dijete umjesto škole koja mjeri pogrešnu stvar?
lines.en: They will, but why blame the child instead of the school that measures the wrong thing?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ipak, ocjene su barem nešto konkretno. Kako drukčije usporediti učenike?
lines.en: Still, grades are at least something concrete. How else do you compare pupils?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se da treba mjerilo, no ono bi trebalo pokazati vještinu, a ne samo dobro pamćenje.
lines.en: I agree you need a measure, but it should show skill, not just a good memory.

days.title: Znanost i tehnologija II
days.objective: Talk about research, systems and devices and defend whether technology helps or dulls us.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentees ideja and pronaci plus the web-register core sustav, model, proces, istrazivanje and uredaj.
drills: Name in Croatian one device you rely on and one you could live without.
Say one sentence about an idea you have for saving time, using ideja.
Explain aloud one way technology has changed how you remember things.

items: Which case marks the means in 'saljemo internetom'?
What is the perfective past participle of pronaci for a plural subject?
How do you form the accusative of a feminine noun like ideja?

activities.title: Sustav, proces i uređaj
activities.intro: Science and tech language centres on sustav (system), model, proces (process), istraživanje (research) and uređaj (device). The verb pronaći (to find) and the noun ideja (idea) fill two frequent gaps.

items.hr: Novo istraživanje pokazuje da kratke šetnje poboljšavaju pamćenje.
items.en: New research shows that short walks improve memory.
items.note: istraživanje = research

items.hr: Cijeli sustav radi loše ako i jedan mali dio zakaže.
items.en: The whole system works badly if even one small part fails.
items.note: sustav = system

items.hr: Dobili smo zanimljivu ideju kako ubrzati cijeli proces.
items.en: We got an interesting idea for how to speed up the whole process.
items.note: ideja = idea; proces = process

items.hr: Znanstvenici su napokon pronašli uzrok kvara.
items.en: The scientists finally found the cause of the fault.
items.note: pronaći = to find

items.hr: Ovaj model uređaja troši puno manje struje od starijega.
items.en: This model of the device uses much less electricity than the older one.
items.note: model = model; uređaj = device

activities.title: Rješenja i ideje
activities.intro: Practise the language of systems, research and devices.

questions.prompt: Konačno smo ___ rješenje koje svima odgovara.
questions.explanation: A completed find takes the perfective past pronašli; pronaći is the infinitive and pronalazimo the present.

questions.prompt: Podatke šaljemo ___ preko sigurne veze.
questions.explanation: A spiral to the instrumental: the means by which something is sent stands in the instrumental, internetom.

questions.prompt: Ovaj novi ___ mjeri broj koraka i kvalitetu sna.
questions.explanation: The thing that measures is the device, uređaj, the nominative subject.

questions.prompt: Imam dobru ___ kako bismo uštedjeli vrijeme na sastancima.
questions.explanation: The object of imati is ideja in the accusative, ideju.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Scientists have found a new solution.'
questions.explanation: The auxiliary su plus pronašli forms the perfect; novo rješenje is the neuter accusative object.

activities.title: Pomaže li nam tehnologija?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend thinks technology makes us lazy; you argue it frees the mind if used well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sva ta tehnologija nas zaglupljuje. Više se nitko ne trudi ništa zapamtiti.
lines.en: All this technology dumbs us down. Nobody bothers to remember anything anymore.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vidim to drukčije. Kad me stroj oslobodi pamćenja brojeva, imam više prostora za ideje.
lines.en: I see it differently. When the machine frees me from remembering numbers, I have more room for ideas.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ideje? Ljudi cijeli dan zure u ekran i ne razgovaraju.
lines.en: Ideas? People stare at a screen all day and don't talk.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To je stvarni problem, priznajem, no kriv je način korištenja, a ne sam uređaj.
lines.en: That's a real problem, I admit, but it's the way of using it that's to blame, not the device itself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Uvijek kažeš tako, ali sustav je napravljen da nas drži prikovanima za ekran.
lines.en: You always say that, but the system is built to keep us glued to the screen.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se da nas žele zadržati. Zato biram alat svjesno, umjesto da alat bira mene.
lines.en: I agree they want to keep us. That's why I choose the tool consciously, instead of the tool choosing me.

days.title: Umjetnost II
days.objective: Talk about theatre, exhibitions and music and defend whether art has to be beautiful to matter.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the arts core kazaliste, predstava, izlozba, umjetnik, publika, glazba, djelo and koncert.
drills: Name in Croatian the last performance or exhibition you saw and one word for how it felt.
Say one sentence about a kind of music that helps you concentrate.
Argue in one breath whether art must please to be good.

items: How do the demonstratives ovaj and onaj contrast near and far?
Which case does u take to say where an event took place?
What is the standard Croatian word for a theatre?

activities.title: Kazalište, izložba i glazba
activities.intro: The arts bring kazalište (theatre), predstava (performance), izložba (exhibition), umjetnik (artist), publika (audience), glazba (music) and djelo (work). Each sentence takes a small aesthetic stand.

items.hr: Sinoć smo bili u kazalištu na predstavi koja me nasmijala do suza.
items.en: Last night we were at the theatre at a performance that made me laugh to tears.
items.note: kazalište = theatre; predstava = performance

items.hr: Ta izložba slika mladih umjetnika privukla je puno publike.
items.en: That exhibition of paintings by young artists drew a big audience.
items.note: izložba = exhibition; umjetnik = artist; publika = audience

items.hr: Glazba mi pomaže kad učim; bez nje se teže koncentriram.
items.en: Music helps me when I study; without it I concentrate less easily.
items.note: glazba = music

items.hr: Njegovo najpoznatije djelo visi u galeriji već sto godina.
items.en: His most famous work has been hanging in the gallery for a hundred years.
items.note: djelo = work

items.hr: Poslije koncerta cijela je publika ustala i dugo pljeskala.
items.en: After the concert the whole audience stood up and applauded for a long time.
items.note: koncert = concert

activities.title: U galeriji i kazalištu
activities.intro: Practise the language of performances, exhibitions and music.

questions.prompt: Karte za sutrašnji ___ rasprodane su već tjedan dana.
questions.explanation: The object 'tickets for tomorrow's concert' takes the accusative koncert after za; the others are locative, genitive and instrumental.

questions.prompt: Sviđa mi se ___ slika na zidu do prozora, ne ona kraj vrata.
questions.explanation: A spiral to demonstratives: the near picture is the subject, feminine nominative ova, contrasted with the far ona.

questions.prompt: U galeriji je otvorena nova ___ fotografija iz cijeloga svijeta.
questions.explanation: The new thing that opened is izložba (exhibition), the feminine nominative subject.

questions.prompt: Cijela ___ ustala je na kraju predstave i dugo pljeskala.
questions.explanation: The whole audience is publika, the feminine nominative subject of ustala.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'We were at the theatre yesterday.'
questions.explanation: Being present somewhere takes u plus the locative, u kazalištu, with the clitic smo in second position.

activities.title: Mora li umjetnost biti lijepa?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend rejects modern art as empty; you argue that meaning matters more than beauty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne razumijem modernu umjetnost. Bijelo platno s jednom crtom nije djelo.
lines.en: I don't understand modern art. A white canvas with one line isn't a work of art.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Razumijem tvoju ljutnju, ali mislim da umjetnost ne mora biti lijepa da bi nešto značila.
lines.en: I understand your frustration, but I think art doesn't have to be beautiful to mean something.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znači, sve prolazi? I moje dijete može objesiti crtež u galeriju?
lines.en: So anything goes? My child can hang a drawing in a gallery too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne baš sve. Razlika je u tome je li iza djela ideja koju autor može objasniti.
lines.en: Not quite anything. The difference is whether there's an idea behind the work that the author can explain.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni je izložba uspjela tek ako iz nje izađem s nekim osjećajem.
lines.en: For me an exhibition succeeds only if I come out of it with some feeling.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu se potpuno slažem. Ako te predstava ili slika ostavi ravnodušnim, promašila je cilj.
lines.en: There I fully agree. If a performance or a painting leaves you indifferent, it has missed its aim.

days.title: Povijest i sjećanje
days.objective: Talk about history and remembrance and defend whether old heritage should yield to new building.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentee jednom plus the historical core povijest, stoljece, spomenik, sjecanje, dogadaj and bastina.
drills: Name in Croatian one historical site in your area and roughly how old it is.
Retell in two sentences one event from your town's past.
Say one sentence beginning 'Jednom davno' about how a place used to look.

items: When does the perfective past mark a sudden event against a background?
Which case follows the phrase 'vise od' with a number?
Where does the clitic je sit in a sentence beginning with a demonstrative like ovaj?

activities.title: Spomenik, stoljeće i baština
activities.intro: History talk uses povijest (history), stoljeće (century), spomenik (monument), sjećanje (memory), događaj (event) and baština (heritage). The adverb jednom (once) opens many a story.

items.hr: Ovaj je spomenik podignut u čast ljudima koji su poginuli u ratu.
items.en: This monument was raised in honour of the people who died in the war.
items.note: spomenik = monument

items.hr: Naš grad ima bogatu povijest koja seže više od tisuću godina unatrag.
items.en: Our city has a rich history that reaches back more than a thousand years.
items.note: povijest = history

items.hr: U devetnaestom stoljeću ovdje je bila samo mala ribarska luka.
items.en: In the nineteenth century there was only a small fishing harbour here.
items.note: stoljeće = century

items.hr: Jednom davno na ovom su trgu održavali sajmove svake nedjelje.
items.en: Once upon a time they held fairs on this square every Sunday.
items.note: jednom = once

items.hr: Sjećanje na stare običaje čuva se u pjesmama i pričama.
items.en: The memory of old customs is preserved in songs and stories.
items.note: sjećanje = memory

activities.title: Prošli dani
activities.intro: Practise the language of history and remembrance.

questions.prompt: Taj se povijesni ___ dogodio prije više od sto godina.
questions.explanation: The subject 'that historical event' takes the nominative događaj; the other endings are genitive, dative and instrumental.

questions.prompt: Dok su radnici kopali temelje, ___ su na rimske novčiće.
questions.explanation: A spiral to aspect: the digging is the background, and the sudden discovery takes the perfective naišli.

questions.prompt: Stari grad pod zaštitom je jer je dio svjetske ___.
questions.explanation: 'Part of' takes the genitive, so baština (heritage) becomes baštine.

questions.prompt: U prošlom ___ ovaj kraj nije imao ni struje ni ceste.
questions.explanation: 'In the past century' takes u plus the locative; stoljeće becomes stoljeću.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'This monument is more than a hundred years old.'
questions.explanation: The clitic je takes second position right after Ovaj; star agrees with spomenik, and 'više od sto godina' gives the age.

activities.title: Čuvati staro ili graditi novo?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend would clear an old monument for a new square; you argue that a city needs its memory.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Taj stari spomenik samo smeta prometu. Bolje da naprave novi trg.
lines.en: That old monument just gets in the way of traffic. Better they make a new square.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne slažem se. Grad bez povijesti je kao čovjek bez sjećanja.
lines.en: I disagree. A city without history is like a person without memory.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali ne možemo živjeti u prošlosti. Ljudima trebaju stanovi, a ne kamenje.
lines.en: But we can't live in the past. People need flats, not stones.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trebaju, istina. No jednom kad srušiš baštinu, ne možeš je vratiti ni za sav novac.
lines.en: They do, true. But once you tear down heritage, you can't bring it back for all the money in the world.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Koga danas brine događaj od prije dvjesto godina?
lines.en: Who today cares about an event from two hundred years ago?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Možda malo koga sada, ali stoljeće koje zaboravi odakle je došlo lako izgubi i kamo ide.
lines.en: Maybe few people now, but a century that forgets where it came from easily loses where it's going too.

days.title: Putovanja II: granica i prtljaga
days.objective: Handle borders, luggage and delays when travelling and defend planning a trip against leaving it to chance.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentee gospodin plus the travel core granica, putovnica, carina, odgoda, prtljaga, let and vlak.
drills: Describe in three sentences the last time a trip did not go to plan.
Say one polite sentence to an official at a counter, addressing him as gospodine.
Argue in one breath for booking ahead or travelling freely.

items: What is the vocative form used to address a man politely?
Which case follows the preposition bez?
Which case follows the preposition s meaning 'from' a place like a platform?

activities.title: Na granici i u zračnoj luci
activities.intro: Travel brings granica (border), putovnica (passport), carina (customs), odgoda (delay, postponement), prtljaga (luggage) and let (flight). Officials are addressed politely: gospodine.

items.hr: Na granici su nam pregledali putovnice, ali sve je prošlo brzo.
items.en: At the border they checked our passports, but it all went quickly.
items.note: granica = border; putovnica = passport

items.hr: Zbog magle je let imao veliku odgodu, pa smo satima sjedili u zračnoj luci.
items.en: Because of the fog the flight had a big delay, so we sat in the airport for hours.
items.note: let = flight; odgoda = delay

items.hr: Gospodin za šalterom ljubazno mi je objasnio gdje predati prtljagu.
items.en: The gentleman at the counter kindly explained to me where to hand in the luggage.
items.note: gospodin = gentleman; prtljaga = luggage

items.hr: Kroz carinu prođeš brže ako nemaš ništa za prijaviti.
items.en: You get through customs faster if you have nothing to declare.
items.note: carina = customs

items.hr: Vlak je kasnio pola sata, pa sam propustio vezu za Rijeku.
items.en: The train was half an hour late, so I missed the connection to Rijeka.
items.note: vlak = train

activities.title: Na putu
activities.intro: Practise the language of borders, luggage and delays.

questions.prompt: Molim vas, s kojeg ___ kreće vlak za Split?
questions.explanation: 'From which platform' takes s plus the genitive, so peron becomes perona.

questions.prompt: Dobar dan, ___! Mogu li vidjeti vašu kartu?
questions.explanation: A spiral to the vocative: to address a man you use gospodine, not the nominative gospodin.

questions.prompt: Bez valjane ___ ne možeš prijeći granicu.
questions.explanation: The preposition bez takes the genitive, so putovnica becomes putovnice.

questions.prompt: Zbog gustog snijega naš je let imao veliku ___.
questions.explanation: The delay is the object of imati in the accusative; odgoda becomes odgodu.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'The train was half an hour late.'
questions.explanation: The clitic je stands second; kasnio is the past of kasniti, and 'pola sata' takes the genitive sata.

activities.title: Isplaniran put ili sloboda?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend travels on impulse; you defend planning, but grant the charm of surprise.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mrzim planirati putovanje. Kupim kartu i krenem, bez rasporeda.
lines.en: I hate planning a trip. I buy a ticket and set off, without a schedule.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meni je to previše rizično. Bez rezervacije znaš da ćeš zbog gužve ostati bez sobe.
lines.en: That's too risky for me. Without a reservation you know that because of the crowds you'll be left without a room.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali baš tako doživiš prave stvari. Odgoda leta me jednom odvela u najljepši grad.
lines.en: But that's exactly how you experience the real things. A flight delay once took me to the most beautiful city.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Priznajem da iznenađenje ima čar. No kad putujem s djecom, mir mi je važniji od pustolovine.
lines.en: I admit that surprise has its charm. But when I travel with children, peace matters more to me than adventure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: S djecom da, tu popuštam. Ali sam bih uvijek birao slobodu.
lines.en: With children, yes, there I give in. But on my own I'd always choose freedom.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pošteno. Možda je pravi odgovor lagani plan koji ostavlja mjesta i za slučaj.
lines.en: Fair. Maybe the right answer is a light plan that leaves room for chance too.

days.title: Odnosi II: obitelj i ljubav
days.objective: Talk about family, partners and reconciliation and defend whether marriage still means anything.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the worst spoken absentees mama, tata, djevojka and decko, plus brak, svada, pomirenje, vjencanje and veza.
drills: Name in Croatian three members of your family and one word for your relationship with each.
Describe in two sentences how you make up after an argument.
Argue in one breath for or against marriage.

items: What is the correct order of the clitics ste and se in a question?
Which case does u take to say you are 'in a relationship'?
Which case follows 'doci do' to say something 'came to' happen?

activities.title: Mama, tata i ljubav
activities.intro: The most everyday family words are mama, tata, djevojka (girlfriend) and dečko (boyfriend). Around them sit brak (marriage), svađa (quarrel), pomirenje (reconciliation) and vjenčanje (wedding).

items.hr: Mama i tata su u braku već trideset godina i još se drže za ruke.
items.en: Mum and dad have been married for thirty years and still hold hands.
items.note: mama = mum; tata = dad; brak = marriage

items.hr: Moja djevojka i ja rijetko se svađamo, ali kad se posvađamo, brzo se pomirimo.
items.en: My girlfriend and I rarely quarrel, but when we do, we make up quickly.
items.note: djevojka = girlfriend; pomiriti se = to make up

items.hr: Njezin dečko je jučer prvi put došao na obiteljski ručak.
items.en: Her boyfriend came to the family lunch for the first time yesterday.
items.note: dečko = boyfriend

items.hr: Nakon svake svađe dolazi pomirenje, samo ako oboje to žele.
items.en: After every quarrel comes reconciliation, but only if both want it.
items.note: svađa = quarrel; pomirenje = reconciliation

items.hr: Prava ljubav nije samo osjećaj nego i odluka da ostaneš kad je teško.
items.en: True love is not just a feeling but also a decision to stay when it's hard.
items.note: ljubav = love

activities.title: Obitelj i veze
activities.intro: Practise the language of family, partners and making up.

questions.prompt: Već tri godine je u ozbiljnoj ___ s kolegom s posla.
questions.explanation: 'In a relationship' takes u plus the locative, so veza becomes vezi.

questions.prompt: Zašto ___ opet posvađali oko takve sitnice?
questions.explanation: A spiral to clitic order: the auxiliary ste comes before the reflexive se, giving 'ste se'.

questions.prompt: Na ___ je bilo dvjesto uzvanika i svirala je živa glazba.
questions.explanation: 'At the wedding' takes na plus the locative; vjenčanje becomes vjenčanju.

questions.prompt: Posvađali su se ujutro, a do večeri je već došlo do ___.
questions.explanation: 'It came to' takes doći do plus the genitive, so pomirenje becomes pomirenja.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Mum and dad have been married for thirty years.'
questions.explanation: 'To be married' is 'biti u braku' with the locative; the duration 'trideset godina' takes the genitive plural.

activities.title: Je li brak još potreban?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend sees marriage as a needless formality; you argue it is a public promise that changes something.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Brak je danas nepotreban papir. Ljubav ne treba potvrdu države.
lines.en: Marriage today is a needless piece of paper. Love doesn't need a state's confirmation.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Razumijem misao, ali za mene brak nije papir nego javno obećanje.
lines.en: I understand the thought, but for me marriage isn't paper but a public promise.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Obećanje možeš dati i bez vjenčanja. Zašto trošiti novac na svečanost?
lines.en: You can make a promise without a wedding too. Why spend money on a ceremony?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Možeš, slažem se. No nešto se promijeni kad to kažeš pred svima koje voliš.
lines.en: You can, I agree. But something changes when you say it in front of everyone you love.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A kad dođe prva velika svađa, taj papir te neće spasiti.
lines.en: And when the first big quarrel comes, that paper won't save you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Neće samo po sebi, tu imaš pravo. Ali pomiriti se lakše je kad si rekao ostajem, a ne vidjet ću.
lines.en: Not by itself, there you're right. But making up is easier when you've said 'I'm staying', not 'we'll see'.

days.title: Svakodnevni mali razgovori
days.objective: Handle small talk when you run into someone and defend actually staying in touch rather than leaving it to chance.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentees opet and nikada plus everyday-talk words bas, red, cekati, guzva, zauzet and javiti se.
drills: Practise aloud three warm openers for meeting someone you haven't seen in a while.
Say two sentences complaining lightly about a queue or a crowd.
Make a concrete plan to meet a friend, with a day rather than 'sometime'.

items: Why does nikada need a negated verb in the same clause?
Which case does u take to say you are waiting 'in a queue'?
How does the reflexive verb javiti se behave in the future tense?

activities.title: Baš mi je drago
activities.intro: Casual talk runs on small words: baš (really, just), opet (again), nikada (never), plus red (order, queue), gužva (crowd) and the reflexive javiti se (to get in touch). Each line sounds like real spoken Croatian.

items.hr: Baš mi je drago što smo se opet sreli; čini mi se da se nismo vidjeli sto godina.
items.en: I'm really glad we ran into each other again; it feels like we haven't seen each other in ages.
items.note: baš = really, just; opet = again

items.hr: Ma daj, ti nikada nisi stigao na vrijeme, a sad si prvi.
items.en: Oh come on, you were never on time, and now you're first.
items.note: nikada = never

items.hr: Kakva gužva danas u gradu! Pola sata sam čekao u redu na pošti.
items.en: What a crowd in town today! I waited half an hour in the queue at the post office.
items.note: gužva = crowd; red = queue, order

items.hr: Ako slučajno prolaziš pored mene, javi se pa idemo na kavu.
items.en: If you happen to pass by me, get in touch and we'll go for a coffee.
items.note: slučajno = by chance; javiti se = to get in touch

items.hr: Nemam vremena za dugu priču, sav sam zauzet do kraja tjedna.
items.en: I don't have time for a long chat, I'm completely busy until the end of the week.
items.note: zauzet = busy

activities.title: Usput na ulici
activities.intro: Practise the small words and phrases of everyday conversation.

questions.prompt: ___ te dugo nisam vidio! Gdje si nestao?
questions.explanation: Baš adds emphatic 'really' in speech; vrlo, jako and puno modify amounts, not this kind of exclamation.

questions.prompt: On ___ ne kasni, zato me sada brine što ga još nema.
questions.explanation: A spiral to double negation: the negated ne kasni needs nikada, which cannot stand with a positive verb.

questions.prompt: Strpi se malo, moramo pričekati u ___ kao i svi ostali.
questions.explanation: 'In the queue' takes u plus the locative; red becomes redu.

questions.prompt: Sav sam ___ ovaj tjedan, javit ću ti se čim uhvatim malo vremena.
questions.explanation: 'I'm completely busy' uses the adjective zauzet agreeing with the masculine speaker.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'We haven't seen each other for ages.'
questions.explanation: The idiom 'sto godina' means 'for ages'; the reflexive se follows the negated auxiliary nismo.

activities.title: Hajde da se stvarno čujemo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You bump into an old friend; you both admit you never keep in touch, and you push for a real plan.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Baš mi je drago! Nismo se vidjeli sto godina, gdje si nestao?
lines.en: I'm so glad! We haven't seen each other in ages, where did you disappear to?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam, sav sam zauzet s poslom. Uvijek kažem da ću se javiti, pa nikada ne stignem.
lines.en: I know, I'm completely swamped with work. I always say I'll get in touch, then I never manage.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Svi to kažu, a onda se slučajno sretnemo u redu na pošti.
lines.en: Everyone says that, and then we run into each other by chance in the queue at the post office.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Imaš pravo, previše se oslanjamo na slučaj. Hajde da ovaj put stvarno dogovorimo kavu.
lines.en: You're right, we rely on chance too much. Let's actually arrange a coffee this time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vjerovat ću kad vidim. Ti opet imaš tisuću obveza.
lines.en: I'll believe it when I see it. You've got a thousand obligations again.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zaslužio sam tu sumnju. Zato biram odmah datum, ne nekad ovih dana.
lines.en: I've earned that doubt. That's why I'm picking a date right now, not 'sometime soon'.

days.title: Vjera i tradicija
days.objective: Talk about faith, holidays and customs and defend celebrating traditions even without belief.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the spoken absentee bog plus the cultural core crkva, misa, blagdan, obicaj, vjera, post and tradicija.
drills: Name in Croatian two holidays your family keeps and one custom for each.
Say one sentence about a tradition you would not want to lose.
Argue in one breath whether customs mean anything without belief.

items: When does the imperfective present describe a repeated, habitual action?
Which case does the accusative feminine noun vjera take after postovati?
What is the standard Croatian word for a church holiday, a blagdan?

activities.title: Bog, crkva i običaji
activities.intro: Cultural and religious life brings bog (god), crkva (church), misa (mass), blagdan (holy day, holiday), običaj (custom), vjera (faith) and post (fasting). Each sentence takes a respectful position.

items.hr: Za Božić se cijela obitelj okupi za istim stolom, ma koliko bili udaljeni preko godine.
items.en: At Christmas the whole family gathers around the same table, however far apart they are during the year.
items.note: obitelj = family

items.hr: Nedjeljom baka ide na misu, a djed je čeka pred crkvom.
items.en: On Sundays grandma goes to mass, and grandpa waits for her in front of the church.
items.note: misa = mass; crkva = church

items.hr: Mnogi ne vjeruju u Boga, ali poštuju stare običaje i blagdane.
items.en: Many don't believe in God, yet they respect the old customs and holidays.
items.note: bog = god; običaj = custom; blagdan = holiday

items.hr: Post prije Uskrsa za neke je vjera, a za druge samo navika.
items.en: The fast before Easter is faith for some and mere habit for others.
items.note: post = fast; vjera = faith

items.hr: Svaki kraj ima svoju tradiciju koju je šteta zaboraviti.
items.en: Every region has its own tradition that it would be a shame to forget.
items.note: tradicija = tradition

activities.title: Blagdani i običaji
activities.intro: Practise the language of faith, holidays and customs.

questions.prompt: Na ___ se okuplja mnogo vjernika, osobito za velike blagdane.
questions.explanation: 'At mass' takes na plus the locative; misa becomes misi.

questions.prompt: Svake nedjelje baka ___ u crkvu, kiša ili sunce.
questions.explanation: A spiral to aspect: a repeated, habitual action takes the imperfective ide, while ode and pođe mark a single completed going.

questions.prompt: Iako nije vjernik, poštuje tuđu ___ i nikada je ne ismijava.
questions.explanation: The object of poštovati is vjera in the accusative, vjeru.

questions.prompt: Božić i Uskrs najveći su kršćanski ___.
questions.explanation: The plural subject 'the greatest Christian holidays' is blagdani.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Every region has its own customs.'
questions.explanation: Svaki kraj is the subject; svoje refers back to it, and običaje is the accusative plural object.

activities.title: Slaviti bez vjere?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend calls it hypocritical to keep holidays without belief; you argue custom can carry other meaning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne vjerujem u Boga, pa mi je licemjerno slaviti blagdane.
lines.en: I don't believe in God, so celebrating holidays feels hypocritical to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne bih to nazvao licemjerjem. Za mene je blagdan i običaj koji drži obitelj na okupu.
lines.en: I wouldn't call it hypocrisy. For me a holiday is also a custom that keeps the family together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali običaj bez vjere je prazan. Zašto post ako ne vjeruješ?
lines.en: But a custom without faith is empty. Why fast if you don't believe?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Post shvaćam kao odmor od viška, a ne samo kao vjeru. Tradicija može imati više značenja.
lines.en: I understand fasting as a rest from excess, not only as faith. A tradition can have more than one meaning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni se čini da tako biraš samo ono što ti odgovara.
lines.en: It seems to me that this way you pick only what suits you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Možda i biram, priznajem. No radije čuvam lijep običaj nego da ga odbacim jer mi ne pristaje savršeno.
lines.en: Maybe I do pick, I admit. But I'd rather keep a fine custom than throw it away because it doesn't fit me perfectly.

days.title: Čitam hrvatski
days.objective: Talk about reading habits in Croatian and defend paper against the screen, or the other way round.
days.paretoFocus: Hosts the reading core knjiga, novine, clanak, citatelj, roman, izdanje and pisac, closing the theme block with a language-learning day.
drills: Name in Croatian one book, one newspaper and one article you have read recently.
Say two sentences about whether you read more on paper or on a screen.
Argue in one breath which format helps you finish a long text.

items: How does the relative pronoun koji take the preposition o in the feminine?
Which case follows the quantity word nekoliko?
How does a masculine noun like pisac change in the nominative versus the genitive?

activities.title: Knjige, novine i članci
activities.intro: Reading brings knjiga (book), novine (newspaper, plural only), članak (article), čitatelj (reader), roman (novel), izdanje (edition) and pisac (writer). Each line models an opinion about reading.

items.hr: Otkad čitam novine na hrvatskom, mnogo brže shvaćam vijesti.
items.en: Since I read the newspaper in Croatian, I grasp the news much faster.
items.note: novine = newspaper (plural only)

items.hr: Ovaj roman ima šesto stranica, ali tako je napet da ga čitam u dahu.
items.en: This novel has six hundred pages, but it's so gripping that I read it in one breath.
items.note: roman = novel; stranica = page

items.hr: Najviše volim članke u kojima novinar iznese jasan stav.
items.en: I most like articles in which the journalist states a clear position.
items.note: članak = article

items.hr: Novo izdanje knjige ima i rječnik na kraju, što početniku puno pomaže.
items.en: The new edition of the book also has a dictionary at the end, which helps a beginner a lot.
items.note: izdanje = edition; knjiga = book

items.hr: Dobar pisac zna reći mnogo s malo riječi, i po tome ga prepoznaš.
items.en: A good writer knows how to say a lot with few words, and that's how you recognize one.
items.note: pisac = writer

activities.title: Što i kako čitaš
activities.intro: Practise the language of books, papers and reading.

questions.prompt: Svaki dobar ___ zna da naslov mora privući pažnju.
questions.explanation: The subject 'every good writer' takes the nominative pisac; note how the c changes before the endings pisca, piscu, piscem.

questions.prompt: To je knjiga o ___ ti pričam već tjedan dana.
questions.explanation: A spiral to the relative pronoun: after the preposition o it stands in the locative feminine, o kojoj.

questions.prompt: Preskočio sam prvih nekoliko ___ jer su bile dosadne.
questions.explanation: The quantity word nekoliko takes the genitive plural, so stranica keeps that form.

questions.prompt: Postao je vjeran ___ ove rubrike i ne propušta nijedan broj.
questions.explanation: A loyal reader is a čitatelj, the nominative subject after postao je.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I read the newspaper every morning.'
questions.explanation: Novine is the accusative object, plural in form; 'svako jutro' gives the habitual time.

activities.title: Papir ili ekran?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend reads only on a phone; you defend paper for long texts, then find where you agree.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tko još čita novine na papiru? Sve piše na mobitelu, i to besplatno.
lines.en: Who still reads a paper newspaper? Everything's on the phone, and for free.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se da je praktičnije, ali na papiru pročitam cijeli članak, a na ekranu preletim tri retka.
lines.en: I agree it's more practical, but on paper I read the whole article, while on the screen I skim three lines.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je navika. I roman se danas može čitati na ekranu jednako dobro.
lines.en: That's habit. A novel can be read just as well on a screen today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Može, priznajem. No mene ekran stalno prekida porukama, pa teže pratim priču.
lines.en: It can, I admit. But the screen keeps interrupting me with messages, so I follow the story less easily.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ipak, izdanje na papiru zauzima pola sobe i skuplja prašinu.
lines.en: Still, a paper edition takes up half the room and collects dust.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu imaš pravo. Zato knjige biram pažljivo, a novine čitam na ekranu ujutro.
lines.en: There you're right. That's why I choose books carefully, but read the newspaper on the screen in the morning.

days.title: Slušanje: kratke obavijesti
days.objective: Predict the content of short public announcements before you hear them and catch the one key detail on the second play.
days.paretoFocus: Half of everyday listening is announcements at stations, shops and airports; if you predict the place and the number in advance, you understand them even at speed.
drills: Read each transcript aloud once for gist, then again hunting only for numbers.
For every notice, write the single most important detail in three words.
Cover the English and retell each announcement in your own Croatian.

items: Recall the predict-then-confirm habit: guess topic, then listen for one detail.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say aloud two announcements you might hear at a train station.

activities.title: Obavijesti u prijevozu i trgovini
activities.intro: In the app these listening texts are written out, but treat each as something you hear twice. First read for the gist: what place, what situation. Then read again for the single detail that matters, usually a time, a number or a description.

items.hr: Poštovani putnici, vlak za Rijeku kasni deset minuta.
items.en: Dear passengers, the train to Rijeka is ten minutes late.

items.hr: Trgovina se zatvara u devet sati, molimo vas da priđete blagajni.
items.en: The shop closes at nine, please come to the till.

items.hr: Sutra se očekuje kiša i jak vjetar na moru.
items.en: Rain and strong wind are expected tomorrow on the coast.

items.hr: Molimo roditelje dječaka u plavoj majici da dođu do informacija.
items.en: We ask the parents of the boy in the blue shirt to come to the information desk.

items.hr: Zbog radova autobus broj sedam danas ne staje na glavnom trgu.
items.en: Because of roadworks, bus number seven does not stop at the main square today.

activities.title: Vježba: hvatanje detalja
activities.intro: Confirm the strategy, then pin down the exact Croatian detail each notice carries.

questions.prompt: Before a station announcement plays, which two details are most worth predicting?
options: A person's full name and home address
A film title and its director
questions.explanation: Transport notices almost always pair a destination with a time or platform number, so prime your ear for both.

questions.prompt: Complete the delay notice, the verb for 'is running late': Vlak za Rijeku ___ deset minuta.
questions.explanation: Kasniti in the third person singular is kasni; the whole notice hangs on this one verb.

questions.prompt: Complete the change notice, the verb for 'stops': Zbog radova autobus danas ne ___ na glavnom trgu.
questions.explanation: Stajati here means to stop (at a stop); third person singular is staje.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'The shop closes at nine.'
questions.explanation: Zatvarati se is reflexive here; the time phrase u devet sati stays together at the end.

questions.prompt: Spiral review: which form correctly completes the location phrase 'na glavnom ___' (square)?
questions.explanation: After na for a static location Croatian uses the locative; glavni trg becomes na glavnom trgu.

activities.title: Na kolodvoru
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You did not catch an announcement and ask a fellow passenger to confirm the detail.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jeste li čuli obavijest? Nešto su rekli o našem vlaku.
lines.en: Did you hear the announcement? They said something about our train.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nisam sve razumio. Kasni li vlak za Rijeku?
lines.en: I didn't catch it all. Is the train to Rijeka late?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da, kasni deset minuta zbog radova na pruzi.
lines.en: Yes, it's ten minutes late because of works on the line.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znate li s kojeg perona onda kreće?
lines.en: Do you know which platform it leaves from then?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: S drugoga perona, tako su barem rekli.
lines.en: From platform two, at least that's what they said.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala, sad mi je jasno. Onda imamo još malo vremena.
lines.en: Thanks, now it's clear to me. So we still have a little time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Imamo, možemo popiti kavu dok čekamo.
lines.en: We do, we can have a coffee while we wait.

days.title: Slušanje: točno ili netočno
days.objective: Judge true/false statements about a longer spoken text by spotting 'almost true' traps, a changed number, a negation or a swapped detail.
days.paretoFocus: The commonest listening trap is a statement that is ninety percent right; learning to feel the one word that flips it from true to false is what raises your score.
drills: Read the monologue, then judge each statement true or false and underline the deciding word.
Rewrite two false statements so they become true.
Invent one 'almost true' trap of your own about the text.

items: Recall the three trap types: a wrong number, a hidden negation, a swapped detail.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Explain in Croatian why a statement can be false because of a single word.

activities.title: Monolog i zamke
activities.intro: Here is a short spoken text followed by statements. A statement is false if even one detail contradicts the text. Watch three traps: a number that has been changed, a negation slipped in or removed, and a detail moved to the wrong person or place.

items.hr: Zovem se Marina i već pet godina radim u knjižnici u središtu grada.
items.en: My name is Marina and I have worked in a library in the city centre for five years.

items.hr: Posao volim, ali mi je najteže rano ustajati zimi.
items.en: I love the job, but the hardest thing for me is getting up early in winter.

items.hr: Na posao ne idem autom, nego uvijek biciklom.
items.en: I don't go to work by car, but always by bike.

items.hr: Vikendom ne radim, pa tada najviše čitam i šetam uz more.
items.en: I don't work at weekends, so that's when I read most and walk by the sea.

items.hr: Trap example: 'Marina radi autom' is false, because she says biciklom, never autom.
items.en: The negation ne idem autom, nego biciklom is exactly what flips the statement.

activities.title: Vježba: prepoznaj zamku
activities.intro: Decide what makes each statement match or contradict the text.

questions.prompt: The text says 'Na posao ne idem autom, nego biciklom.' Which statement is FALSE?
questions.explanation: The negation ne idem autom flips it; the trap statement drops the negation and is therefore false.

questions.prompt: From the text, complete how long she has worked there, the number word: Marina radi u knjižnici već ___ godina.
questions.explanation: The text says pet godina; a trap version would swap the number, so this detail must be caught exactly.

questions.prompt: Complete what she finds hardest, one word for the season: Najteže joj je rano ustajati ___.
questions.explanation: Zima in the temporal sense is zimi (in winter); moving it to ljeti would make a false statement.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'She doesn't work at weekends.'
questions.explanation: The time word vikendom opens the sentence and ne sits right before the verb radi.

questions.prompt: Spiral review: pick the correctly aspected verb for a repeated weekend habit: 'Vikendom ona ___ uz more.'
questions.explanation: A regular, repeated habit takes the imperfective present šeta; the perfective prošeta would mean one completed walk.

activities.title: Provjera razumijevanja
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You compare notes with a classmate after a listening task and correct a trap.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja sam napisala da Marina ide na posao autom.
lines.en: I wrote that Marina goes to work by car.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To nije točno, rekla je da ne ide autom, nego biciklom.
lines.en: That's not right, she said she doesn't go by car, but by bike.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Aha, promaknula mi je ona riječ ne.
lines.en: Ah, I missed that word not.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Upravo tako, jedna riječ mijenja cijelu rečenicu.
lines.en: Exactly, one word changes the whole sentence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A koliko godina radi u knjižnici, tri ili pet?
lines.en: And how many years has she worked in the library, three or five?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pet, ne tri. Uvijek dvaput provjerim brojeve.
lines.en: Five, not three. I always double-check the numbers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar savjet, brojevi su najčešća zamka.
lines.en: Good advice, numbers are the commonest trap.

days.title: Slušanje: tko što kaže
days.objective: Follow a conversation between two speakers and keep straight who holds which opinion.
days.paretoFocus: Real conversations do not label their speakers; being able to answer 'who thinks the flat is too expensive?' is a distinct skill the exam tests directly.
drills: Read the dialogue and label each opinion with the speaker who holds it.
List one point on which the two speakers disagree and one on which they agree.
Retell the conversation as a short report: 'He thinks..., but she thinks...'.

items: Recall the tactic: attach every opinion to a name as you listen.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say who, in the text, wanted to rent the flat and who was against it.

activities.title: Dva govornika, dva mišljenja
activities.intro: When two people speak, track each opinion to its owner. Note the phrases that introduce a view (mislim da, po meni, ne slažem se) and mark whether the next speaker agrees or objects. Ask yourself after each turn: whose position is this now.

items.hr: Ivan: Ovaj stan mi je predobar da bismo ga propustili.
items.en: Ivan: This flat is too good for us to miss.

items.hr: Petra: Meni je preskup, najam je previsok za nas.
items.en: Petra: To me it's too expensive, the rent is too high for us.

items.hr: Ivan: Znam, ali je blizu posla i to štedi vrijeme.
items.en: Ivan: I know, but it's near work and that saves time.

items.hr: Petra: Slažem se da je lokacija odlična, no ipak sam oprezna.
items.en: Petra: I agree the location is excellent, but I'm still cautious.

items.hr: Whose worry is the price? Petra's. Who values the location? Both, but it persuades only Ivan.
items.en: Keep each stance tied to its speaker so a later question cannot confuse you.

activities.title: Vježba: pripiši mišljenje
activities.intro: Match each view to the right speaker and the phrase that reveals it.

questions.prompt: In the text, who considers the flat too expensive?
questions.explanation: Petra says meni je preskup; Ivan never calls it expensive, so the worry about price is hers.

questions.prompt: Which phrase signals that a speaker is about to disagree while still conceding a point?
questions.explanation: Conceding then objecting, slažem se da lokacija je odlična, no, is the classic partial-disagreement move.

questions.prompt: Complete Ivan's argument for the flat, the adjective for 'near': Stan je ___ posla, pa štedimo vrijeme.
questions.explanation: Blizu takes the genitive, blizu posla, and is Ivan's main reason in favour.

questions.prompt: Complete Petra's objection, one word meaning 'too expensive': Meni je stan ___.
questions.explanation: The prefix pre- means too, so preskup is 'too expensive', Petra's key word.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I don't agree with you.'
questions.explanation: Slagati se s + instrumental; the clitic se follows the verb and s tobom closes the phrase.

activities.title: Tko je bio za, a tko protiv
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks you to report who thought what in a conversation you both heard.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nisam pratila do kraja, tko je bio za stan?
lines.en: I didn't follow to the end, who was for the flat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ivan je bio za, njemu je lokacija najvažnija.
lines.en: Ivan was for it, the location matters most to him.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A Petra? Činilo mi se da je nešto prigovarala.
lines.en: And Petra? It seemed to me she was objecting to something.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Petri je najam bio previsok, zato je bila oprezna.
lines.en: For Petra the rent was too high, that's why she was cautious.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znači, slagali su se samo oko lokacije?
lines.en: So they only agreed about the location?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Točno, oko cijene se nisu složili.
lines.en: Exactly, about the price they didn't agree.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sad mi je jasno tko je što mislio.
lines.en: Now it's clear to me who thought what.

days.title: Čitanje: životopis i pitanja
days.objective: Answer open questions on a short biography quickly by scanning for dates, places and roles.
days.paretoFocus: Biography texts are a fixed reading genre; once you know they run birth, studies, work, achievement, you find each answer without reading every word.
drills: Scan the text and mark every date, place name and job in under a minute.
Answer each question in the shortest true phrase, not a full sentence.
Turn the text into a five-point timeline from memory.

items: Recall the biography skeleton: born, studied, worked, achieved, later life.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say aloud in Croatian one date using the genitive, such as a birth year.

activities.title: Kako čitati životopis
activities.intro: A biography follows a predictable order. Read the questions first, then scan the text for the matching signal: a year for 'when', a city for 'where', a noun of profession for 'what did she do'. Answer with a short phrase and move on.

items.hr: Ana Kovač rođena je tisuću devetsto osamdesete godine u Splitu.
items.en: Ana Kovac was born in nineteen eighty in Split.

items.hr: Studirala je biologiju u Zagrebu i diplomirala s odličnim uspjehom.
items.en: She studied biology in Zagreb and graduated with excellent results.

items.hr: Nakon studija radila je kao istraživačica u jednom institutu.
items.en: After her studies she worked as a researcher at an institute.

items.hr: Godine dvije tisuće petnaeste objavila je zapaženu knjigu o moru.
items.en: In two thousand and fifteen she published a notable book about the sea.

items.hr: Danas živi i predaje na obali, blizu rodnoga grada.
items.en: Today she lives and teaches on the coast, near her home town.

activities.title: Vježba: pronađi odgovor
activities.intro: Practise finding the exact fact each question asks for.

questions.prompt: The question is 'Gdje je Ana studirala?' Which scanning target finds the answer fastest?
options: A city name after the verb studirati
questions.answer: A city name after the verb studirati
questions.explanation: Where questions are answered by a place, so scan for the city tied to studirala je: u Zagrebu.

questions.prompt: Answer in one word, the profession she had after her studies: Radila je kao ___.
questions.explanation: The text names her role as istraživačica; the feminine form matches Ana.

questions.prompt: Answer with the city, where was Ana born: Rođena je u ___.
questions.explanation: Born in questions want the place in the locative after u: Split becomes u Splitu.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'She published a book about the sea.'
questions.explanation: Objaviti in the past with je; the theme phrase o moru uses the locative after o.

questions.prompt: Spiral review: which is the correct genitive year phrase for 'in 1980'?
questions.explanation: Croatian dates the year in the genitive: osamdesete godine, only the last element is ordinal.

activities.title: Razgovor o životopisu
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your tutor checks whether you found the key facts in the biography.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li pronašao kada je Ana rođena?
lines.en: Did you find when Ana was born?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam, rođena je tisuću devetsto osamdesete u Splitu.
lines.en: I did, she was born in nineteen eighty in Split.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro. A što je studirala i gdje?
lines.en: Good. And what did she study and where?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Studirala je biologiju u Zagrebu.
lines.en: She studied biology in Zagreb.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Koje je godine objavila knjigu?
lines.en: In which year did she publish the book?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dvije tisuće petnaeste, knjigu o moru.
lines.en: In two thousand fifteen, a book about the sea.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično, sve si našao bez čitanja svake riječi.
lines.en: Excellent, you found it all without reading every word.

days.title: Čitanje: stav autora
days.objective: Identify an author's opinion in a short column by reading the signal words that separate fact from stance.
days.paretoFocus: Exam reading is not only 'what happened' but 'what does the writer think'; a handful of signal phrases (smatram, nažalost, srećom) hand you the answer.
drills: Underline every phrase in the text that reveals opinion rather than fact.
State the author's main position in one sentence beginning with 'Autor smatra da'.
Separate the text's facts from its opinions in two short lists.

items: Recall opinion signals: smatram, po mome mišljenju, nažalost, srećom, čini se da.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say one fact and one opinion about your own town in Croatian.

activities.title: Gdje se skriva mišljenje
activities.intro: To find the author's position, ignore the neutral facts and hunt for stance markers. Words like smatram, mislim da, nažalost and srećom flag an opinion; adjectives of judgement (važno, pogrešno, korisno) do the same. The author's main view is usually one clear claim the rest of the text supports.
