items.hr: Smatram da bi gradovi trebali imati više zelenih površina.
items.en: I believe that cities should have more green spaces.

items.hr: Nažalost, mnogi parkovi danas nestaju zbog novih zgrada.
items.en: Unfortunately, many parks today are disappearing because of new buildings.

items.hr: Činjenica je da zelenilo čisti zrak, to nije stvar mišljenja.
items.en: It's a fact that greenery cleans the air, that is not a matter of opinion.

items.hr: Po mome mišljenju, svaka nova četvrt treba imati park.
items.en: In my opinion, every new district should have a park.

items.hr: Srećom, sve više ljudi traži takve promjene.
items.en: Fortunately, more and more people are asking for such changes.

activities.title: Vježba: činjenica ili stav
activities.intro: Tell the author's opinions apart from the neutral facts.

questions.prompt: Which sentence states the author's OPINION rather than a fact?
options: Smatram da bi gradovi trebali imati više zelenih površina.
Mnogi parkovi nestaju zbog novih zgrada.
U gradu postoji jedan veliki park.
questions.answer: Smatram da bi gradovi trebali imati više zelenih površina.
questions.explanation: Smatram da signals a personal stance; the others report facts the author does not claim as mere opinion.

questions.prompt: Which word tells you the author regrets what he reports?
questions.explanation: Nazalost (unfortunately) colours the statement with regret, marking the author's negative stance.

questions.prompt: Complete the stance opener, two words meaning 'in my opinion': ___ ___ mišljenju, svaka četvrt treba park.
questions.explanation: The fixed phrase po mome mišljenju introduces a personal view; it must be caught to read stance.

questions.prompt: Complete the verb of belief the author uses: ___ da bi gradovi trebali imati više parkova.
questions.explanation: Smatrati da is a core opinion verb, 'I hold that'; it flags the sentence as a position.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Fortunately, more and more people want change.'
questions.explanation: Srecom opens with the author's positive stance; sve više + genitive plural ljudi is 'more and more people'.

activities.title: Slažeš li se s autorom
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You discuss the column and say whether you share the author's view.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kakav je stav autora prema zelenim površinama?
lines.en: What is the author's position on green spaces?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Smatra da svaki novi dio grada treba imati park.
lines.en: He believes every new part of the city should have a park.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A po čemu vidiš da je to njegovo mišljenje, a ne činjenica?
lines.en: And how do you see that this is his opinion, not a fact?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Po riječima smatram i po mome mišljenju, to su znakovi stava.
lines.en: By the words I believe and in my opinion, those are stance markers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažeš li se ti s njim?
lines.en: Do you agree with him?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uglavnom da, ali mislim da je i promet velik problem.
lines.en: Mostly yes, but I think traffic is a big problem too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro zapažanje, i to bi vrijedilo spomenuti.
lines.en: Good observation, that would be worth mentioning too.

days.title: Čitanje: popuni prazninu
days.objective: Fill gaps in a text from a word bank by first deciding the case and number each slot needs.
days.paretoFocus: Gap-fill rewards grammar, not guessing; if you read the whole sentence and ask which case the slot wants, you place and inflect the bank word correctly.
drills: For each gap, name the case it needs before you choose a word.
Fill the whole text, then reread it once to hear whether every ending fits.
List which preposition in the text forced which case.

items: Recall the routine: read the sentence, decide the case, then pick and inflect.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name the case each of these takes: bez, prema, s, o.

activities.title: Strategija za praznine
activities.intro: Never fill a gap on meaning alone. Read to the end of the sentence, spot the trigger (a preposition, a number, a verb that governs a case), then choose the bank word and put it in the right form. A slot after bez wants the genitive; after prema, the dative; after s, the instrumental.

items.hr: Ne mogu zamisliti jutro bez ___ (kava). Answer: kave, genitive after bez.
items.en: I can't imagine a morning without coffee. Kave is the genitive after bez.

items.hr: Idemo prema ___ (centar). Answer: centru, dative after prema.
items.en: We're heading towards the centre. Centru is the dative after prema.

items.hr: Razgovarala je s ___ (susjeda). Answer: susjedom, instrumental after s.
items.en: She talked with the neighbour. Susjedom is the instrumental after s.

items.hr: Kupili smo pet ___ (jabuka). Answer: jabuka, genitive plural after five.
items.en: We bought five apples. Jabuka is the genitive plural after five.

items.hr: Knjiga je na ___ (stol). Answer: stolu, locative after na for place.
items.en: The book is on the table. Stolu is the locative after na for a place.

activities.title: Vježba: pravi oblik
activities.intro: Choose or build the exact form each slot demands.

questions.prompt: Which trigger decides the case in 'Idemo prema ___ '?
options: The preposition prema, which takes the dative
questions.answer: The preposition prema, which takes the dative
questions.explanation: Prema governs the dative, so the slot must be dative regardless of the noun's meaning.

questions.prompt: Put the noun in the right form after bez: Ne mogu zamisliti jutro bez ___ . (kava)
questions.explanation: Bez takes the genitive; kava becomes kave.

questions.prompt: Put the noun in the right form after s: Razgovarala je s ___ o poslu. (susjeda)
questions.explanation: S in the sense of 'with' takes the instrumental; susjeda becomes susjedom.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'We bought five apples.'
questions.explanation: Numbers five and up take the genitive plural, so jabuka, not jabuke.

questions.prompt: Spiral review: which case does na take for a static location, as in 'na ___' (table)?
questions.explanation: For where something is (not motion to), na takes the locative: na stolu.

activities.title: Provjera zadatka
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a partner compare answers on a gap-fill and justify the cases.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što si stavio u prvu prazninu, bez čega ne možeš zamisliti jutro?
lines.en: What did you put in the first gap, without what can't you imagine a morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bez kave, jer bez traži genitiv.
lines.en: Without coffee, because bez wants the genitive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A poslije prema, koji oblik ide?
lines.en: And after prema, which form goes there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dativ, dakle prema centru.
lines.en: The dative, so towards the centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja sam se zabunila kod broja pet.
lines.en: I got confused at the number five.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Poslije pet ide genitiv množine, pet jabuka.
lines.en: After five comes the genitive plural, five apples.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sad razumijem, oblik ovisi o okidaču, ne o značenju.
lines.en: Now I understand, the form depends on the trigger, not the meaning.

days.title: Struktura: izbor oblika
days.objective: Choose correct grammatical forms at speed by eliminating options that break a rule you already know.
days.paretoFocus: The grammar section is won by recognition, not deliberation; a quick elimination habit clears the easy points fast and leaves time for the hard ones.
drills: For each item, cross out two options in five seconds using one clear rule.
Note which rule decided each answer: agreement, case after a preposition, or spelling.
Redo the set from memory, aiming for both speed and accuracy.

items: Recall the elimination order: check case triggers, then agreement, then spelling.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
State the rule for ć versus č in the futur ending, from memory.

activities.title: Brzo prepoznavanje oblika
activities.intro: Under time, do not translate; recognise. First eliminate any option in the wrong case after a preposition. Then drop options that fail agreement with the noun. What remains is usually the answer, and a final spelling check (bismo not bi smo, doći ću written apart) confirms it.

items.hr: Bez dobre ___ nema uspjeha. (priprema) Answer: pripreme, genitive after bez.
items.en: Without good preparation there is no success. Pripreme is the genitive after bez.

items.hr: Sutra ću ti ___ sve. (objasniti) Answer: objasniti, infinitive with futur ću.
items.en: Tomorrow I'll explain everything to you. Objasniti stays infinitive with the futur ću.

items.hr: Kad bismo imali vremena, ___ do mora. (otići) Answer: otišli bismo.
items.en: If we had time, we'd go to the sea. The conditional is otisli bismo.

items.hr: Vidio sam dvije nove ___ . (zgrada) Answer: zgrade, after two.
items.en: I saw two new buildings. After two the count form is zgrade.

items.hr: Radujem se ___ . (ljeto) Answer: ljetu, dative after radovati se.
items.en: I'm looking forward to summer. Ljetu is the dative after radovati se.

activities.title: Vježba: izaberi oblik
activities.intro: Apply the elimination habit to each form choice.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct conditional: 'Kad bismo imali vremena, ___ do mora.'
questions.explanation: A kad bismo clause pairs with the conditional otisli bismo; bismo is one word and the l-participle agrees.

questions.prompt: Choose the count form after 'dvije': 'Vidio sam dvije nove ___ .'
questions.explanation: Two, three and four take the special count form; feminine zgrada becomes zgrade.

questions.prompt: Put the noun in the right case after bez: Bez dobre ___ nema uspjeha. (priprema)
questions.explanation: Bez takes the genitive; priprema becomes pripreme.

questions.prompt: Keep the verb in the form the futur ću needs: Sutra ću ti ___ sve. (objasniti)
questions.explanation: After the clitic ću the main verb stays in the infinitive: objasniti.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I'm looking forward to summer.'
questions.explanation: Radovati se governs the dative, so ljeto becomes ljetu.

activities.title: Uvježbavamo pod satom
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner quizzes you on quick form choices before the exam.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Brzo, koji je kondicional: kad bismo imali vremena, ___ ?
lines.en: Quick, what's the conditional: if we had time, we would...?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Otišli bismo, i bismo je jedna riječ.
lines.en: We would go, and bismo is one word.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro. A poslije dvije, zgrade ili zgrada?
lines.en: Good. And after two, zgrade or zgrada?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zgrade, jer dvije traži poseban brojevni oblik.
lines.en: Zgrade, because two takes the special count form.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zadnje, kako pišeš futur od objasniti s ću?
lines.en: Last, how do you write the future of explain with ću?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ću objasniti, glavni glagol ostaje u infinitivu.
lines.en: Ću objasniti, the main verb stays in the infinitive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično, ideš brzo i točno.
lines.en: Excellent, you're going fast and accurately.

days.title: Struktura: vid i prefiks
days.objective: Pick the right aspect and prefixed verb in context by asking whether the action is a process or a result.
days.paretoFocus: Aspect is the hardest grammar point for learners and a whole exam block; a single question, process or result, resolves most choices.
drills: For each sentence, decide process or result before choosing the verb.
Match five prefixes to their meaning: po-, pro-, na-, do-, iz-.
Rewrite three imperfective sentences as completed, perfective ones.

items: Recall the test question: is the action ongoing or finished at that moment.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give an imperfective and perfective pair for 'to read' and use each in a sentence.

activities.title: Proces ili rezultat
activities.intro: To choose aspect, ask what the sentence pictures. An ongoing or repeated action wants the imperfective (čitati, pisati); a single completed result wants the perfective (pročitati, napisati). Prefixes add meaning and usually make a verb perfective: pro- often means all the way through, do- means up to the end.

items.hr: Cijelo jutro sam čitao, ali nisam pročitao knjigu.
items.en: I read all morning, but I didn't finish the book.

items.hr: Dok sam pisao pismo, zazvonio je telefon.
items.en: While I was writing the letter, the phone rang.

items.hr: Napokon sam napisao sve poruke.
items.en: I finally wrote all the messages.

items.hr: Svaki dan pišem u dnevnik po pola sata.
items.en: Every day I write in my diary for half an hour.

items.hr: Kad pročitaš članak, javi mi mišljenje.
items.en: When you've finished reading the article, tell me your view.

activities.title: Vježba: izaberi vid
activities.intro: Let process or result decide each choice.

questions.prompt: Which verb fits a finished result: 'Napokon sam ___ sve poruke.'
questions.explanation: Napokon points to completion, so the perfective napisao is right; pisao would leave the action open.

questions.prompt: Which verb fits a repeated daily habit: 'Svaki dan ___ u dnevnik.'
questions.explanation: A regular habit takes the imperfective present pišem; the perfective would mean one single completed writing.

questions.prompt: Choose the aspect that shows an unfinished action: Cijelo jutro sam ___ , ali nisam pročitao knjigu. (read)
questions.explanation: Cijelo jutro plus nisam procitao frames an ongoing process, so the imperfective citao fits.

questions.prompt: Complete with the perfective for a completed reading: Kad ___ članak, javi mi mišljenje. (you read)
questions.explanation: The when-you-have-done-it meaning needs the perfective procitas.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'The phone rang while I was writing.'
questions.explanation: The background pisao is imperfective; the single interrupting event zazvonio is perfective.

activities.title: Objasni razliku
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner mixes up two aspect forms and you explain the difference.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Rekao bih napisao sam knjigu jutros, je li dobro?
lines.en: I'd say I wrote a book this morning, is that right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ovisi, jesi li je završio ili si samo pisao?
lines.en: It depends, did you finish it or were you just writing?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nisam je završio, pisao sam samo jedno poglavlje.
lines.en: I didn't finish it, I was only writing one chapter.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Onda kaži pisao sam, jer radnja nije dovršena.
lines.en: Then say I was writing, because the action isn't completed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A kad kažem pročitao sam, to znači do kraja?
lines.en: And when I say I read it, that means to the end?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tako je, pročitao znači rezultat, cijelu knjigu.
lines.en: That's right, procitao means the result, the whole book.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sad mi je vid puno jasniji.
lines.en: Now aspect is much clearer to me.

days.title: Pisanje: opis slike
days.objective: Write a clear seven-sentence picture description using a fixed structure and a self-check rubric.
days.paretoFocus: The picture task is scored on structure and control, not imagination; a reliable order plus a checklist earns the mark every time.
drills: Describe a room or street you can see in seven Croatian sentences following the order taught.
Run your text through the four-point rubric and fix each miss.
Rewrite one flat sentence to add a detail of place or mood.

items: Recall the order: general scene, place, people, actions, detail, mood, closing thought.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
List four rubric checks: case endings, aspect, agreement, no repetition.

activities.title: Struktura opisa
activities.intro: Describe a picture in a fixed order so nothing is forgotten. Open with the general scene (Na slici vidim), then say where it is, who is there, what they are doing, one telling detail, the mood, and a short closing thought. Vary your openers so sentences do not all start the same way.

items.hr: Na slici vidim mali trg u središtu grada.
items.en: In the picture I see a small square in the city centre.

items.hr: U pozadini se nalazi stara crkva, a ispred nje fontana.
items.en: In the background there is an old church, and in front of it a fountain.

items.hr: Nekoliko ljudi sjedi na klupama i razgovara.
items.en: Several people are sitting on benches and talking.

items.hr: Djevojčica hrani golubove dok se smije.
items.en: A little girl is feeding the pigeons while laughing.

items.hr: Cijeli prizor djeluje mirno i ugodno.
items.en: The whole scene feels calm and pleasant.

activities.title: Vježba: gradimo opis
activities.intro: Practise the openers, agreement and mood words the description needs.

questions.prompt: Which opener best begins a picture description?
questions.explanation: Na slici vidim names the scene and is the standard, safe opening for this task.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly places something in the background?
options: U pozadini se nalazi stara crkva.
U pozadinu se nalazi stara crkva.
U pozadini se nalaze stara crkva.
questions.answer: U pozadini se nalazi stara crkva.
questions.explanation: U pozadini is locative for position, and nalazi se agrees with the singular crkva.

questions.prompt: Add the mood word so the scene feels calm: Cijeli prizor djeluje ___ i ugodno. (calm)
questions.explanation: Djelovati takes an adverb of impression; mirno pairs naturally with ugodno.

questions.prompt: Complete the action of the people, one verb: Nekoliko ljudi sjedi i ___ na klupama. (are talking)
questions.explanation: Nekoliko ljudi is treated as singular, so the verb is razgovara.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'A little girl is feeding the pigeons.'
questions.explanation: Hraniti takes the accusative object golubove; the present tense shows the ongoing action.

activities.title: Provjeri moj opis
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your tutor checks your picture description against the rubric.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako počinješ opis slike?
lines.en: How do you begin the picture description?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rečenicom na slici vidim, pa kažem što je u pozadini.
lines.en: With the sentence in the picture I see, then I say what's in the background.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li provjerio slaganje, na primjer crkva se nalazi?
lines.en: Did you check agreement, for example the church is located?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam, jednina crkva traži nalazi se, ne nalaze se.
lines.en: I did, the singular church wants nalazi se, not nalaze se.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A jesi li završio kakvim dojmom?
lines.en: And did you end with some impression?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, napisao sam da prizor djeluje mirno i ugodno.
lines.en: Yes, I wrote that the scene feels calm and pleasant.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično, struktura ti je potpuna.
lines.en: Excellent, your structure is complete.

days.title: Pisanje: formalno pismo
days.objective: Write a short formal letter with the correct opening and closing formulas, polite register and dates in the genitive.
days.paretoFocus: Formal writing is judged mostly on register and layout; the fixed formulas and date forms are quick to learn and heavily rewarded.
drills: Write the opening and closing of a formal enquiry from memory.
Write today's date and your birth date in the Croatian genitive form.
Turn three casual sentences into polite, formal ones with vi.

items: Recall the frame: Poštovani, body with vi-forms, S poštovanjem, your name.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say the date of the first of May in Croatian, in the genitive.

activities.title: Ton i datumi u pismu
activities.intro: A formal letter opens with Poštovani or Poštovana and closes with S poštovanjem. Use the vi-form throughout and the conditional to soften requests (Molio bih vas). Write dates in the genitive: prvoga svibnja, desetoga lipnja. Keep sentences complete and avoid casual words.

items.hr: Poštovani, obraćam vam se zbog oglasa za stan.
items.en: Dear Sir or Madam, I am writing to you about the flat advertisement.

items.hr: Molio bih vas da mi pošaljete više informacija.
items.en: I would kindly ask you to send me more information.

items.hr: Slobodan sam za razgovor od desetoga lipnja nadalje.
items.en: I am free for a meeting from the tenth of June onwards.

items.hr: Unaprijed vam zahvaljujem na odgovoru.
items.en: Thank you in advance for your reply.

items.hr: S poštovanjem, Ivan Horvat.
items.en: Yours faithfully, Ivan Horvat.

activities.title: Vježba: formalni izrazi
activities.intro: Choose the register and date forms a formal letter requires.

questions.prompt: Which opening is correct for a formal letter to someone you don't know?
questions.explanation: Postovani is the neutral formal salutation; the others are informal and wrong in this register.

questions.prompt: Which closing matches a formal letter?
questions.explanation: S postovanjem is the standard formal sign-off; the rest are friendly and too casual.

questions.prompt: Soften the request with the conditional of moliti: ___ bih vas da mi javite cijenu. (I would ask)
questions.explanation: The conditional molio bih vas makes the request polite and formal.

questions.prompt: Write the date in the genitive, the day: Slobodan sam od ___ lipnja. (the tenth)
questions.explanation: Croatian dates the day in the genitive ordinal: desetoga lipnja.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Thank you in advance for your reply.'
questions.explanation: Zahvaljivati takes the dative person vam and na plus the locative odgovoru.

activities.title: Pišemo prijavu
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You ask a tutor to check the register of your formal letter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako si oslovio primatelja na početku?
lines.en: How did you address the recipient at the start?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Napisao sam Poštovani, jer ne poznajem osobu.
lines.en: I wrote Dear Sir or Madam, because I don't know the person.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro. Jesi li koristio vi kroz cijelo pismo?
lines.en: Good. Did you use the vi-form throughout the letter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam, i molbu sam ublažio s molio bih vas.
lines.en: I did, and I softened the request with I would kindly ask you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A datum, kako si ga napisao?
lines.en: And the date, how did you write it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: U genitivu, od desetoga lipnja nadalje.
lines.en: In the genitive, from the tenth of June onwards.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sve je u redu, ton je pristojan i jasan.
lines.en: Everything is fine, the tone is polite and clear.

days.title: Govorenje: izlaganje
days.objective: Structure a two-minute spoken presentation with an introduction, a developed middle with examples, and a conclusion.
days.paretoFocus: The oral exam rewards a talk that is organised, not merely fluent; a simple three-part frame with linking words makes even a short answer sound confident.
drills: Give a two-minute talk on a familiar topic using uvod, razrada, zaključak.
Insert three linking words to move between your points.
End with one clear opinion sentence that sums up your talk.

items: Recall the frame: introduce the topic, develop with examples, conclude with a view.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
List three connectors: kao prvo, na primjer, zaključno.

activities.title: Kako izložiti temu
activities.intro: A good spoken presentation has three parts. Open by naming the topic and your plan (Danas bih govorio o). Develop it with two points, each backed by an example and joined by connectors (kao prvo, osim toga, na primjer). Close with a clear conclusion (zaključno, mislim da). Practise so the frame becomes automatic.

items.hr: Danas bih govorio o prednostima života u malom gradu.
items.en: Today I'd like to speak about the advantages of life in a small town.

items.hr: Kao prvo, sve je blizu i ljudi se poznaju.
items.en: First of all, everything is close and people know one another.

items.hr: Osim toga, zrak je čišći, na primjer manje je prometa.
items.en: Besides, the air is cleaner, for example there is less traffic.

items.hr: S druge strane, ima manje posla i sadržaja.
items.en: On the other hand, there is less work and fewer amenities.

items.hr: Zaključno, meni osobno mali grad više odgovara.
items.en: In conclusion, personally a small town suits me better.

activities.title: Vježba: veznici i struktura
activities.intro: Practise the connectors and framing sentences of a presentation.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best OPENS a presentation?
questions.explanation: This names the topic and signals a plan, the natural opener; the others belong later in the talk.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a contrasting point?
questions.explanation: S druge strane (on the other hand) flags a contrast; the others add or introduce examples.

questions.prompt: Add the connector that introduces an example, two words: Zrak je čišći, ___ ___ manje je prometa.
questions.explanation: Na primjer introduces the supporting example that develops a point.

questions.prompt: Complete the closing phrase, one word for 'in conclusion': ___, meni mali grad više odgovara.
questions.explanation: Zakljucno signals the conclusion and cues the listener that your view follows.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Today I'd like to speak about travel.'
questions.explanation: The conditional govorio bih softens the opener; o putovanju uses the locative after o.

activities.title: Uvježbavamo izlaganje
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your tutor coaches the structure of your short talk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O čemu ćeš govoriti i kako počinješ?
lines.en: What will you talk about and how do you begin?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Govorio bih o životu u malom gradu, počinjem s uvodom.
lines.en: I'd speak about life in a small town, I begin with an introduction.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako povezuješ drugu misao s prvom?
lines.en: How do you link the second thought to the first?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Riječima osim toga i na primjer za primjer.
lines.en: With the words besides and for example for an example.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A kako zatvaraš izlaganje?
lines.en: And how do you close the presentation?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zaključno kažem svoje mišljenje, jasno i kratko.
lines.en: In conclusion I state my opinion, clearly and briefly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Točno tako, struktura ti drži cijeli govor.
lines.en: Exactly, the structure holds your whole talk together.

days.title: Probni ispit i analiza
days.objective: Sit a complete timed practice exam, then log your errors by section and read your readiness against the sixty percent rule.
days.paretoFocus: One honest full rehearsal under the clock tells you more than ten easy drills; the error log turns each mistake into a specific thing to fix.
drills: Do all five sections in one sitting under strict time, no dictionary.
Sort every error into its section and label it grammar, vocabulary or strategy.
Pick the two sections closest to the pass line and plan one fix for each.

items: Recall the pass logic: each section must clear its bar; one weak section fails the whole.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name your weakest and strongest section after the mock, in Croatian.

activities.title: Kako iskoristiti probni ispit
activities.intro: Treat the mock like the real thing: fixed time, no help. Afterwards, do not just count the score, build an error log. For each mistake write the section, the correct answer and why you missed it. Group the errors and you will see whether your gap is grammar, vocabulary or exam technique.

items.hr: Na probnom ispitu najviše sam pogriješio u slušanju.
items.en: In the practice exam I made the most mistakes in listening.

items.hr: Vodim popis pogrešaka: rečenica, točan odgovor, razlog.
items.en: I keep an error log: the sentence, the correct answer, the reason.

items.hr: Većina mojih grešaka bila je zbog brzine, ne zbog gramatike.
items.en: Most of my errors were due to speed, not grammar.

items.hr: Svaki dio ispita mora prijeći svoj prag da bih prošao.
items.en: Each part of the exam must clear its threshold for me to pass.

items.hr: Zato vježbam upravo najslabiji dio, a ne najjači.
items.en: So I practise exactly the weakest part, not the strongest.

activities.title: Vježba: analiza rezultata
activities.intro: Practise the language of reviewing and planning after a mock.

questions.prompt: Why must you focus practice on your weakest section?
options: Because every section must separately pass, so one weak part can fail the whole exam
Because the strongest section is worth the most points
Because the weakest section is always listening
questions.answer: Because every section must separately pass, so one weak part can fail the whole exam
questions.explanation: The pass rule is per section; a single section below its bar sinks the whole result, so the weakest is the priority.

questions.prompt: What belongs in a useful error log entry?
options: The correct answer and the reason you missed it
questions.answer: The correct answer and the reason you missed it
questions.explanation: An entry you can act on records the right answer and the cause; a bare score teaches nothing.

questions.prompt: Complete the self-report, the section: Najviše sam pogriješio u ___ . (listening)
questions.explanation: Slusanje in the locative after u is slusanju, naming the listening section.

questions.prompt: Complete the reason for most errors, one word: Pogriješio sam zbog ___, ne zbog gramatike. (speed)
questions.explanation: Brzina in the genitive after zbog is brzine, naming speed as the cause.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Each section must pass.'
questions.explanation: The modal mora is followed by the infinitive proci, standard Croatian for must.

activities.title: Razgovor o spremnosti
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your tutor reviews your mock results and readiness with you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako je prošao probni ispit?
lines.en: How did the practice exam go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro, osim slušanja, ondje sam izgubio najviše bodova.
lines.en: Well, except listening, that's where I lost the most points.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znaš li zašto ti slušanje ide teže?
lines.en: Do you know why listening is harder for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zbog brzine, ne stignem zapisati sve detalje.
lines.en: Because of speed, I don't manage to note every detail.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onda idući tjedan vježbamo baš slušanje.
lines.en: Then next week we practise listening in particular.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se, moram prijeći prag u svakom dijelu.
lines.en: I agree, I have to clear the bar in every section.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Uz ovakav plan spreman si na vrijeme.
lines.en: With a plan like this you'll be ready in time.

days.title: Ponavljanje: sedam padeža u priči
days.objective: Use all seven cases naturally inside one connected story rather than in isolated tables.
days.paretoFocus: Cases only stick when they work together; telling a short story forces every case into its real job at once.
drills: Tell a two-minute story about moving house, using at least five different cases.
For each sentence, name which case each noun is in and why.
Retell the story changing the subject from 'I' to 'she'.

items: Recall the case questions: tko/što, koga/čega, komu, koga/što, o komu, s kim.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say one sentence that uses the genitive, the dative and the instrumental together.

activities.title: Padeži rade zajedno
activities.intro: In a real story every noun lands in the case its role demands. Here the same event pulls in the nominative subject, the accusative object, the genitive of absence, the dative goal, the locative place and the instrumental means. Read for the job each ending is doing.

items.hr: Prošli tjedan preselili smo se u novi stan.
items.en: Last week we moved into a new flat.

items.hr: Prijatelji su nam pomogli nositi kutije.
items.en: Friends helped us carry the boxes.

items.hr: Bez njihove pomoći ne bismo uspjeli.
items.en: Without their help we wouldn't have managed.

items.hr: Namještaj smo prevozili kombijem cijeli dan.
items.en: We transported the furniture by van all day.

items.hr: Navečer smo razgovarali o novim susjedima uz kavu.
items.en: In the evening we talked about the new neighbours over coffee.

activities.title: Vježba: koji padež
activities.intro: Name and build the case each role needs.

questions.prompt: Which case does 'bez' force in 'Bez njihove ___ ne bismo uspjeli.'?
questions.explanation: Bez always takes the genitive; pomoc becomes pomoci to mark absence.

questions.prompt: Which case marks the means in 'Namještaj smo prevozili ___.' (by van)?
questions.explanation: The instrumental expresses the means of transport, so kombi becomes kombijem.

questions.prompt: Put the goal of motion in the right case: Preselili smo se u novi ___ . (flat)
questions.explanation: Motion into with u takes the accusative; masculine inanimate stan keeps its base form.

questions.prompt: Put the topic in the right case after o: Razgovarali smo o novim ___ . (neighbours)
questions.explanation: O takes the locative; the plural susjedi becomes susjedima.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Friends helped us.'
questions.explanation: Pomoci governs the dative, so the helped party is the clitic nam.

activities.title: Selidba
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks how your move went and you narrate it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Čula sam da si se preselio, kako je prošlo?
lines.en: I heard you moved, how did it go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Naporno, ali prijatelji su nam pomogli s kutijama.
lines.en: Tiring, but friends helped us with the boxes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jeste li imali kombi ili ste nosili sve rukama?
lines.en: Did you have a van or did you carry everything by hand?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Imali smo kombi, bez njega ne bismo stigli.
lines.en: We had a van, without it we wouldn't have managed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A kakvi su novi susjedi?
lines.en: And what are the new neighbours like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dragi su, već smo razgovarali o kvartu uz kavu.
lines.en: They're nice, we already talked about the neighbourhood over coffee.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Baš mi je drago, čestitam na novom stanu.
lines.en: I'm really glad, congratulations on the new flat.

days.title: Ponavljanje: vid u pripovijedanju
days.objective: Weave imperfective background and perfective events together to narrate a past episode.
days.paretoFocus: Storytelling in Croatian lives on the aspect contrast; the background sets the scene while completed verbs push the plot forward.
drills: Narrate an evening out, using imperfectives for the setting and perfectives for events.
Underline every perfective verb and check each marks a single completed action.
Retell the same story in the present tense, keeping the aspect.

items: Recall the pattern: imperfective sets the scene, perfective moves the story.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give the aspect pair for 'to arrive' and use each form once.

activities.title: Pozadina i događaj
activities.intro: In narration the imperfective paints what was going on (padala je kiša, svirala je glazba) and the perfective reports what then happened (ušla je, zazvonio je telefon). Mixing them well is what makes a story sound native rather than a list.

items.hr: Sinoć smo sjedili u kafiću kad je počela svirati glazba.
items.en: Last night we were sitting in a cafe when the music started.

items.hr: Dok smo razgovarali, ušla je stara prijateljica.
items.en: While we were talking, an old friend came in.

items.hr: Nismo je vidjeli godinama, pa smo se jako obradovali.
items.en: We hadn't seen her for years, so we were really pleased.

items.hr: Ostali smo do kasno i sve smo ispričali jedni drugima.
items.en: We stayed late and told each other everything.

items.hr: Na kraju smo se dogovorili da se češće viđamo.
items.en: In the end we agreed to see each other more often.

activities.title: Vježba: vid u priči
activities.intro: Choose the aspect the storyline needs at each point.

questions.prompt: Which verb sets the ongoing background: 'Sinoć smo ___ u kafiću kad je počela glazba.'
questions.explanation: The background state uses the imperfective sjedili; the perfective would mark a single sitting-down.

questions.prompt: Which verb reports the single event: 'Dok smo razgovarali, ___ prijateljica.'
questions.explanation: The one completed interrupting event takes the perfective usla je.

questions.prompt: Choose the aspect for a repeated future habit: Dogovorili smo se da se češće ___ . (see each other)
questions.explanation: A repeated arrangement takes the imperfective vidamo, not a one-time perfective.

questions.prompt: Complete the perfective of the plot event: Na kraju smo se ___ da idemo dalje. (agreed)
questions.explanation: A single completed decision uses the perfective dogovorili se.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'The phone rang while we were talking.'
questions.explanation: The frame razgovarali is imperfective; the sudden zazvonio is perfective.

activities.title: Priča o sinoć
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You tell a friend about running into an old acquaintance last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako je bilo sinoć u gradu?
lines.en: How was last night out?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Super, sjedili smo u kafiću kad je ušla stara prijateljica.
lines.en: Great, we were sitting in a cafe when an old friend walked in.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma nemoj, koliko je dugo niste vidjeli?
lines.en: No way, how long hadn't you seen her?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Godinama, pa smo se svi jako obradovali.
lines.en: For years, so we were all really pleased.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I, jeste li dugo ostali?
lines.en: And, did you stay long?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ostali smo do kasno i sve ispričali.
lines.en: We stayed late and told each other everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Baš lijepo kad se stari prijatelji opet nađu.
lines.en: It's lovely when old friends meet again.

days.title: Ponavljanje: enklitike i red riječi
days.objective: Place the short unstressed words in their correct second-position order inside real sentences.
days.paretoFocus: Clitic order is the single feature that most marks a sentence as native or not; getting je, se, mu, ga in the right slot is worth constant review.
drills: Rewrite five sentences moving the clitic cluster to the right second position.
Order a cluster with three clitics: an auxiliary, a dative and an accusative.
Say three sentences that begin with a conjunction and keep clitics in place.

items: Recall the clitic order: li, auxiliary, se, dative, accusative, je last.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Place the clitics correctly in 'I gave it to him', in Croatian.

activities.title: Gdje idu kratke riječi
activities.intro: Unstressed clitics gather in second position in a fixed order: the question li, then the auxiliary (sam, si, će), then se, then a dative pronoun, then an accusative, and je comes last of all. After an opening conjunction the cluster still sits right after the first strong word.

items.hr: Vratio sam mu ga jutros.
items.en: I returned it to him this morning.

items.hr: Jesi li joj to već rekao?
items.en: Have you already told her that?

items.hr: Knjigu sam joj posudila prošli tjedan.
items.en: I lent her the book last week.

items.hr: Nadam se da će nam se javiti.
items.en: I hope he'll get in touch with us.

items.hr: Vidjela ga je na trgu i odmah prepoznala.
items.en: She saw him on the square and recognised him at once.

activities.title: Vježba: poredaj enklitike
activities.intro: Order the clitic clusters correctly.

questions.prompt: Which order is correct?
questions.explanation: The auxiliary sam precedes the dative mu, which precedes the accusative ga; the participle opens the sentence.

questions.prompt: Where does 'je' go in 'Vidjela ___ na trgu.'?
questions.explanation: The auxiliary je comes last in the cluster, after the accusative ga: vidjela ga je.

questions.prompt: Insert the question clitic in second position, one word: Jesi ___ joj to rekao?
questions.explanation: The interrogative li sits right after the first word, opening the clitic cluster.

questions.prompt: Place the reflexive and dative after the future clitic, three words: Nadam se da ___ ___ ___ javiti.
questions.explanation: The order is auxiliary ce, dative nam, then se, giving ce nam se javiti.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I lent her the book.'
questions.explanation: After the fronted object Knjigu the cluster runs sam then joj, then the participle.

activities.title: Tračevi uz kavu
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a friend swap news about a mutual acquaintance, using lots of clitics.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li ga vidjela ovaj tjedan?
lines.en: Have you seen him this week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam, sreli smo se slučajno na trgu.
lines.en: I have, we ran into each other on the square.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je li ti rekao što je bilo s poslom?
lines.en: Did he tell you what happened with the job?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rekao mi je da su ga primili, jako se veseli.
lines.en: He told me they took him on, he's really pleased.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Super, hoće li nam se pridružiti u subotu?
lines.en: Great, will he join us on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Javit će nam se do petka.
lines.en: He'll get in touch with us by Friday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično, onda ga očekujemo.
lines.en: Excellent, then we'll expect him.

days.title: Ponavljanje: prezentski razredi
days.objective: Conjugate the main present-tense verb classes correctly while talking about routine.
days.paretoFocus: The present tense underlies everything you say; keeping the tricky classes (pisati to pišem, moći to mogu) automatic frees your attention for meaning.
drills: Describe a typical work day using verbs from three different present classes.
Conjugate five awkward verbs through all persons: pisati, moći, htjeti, jesti, ići.
Turn your day description into a question form for a partner.

items: Recall the shifts: pisati to pišem, kazati to kažem, moći to mogu.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give the full present of htjeti from memory.

activities.title: Nepravilni prezent
activities.intro: Several common verbs change their stem in the present. Pisati becomes pišem, kazati becomes kažem, moći gives mogu and može, htjeti gives hoću and hoće. Reviewing these together stops the errors that creep in when you speak at speed.

items.hr: Svako jutro pišem popis obveza za taj dan.
items.en: Every morning I write a list of tasks for that day.

items.hr: Ne mogu raditi bez kave, to svi znaju.
items.en: I can't work without coffee, everyone knows that.

items.hr: Hoćeš li sa mnom na ručak u podne?
items.en: Do you want to come with me for lunch at noon?

items.hr: Kolegica često putuje pa je rijetko u uredu.
items.en: My colleague often travels, so she's rarely in the office.

items.hr: Poslije posla idem na tečaj hrvatskoga.
items.en: After work I go to a Croatian course.

activities.title: Vježba: oblici prezenta
activities.intro: Produce the right present-tense forms.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct present of pisati for 'I': 'Svako jutro ___ popis.'
questions.explanation: Pisati shifts s to š in the present: pisem, not the regular-looking pisam.

questions.prompt: Which is correct: 'Ne ___ raditi bez kave.'
questions.explanation: Moci has an irregular first person mogu; the third person is moze.

questions.prompt: Complete the present of htjeti for 'you': ___ li sa mnom na ručak? (do you want)
questions.explanation: Htjeti has the present hoces; the question uses the li clitic.

questions.prompt: Complete the present of putovati for 'she': Kolegica često ___ . (travels)
questions.explanation: Verbs in -ovati form the present in -ujem, so putovati gives putuje.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'After work I go to a course.'
questions.explanation: Ici has the present idem; poslije takes the genitive posla.

activities.title: Kakav ti je radni dan
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A new colleague asks about your daily routine at work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako izgleda tvoj tipičan radni dan?
lines.en: What does your typical work day look like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ujutro pišem popis zadataka i pijem kavu.
lines.en: In the morning I write a task list and drink coffee.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Radiš li i vikendom?
lines.en: Do you work at weekends too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne, vikendom ne radim, tada se odmaram.
lines.en: No, at weekends I don't work, then I rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hoćeš li danas na ručak s nama?
lines.en: Do you want to come to lunch with us today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hoću rado, ali moram prvo završiti izvještaj.
lines.en: I'd love to, but I have to finish a report first.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Naravno, čekamo te u podne.
lines.en: Of course, we'll wait for you at noon.

days.title: Ponavljanje: kondicional i pretpostavke
days.objective: Express hypotheses, polite wishes and unreal conditions with the conditional.
days.paretoFocus: The conditional carries politeness and 'what if' thinking, both essential at this level for opinions and plans.
drills: Say four things you would do if you had more free time.
Turn three direct requests into polite conditional ones.
Build two full if-clauses of the form 'Kad bih..., ...'.

items: Recall the conditional auxiliary: bih, bi, bi, bismo, biste, bi.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'I would like a coffee' politely with the conditional.

activities.title: Bih, bi, bismo
activities.intro: The conditional joins the auxiliary bih, bi, bismo with the l-participle: išao bih, htjeli bismo. It softens requests (Htio bih, Mogli biste), states wishes and builds unreal conditions with kad: Kad bih imao vremena, putovao bih.

items.hr: Kad bih imao više vremena, učio bih još jedan jezik.
items.en: If I had more time, I would learn another language.

items.hr: Htjela bih rezervirati stol za dvoje.
items.en: I would like to book a table for two.

items.hr: Biste li mi mogli pomoći oko prijave?
items.en: Could you help me with the application?

items.hr: Da imamo auto, otišli bismo na more svaki vikend.
items.en: If we had a car, we'd go to the sea every weekend.

items.hr: Na tvome mjestu ne bih se brinuo.
items.en: In your place I wouldn't worry.

activities.title: Vježba: gradimo kondicional
activities.intro: Form polite and hypothetical conditionals correctly.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct conditional for 'we': 'Da imamo auto, ___ na more.'
questions.explanation: The we-form auxiliary is bismo and the participle agrees: otisli bismo.

questions.prompt: Which softens a request most politely?
questions.explanation: The conditional question Biste li mogli is the polite formal request; the others are direct commands.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite wish with the conditional of htjeti, one word: ___ bih rezervirati stol. (I would like)
questions.explanation: Htio bih (or htjela bih) is the standard polite 'I would like'.

questions.prompt: Complete the unreal condition, the auxiliary: Kad ___ imao vremena, više bih čitao. (I would)
questions.explanation: The first person conditional auxiliary is bih; it pairs with the l-participle imao.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'In your place I wouldn't worry.'
questions.explanation: The negated conditional keeps ne before the auxiliary bih, then the reflexive se and participle.

activities.title: Da imam vremena
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a friend dream about what you'd do with more time and money.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što bi radio kad bi imao više slobodnog vremena?
lines.en: What would you do if you had more free time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Učio bih hrvatski svaki dan i više putovao.
lines.en: I'd study Croatian every day and travel more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A da imaš i više novca?
lines.en: And if you also had more money?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da imam novca, kupio bih mali stan uz more.
lines.en: If I had money, I'd buy a small flat by the sea.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lijep san, i ja bih rado živjela na obali.
lines.en: A nice dream, I'd gladly live on the coast too.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Onda bismo bili susjedi, to bi bilo super.
lines.en: Then we'd be neighbours, that would be great.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tko zna, jednoga dana možda i budemo.
lines.en: Who knows, one day maybe we will be.

days.title: Ponavljanje: stupnjevanje pridjeva
days.objective: Compare things with the comparative and superlative, including the common irregular forms.
days.paretoFocus: Comparison is how you weigh options aloud, from flats to holidays; the irregular forms (bolji, gori, veći) come up constantly.
drills: Compare two cities you know in five sentences using comparatives.
List the comparative and superlative of dobar, loš, velik, malen, dug.
Say which of three options is the best and why.

items: Recall the irregulars: dobar to bolji, loš to gori, velik to veći.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Compare two foods using 'ukusniji od' with the genitive.

activities.title: Komparativ i superlativ
activities.intro: Regular comparatives add -iji (brz to brži is a shortened type, jeftin to jeftiniji is regular), and the superlative prefixes naj-. Learn the irregulars by heart: dobar to bolji to najbolji, loš to gori to najgori, velik to veći, malen to manji. The compared-with noun stands in the genitive after od.

items.hr: Ovaj stan je veći i svjetliji od prošloga.
items.en: This flat is bigger and brighter than the previous one.

items.hr: Najam je jeftiniji, ali je četvrt bučnija.
items.en: The rent is cheaper, but the district is noisier.

items.hr: Za mene je mir najvažniji od svega.
items.en: For me peace is the most important thing of all.

items.hr: Javni prijevoz je ovdje bolji nego u starom kvartu.
items.en: Public transport here is better than in the old neighbourhood.

items.hr: Ovo je najbolja odluka koju smo donijeli.
items.en: This is the best decision we've made.

activities.title: Vježba: usporedi
activities.intro: Build comparatives and superlatives, regular and irregular.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct comparative of dobar: 'Prijevoz je ___ nego prije.'
questions.explanation: Dobar is irregular: the comparative is bolji, not a regular dobriji.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct superlative: 'Mir mi je ___ od svega.'
questions.explanation: The superlative prefixes naj- to the comparative vazniji, giving najvazniji.

questions.prompt: Complete the comparative of jeftin: Najam je ___ nego prije. (cheaper)
questions.explanation: Jeftin forms the regular comparative jeftiniji with the suffix -iji.

questions.prompt: Put the compared noun in the right case after od: Ovaj stan je veći od ___ . (the previous one)
questions.explanation: Comparison with od takes the genitive; prosli becomes prosloga.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'This is the best decision.'
questions.explanation: Najbolja is the irregular superlative of dobar and agrees with the feminine odluka.

activities.title: Koji stan je bolji
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You compare two flats with a friend who is helping you choose.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I, koji ti se stan više svidio?
lines.en: So, which flat did you like more?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Drugi je veći i svjetliji od prvoga.
lines.en: The second is bigger and brighter than the first.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A cijena? Je li skuplji?
lines.en: And the price? Is it more expensive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne, zapravo je jeftiniji, ali je četvrt bučnija.
lines.en: No, it's actually cheaper, but the district is noisier.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što ti je najvažnije od svega?
lines.en: What's the most important thing of all to you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mir, zato mi je prvi ipak bolji izbor.
lines.en: Peace, so the first is a better choice for me after all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onda si donio najbolju odluku za sebe.
lines.en: Then you've made the best decision for yourself.

days.title: Ponavljanje: odnosna zamjenica koji
days.objective: Link two clauses with koji in the right gender, number and case.
days.paretoFocus: Koji is how you describe people and things in longer sentences; its case depends on its job in the second clause, which trips up most learners.
drills: Join five pairs of short sentences into one using koji.
For each koji, state its gender, number and case and why.
Describe three people you know with a koji clause each.

items: Recall: koji agrees in gender and number with its noun, but takes its case from its own clause.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'the friend with whom I travelled' using s kojim.

activities.title: Koji preuzima svoj padež
activities.intro: Koji matches the gender and number of the noun it refers to, but its case comes from its role in the relative clause. Subject: čovjek koji radi. Object: knjiga koju čitam. With a preposition: prijatelj s kojim putujem, grad u kojem živim.

items.hr: To je kolegica koja mi je pomogla oko selidbe.
items.en: That's the colleague who helped me with the move.

items.hr: Knjiga koju čitam govori o povijesti grada.
items.en: The book I'm reading is about the city's history.

items.hr: Prijatelj s kojim putujem živi u Rijeci.
items.en: The friend I travel with lives in Rijeka.

items.hr: Ovo je kvart u kojem sam odrastao.
items.en: This is the neighbourhood I grew up in.

items.hr: Ljudi kojima vjerujem uvijek kažu istinu.
items.en: The people I trust always tell the truth.

activities.title: Vježba: izaberi koji
activities.intro: Pick the correct form of koji for each clause.

questions.prompt: Which fits: 'Knjiga ___ čitam je odlična.' (that I'm reading)
questions.explanation: Koji here is the object of citam, so it is feminine accusative koju, agreeing with knjiga.

questions.prompt: Which fits: 'Prijatelj ___ putujem živi u Rijeci.' (with whom)
questions.explanation: The preposition s takes the instrumental, and the noun is masculine: s kojim.

questions.prompt: Complete with the subject form referring to kolegica: To je kolegica ___ mi je pomogla. (who)
questions.explanation: As the subject of the clause and feminine, koji becomes koja.

questions.prompt: Complete the locative after u referring to kvart: Ovo je kvart u ___ sam odrastao. (which)
questions.explanation: U for place takes the locative; masculine koji becomes kojem.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'The people I trust tell the truth.'
questions.explanation: Vjerovati governs the dative, so koji becomes the plural dative kojima.

activities.title: Tko je tko na zabavi
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. At a party a friend asks who various people are and you answer with koji clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tko je ona žena kraj prozora?
lines.en: Who's that woman by the window?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To je kolegica koja mi je pomogla oko selidbe.
lines.en: That's the colleague who helped me with the move.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A muškarac s kojim razgovara?
lines.en: And the man she's talking to?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To je prijatelj s kojim često putujem.
lines.en: That's the friend I often travel with.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Poznaješ li ovdje još nekoga?
lines.en: Do you know anyone else here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, ono su ljudi kojima najviše vjerujem.
lines.en: Yes, those are the people I trust the most.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Baš lijepo društvo si okupio.
lines.en: You've gathered really nice company.

days.title: Ponavljanje: genitiv u svakodnevici
days.objective: Use the genitive for quantities, dates, absence and part-of after its many everyday triggers.
days.paretoFocus: The genitive is the busiest case in daily speech, from shopping amounts to dates and 'without'; drilling its triggers together builds real fluency.
drills: Write a shopping list with five quantities, each forcing the genitive plural.
Say three dates using the genitive of the day and month.
Make four sentences with bez, blizu, poslije and iz.

items: Recall genitive triggers: numbers five and up, quantities, bez, iz, od, dates.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'a kilo of apples' and 'a glass of water' in Croatian.

activities.title: Kad tražimo genitiv
activities.intro: The genitive appears after quantities (kilogram jabuka, čaša vode), after numbers five and above (šest dana), after many prepositions (bez, iz, od, do, blizu, poslije) and in dates (prvoga svibnja). Recognising the trigger tells you the case before you even reach the noun.

items.hr: Kupila sam kilogram jabuka i litru mlijeka.
items.en: I bought a kilo of apples and a litre of milk.

items.hr: Trebamo još šest jaja za kolač.
items.en: We need six more eggs for the cake.

items.hr: Rođena je prvoga svibnja tisuću devetsto devedesete.
items.en: She was born on the first of May nineteen ninety.

items.hr: Stanujem blizu kolodvora, bez auta mi je lako.
items.en: I live near the station, without a car it's easy for me.

items.hr: Vratili su se iz Njemačke poslije ljeta.
items.en: They came back from Germany after the summer.

activities.title: Vježba: genitiv u praksi
activities.intro: Produce the genitive after its everyday triggers.

questions.prompt: Which is correct after šest: 'Trebamo još šest ___ za kolač.' (eggs)
questions.explanation: Numbers five and up take the genitive plural; jaje becomes jaja.

questions.prompt: Which date form is right: 'Rođena je ___ svibnja.' (on the first of May)
questions.explanation: Dates put the day in the genitive ordinal: prvoga svibnja.

questions.prompt: Complete the quantity phrase after litru: Kupila sam litru ___ . (of milk)
questions.explanation: A measure like litra takes the genitive; mlijeko becomes mlijeka.

questions.prompt: Complete after bez, the noun: Bez ___ mi je lakše u gradu. (a car)
questions.explanation: Bez takes the genitive; auto becomes auta.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'They came back from Germany.'
questions.explanation: Iz takes the genitive of origin, so Njemacka becomes Njemacke.

activities.title: Na tržnici
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You shop at the market and manage quantities in the genitive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar dan, izvolite, što će vam trebati?
lines.en: Good day, go ahead, what will you need?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Molim vas kilogram jabuka i pola kilograma rajčica.
lines.en: A kilo of apples please and half a kilo of tomatoes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odmah. Treba li vam još nešto?
lines.en: Right away. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, šest jaja i litru mlijeka.
lines.en: Yes, six eggs and a litre of milk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Evo, to bi bilo sve?
lines.en: Here you are, would that be everything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bilo bi, hvala, stanujem blizu pa nosim lako.
lines.en: It would be, thanks, I live nearby so it's easy to carry.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ugodan dan i navratite opet.
lines.en: Have a nice day and come again.

days.title: Ponavljanje: futur prvi i drugi
days.objective: Talk about the future with the futur prvi and use the futur drugi in kad and ako clauses.
days.paretoFocus: Making plans and setting conditions both need the future; the futur drugi in time and if clauses is a distinctly Croatian point worth revisiting.
drills: Make five weekend plans using the futur prvi.
Build three sentences of the form 'Kad budem..., ...' with the futur drugi.
Turn two plans into conditions with ako plus the futur drugi.

items: Recall: main-clause future is ću raditi; after kad and ako use budem radio.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'When I finish, I'll call you' with the futur drugi in the kad clause.

activities.title: Futur u planovima i uvjetima
activities.intro: The futur prvi (ću, ćeš plus infinitive, or radit ću) states plain future plans. In a subordinate clause of time or condition after kad, ako or čim, Croatian uses the futur drugi (budem radio), not the present: Kad budem imao vremena, javit ću ti se.

items.hr: Ovaj vikend ćemo obići stari grad.
items.en: This weekend we'll tour the old town.

items.hr: Kad budem imao vremena, javit ću ti se.
items.en: When I have time, I'll get in touch with you.

items.hr: Čim stigneš, nazovi me.
items.en: As soon as you arrive, call me.

items.hr: Ako bude lijepo, ići ćemo na more.
items.en: If the weather's nice, we'll go to the sea.

items.hr: Radit ću do pet, a poslije sam slobodan.
items.en: I'll work until five, and after that I'm free.

activities.title: Vježba: futur u rečenici
activities.intro: Choose the right future form for main and subordinate clauses.

questions.prompt: Which is correct after kad: 'Kad ___ vremena, javit ću ti se.'
questions.explanation: A future time clause with kad takes the futur drugi budem imao, never the plain present.

questions.prompt: Which states a plain future plan: 'Ovaj vikend ___ stari grad.'
questions.explanation: A main-clause plan uses the futur prvi cemo obici; the futur drugi belongs in subordinate clauses.

questions.prompt: Complete the condition after ako with the futur drugi, two words: Ako ___ ___ lijepo, idemo na more. (it is)
questions.explanation: The if-clause about the future uses the futur drugi bude bilo.

questions.prompt: Complete the futur prvi for 'I': ___ ću do pet, a poslije sam slobodan. (I'll work)
questions.explanation: Radit cu is the futur prvi; the t of the infinitive drops before cu when written together apart.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'As soon as you arrive, call me.'
questions.explanation: After cim the perfective present stignes carries future meaning, and nazovi is the imperative.

activities.title: Dogovaramo vikend
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a friend arrange the coming weekend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Imaš li planove za ovaj vikend?
lines.en: Do you have plans for this weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Radit ću u subotu ujutro, a popodne sam slobodan.
lines.en: I'll work Saturday morning, but in the afternoon I'm free.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onda da odemo na more ako bude lijepo?
lines.en: Then shall we go to the sea if the weather's nice?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Može, kad završim s poslom, javit ću ti se.
lines.en: Sure, when I finish work, I'll get in touch.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Super, čim stigneš do plaže, nazovi me.
lines.en: Great, as soon as you get to the beach, call me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hoću, spremit ću i nešto za jelo.
lines.en: I will, I'll also pack something to eat.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onda se vidimo u subotu, veselim se.
lines.en: Then see you Saturday, I'm looking forward to it.

days.title: Ponavljanje: imperativ i upute
days.objective: Give clear instructions and directions with affirmative and negative imperatives.
days.paretoFocus: Recipes, directions and advice all run on the imperative; controlling both the do and the don't forms makes you genuinely useful in daily situations.
drills: Give a five-step recipe using imperatives.
Give directions from the station to your home in the imperative.
Turn three instructions into their negative form with nemoj.

items: Recall: affirmative imperative endings -i, -j, -ite; negative uses nemoj plus infinitive.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'Turn left and go straight on' in Croatian.

activities.title: Reci mi kako
activities.intro: The imperative gives the ti-form (dođi, kreni, pij), the vi-form (dođite, krenite) and the we-form (krenimo). For prohibitions use nemoj, nemojte plus the infinitive. Instructions chain these with sequence words: prvo, zatim, na kraju.

items.hr: Prvo zagrij pećnicu na dvjesto stupnjeva.
items.en: First heat the oven to two hundred degrees.

items.hr: Zatim pomiješaj brašno, jaja i mlijeko.
items.en: Then mix the flour, eggs and milk.

items.hr: Nemoj otvarati pećnicu prije vremena.
items.en: Don't open the oven too early.

items.hr: Na križanju skreni lijevo pa idi ravno.
items.en: At the crossroads turn left, then go straight on.

items.hr: Krenimo odmah da ne zakasnimo.
items.en: Let's set off now so we're not late.

activities.title: Vježba: daj uputu
activities.intro: Form the imperative correctly, positive and negative.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct ti-imperative: '___ lijevo na križanju.' (turn)
questions.explanation: The ti-imperative of skrenuti is skreni; skrenite would be the vi-form.

questions.prompt: Which correctly forbids an action: '___ otvarati pećnicu prerano.'
questions.explanation: A prohibition uses nemoj plus the infinitive otvarati; a da-clause after nemoj is not standard.

questions.prompt: Complete the we-imperative of krenuti, one word: ___ odmah da ne zakasnimo. (let's set off)
questions.explanation: The we-form imperative krenimo means let's set off.

questions.prompt: Complete the sequence word for the first step: ___ zagrij pećnicu na dvjesto stupnjeva. (first)
questions.explanation: Prvo orders the steps of an instruction, first of all.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Mix the flour and the eggs.'
questions.explanation: Pomijesaj is the ti-imperative; the objects brasno and jaja stand in the accusative.

activities.title: Kako do tebe
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is coming over and asks for directions from the station.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Stižem vlakom, kako da dođem do tebe s kolodvora?
lines.en: I'm coming by train, how do I get to your place from the station?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Izađi na glavni izlaz i skreni lijevo.
lines.en: Come out at the main exit and turn left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro, a onda?
lines.en: Okay, and then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Idi ravno do parka pa prijeđi cestu.
lines.en: Go straight to the park, then cross the road.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trebam li skrenuti negdje kod parka?
lines.en: Do I need to turn anywhere near the park?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, iza parka skreni desno, moja je treća zgrada.
lines.en: Yes, behind the park turn right, mine is the third building.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Super, ne brini, naći ću te lako.
lines.en: Great, don't worry, I'll find you easily.

days.title: Ponavljanje: mišljenja i osjećaji
days.objective: State opinions, agree and disagree politely, and name feelings in a discussion.
days.paretoFocus: Discussion is the heart of the speaking exam; a stock of opinion and reaction phrases lets you respond naturally instead of freezing.
drills: Give your opinion on a film in three sentences with different opinion phrases.
Agree with one statement and politely disagree with another.
Name three feelings you had this week and why.

items: Recall openers: mislim da, čini mi se, slažem se, ne bih se složio.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'I'm afraid that...' and 'I'm glad that...' in Croatian.

activities.title: Kako reći što misliš
activities.intro: Open opinions with mislim da, čini mi se da or po meni. Agree with slažem se, imaš pravo; disagree gently with ne bih se složio, nisam siguran. Name feelings with bojim se da, drago mi je, žao mi je, ponosan sam. These phrases keep a discussion flowing.

items.hr: Mislim da je ovaj film predug, iako je lijepo snimljen.
items.en: I think this film is too long, although it's beautifully shot.

items.hr: Slažem se s tobom, ali glazba mi je bila odlična.
items.en: I agree with you, but I found the music excellent.

items.hr: Ne bih se složio da je dosadan, meni je bio napet.
items.en: I wouldn't agree that it's boring, to me it was gripping.

items.hr: Bojim se da nećemo stići na sljedeću predstavu.
items.en: I'm afraid we won't make the next show.

items.hr: Drago mi je što smo ipak izašli večeras.
items.en: I'm glad we went out tonight after all.

activities.title: Vježba: izrazi stav
activities.intro: Use the right opinion, agreement and feeling phrases.

questions.prompt: Which politely signals DISAGREEMENT?
questions.explanation: Ne bih se slozio is the soft, polite way to disagree; the others all express agreement.

questions.prompt: Which phrase names a fear or worry?
questions.explanation: Bojati se da introduces a worry; the others express gladness, pride or agreement.

questions.prompt: Complete the opinion opener, two words: ___ ___ je film predug. (I think that)
questions.explanation: Mislim da is the core phrase for introducing an opinion.

questions.prompt: Complete the feeling of gladness, two words: ___ ___ je što smo izašli. (I'm glad that)
questions.explanation: Drago mi je (sto) expresses being glad about something.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I agree with you.'
questions.explanation: Slagati se s takes the instrumental, so with you is s tobom.

activities.title: Kakav ti je bio film
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You leave the cinema and debate the film with a friend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I, kako ti se svidio film?
lines.en: So, how did you like the film?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mislim da je bio malo predug, ali lijepo snimljen.
lines.en: I think it was a bit too long, but beautifully shot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažem se, no gluma mi je bila slaba.
lines.en: I agree, but I found the acting weak.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne bih se složio, meni je glavni glumac bio odličan.
lines.en: I wouldn't agree, I thought the lead actor was excellent.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Možda imaš pravo. Idemo li opet idući tjedan?
lines.en: Maybe you're right. Shall we go again next week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rado, drago mi je što smo večeras izašli.
lines.en: Gladly, I'm glad we went out tonight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I meni, ovo nam je postao lijep običaj.
lines.en: Me too, this has become a nice habit for us.

days.title: Ponavljanje: neupravni govor
days.objective: Report what someone said, asked or told you to do, adjusting pronouns and using da, li and neka.
days.paretoFocus: Relaying messages is a daily need; Croatian reported speech keeps the tense but shifts pronouns and links with da, whether or neka.
drills: Report three statements a friend made, changing the pronouns correctly.
Turn two direct questions into reported ones with da li becomes je li or the li form.
Report one command using neka or da plus the present.

items: Recall: statements link with da, yes-no questions with je li or -li, commands with neka.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Report 'Come tomorrow' as 'He said I should come tomorrow.'

activities.title: Prenošenje tuđih riječi
activities.intro: To report a statement, keep the tense and add da: Rekao je da dolazi sutra. For a yes-no question, use je li or the li form: Pitala je je li sve u redu. For a command, report it with neka or da plus the present: Rekao je neka dođem.

items.hr: Rekao je da će zakasniti pola sata.
items.en: He said he'd be half an hour late.

items.hr: Pitala me je li mi se svidjela predstava.
items.en: She asked me whether I'd liked the show.

items.hr: Rekli su da nemaju vremena ovaj tjedan.
items.en: They said they don't have time this week.

items.hr: Molila je da joj pomognem oko selidbe.
items.en: She asked me to help her with the move.

items.hr: Šef je rekao neka dođemo ranije.
items.en: The boss said we should come earlier.

activities.title: Vježba: prenesi poruku
activities.intro: Convert direct speech into correct reported speech.

questions.prompt: Report 'Dolazim sutra.' as: 'Rekao je da ___ sutra.'
questions.explanation: The tense stays present but the pronoun shifts from I to he, giving da dolazi.

questions.prompt: Report the question 'Je li sve u redu?' as: 'Pitala je ___ sve u redu.'
questions.explanation: A reported yes-no question uses je li or the li form; da li is not standard Croatian.

questions.prompt: Report the request with the linking word: Molila je ___ joj pomognem. (that)
questions.explanation: A reported request or command links with da plus the present: da joj pomognem.

questions.prompt: Report the command using the particle: Šef je rekao ___ dođemo ranije. (that we should)
questions.explanation: Neka plus the present reports a command: neka dodemo ranije.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'He said he'd be late.'
questions.explanation: The report keeps the futur ce zakasniti and links to Rekao je with da.

activities.title: Što je rekla
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You relay to a friend what a third person said on the phone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li razgovarao s Marijom? Što je rekla?
lines.en: Did you talk to Marija? What did she say?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rekla je da će zakasniti pola sata.
lines.en: She said she'd be half an hour late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A je li pitala nešto za mene?
lines.en: And did she ask anything about me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pitala je je li ti dobro nakon puta.
lines.en: She asked whether you're well after the trip.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Treba li joj nešto?
lines.en: Does she need anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Molila je da joj pomognemo oko selidbe u subotu.
lines.en: She asked us to help her with the move on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Naravno, reci joj da ćemo doći.
lines.en: Of course, tell her we'll come.

days.title: Ponavljanje: velika obnova
days.objective: Combine cases, aspect, clitics and the future in one flowing account of a trip.
days.paretoFocus: The exam mixes everything at once; a final spiral that puts all the level's grammar into a single natural narrative is the best rehearsal there is.
drills: Tell the story of a trip that went wrong, using at least four cases and both aspects.
Include one conditional, one relative clause and one reported sentence.
Retell it in two minutes without stopping, then fix any errors you heard.

items: Recall how the pieces cooperate: case for role, aspect for time, clitics in second place.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Tell one sentence that uses a case, an aspect choice and a clitic together.

activities.title: Sve na jednom mjestu
activities.intro: Here the level's grammar works together: the genitive of absence, the accusative object, aspect for background and event, clitics in second position, a relative koji clause and a future plan. Read for how each rule quietly does its job inside a normal story.

items.hr: Putovali smo prema jugu kad nam se pokvario auto.
items.en: We were driving south when our car broke down.

items.hr: Bez mehaničara ne bismo krenuli dalje do večeri.
items.en: Without a mechanic we wouldn't have moved on until evening.

items.hr: Čovjek koji nam je pomogao nije htio uzeti novac.
items.en: The man who helped us didn't want to take money.

items.hr: Kad stignemo kući, poslat ćemo mu razglednicu.
items.en: When we get home, we'll send him a postcard.

items.hr: Cijeli taj dan pamtit ćemo s osmijehom.
items.en: We'll remember that whole day with a smile.

activities.title: Vježba: sve zajedno
activities.intro: Apply several rules at once, as the exam does.

questions.prompt: Which completes the aspect and clitic correctly: 'Putovali smo kad ___ pokvario auto.'
questions.explanation: The cluster runs dative nam then reflexive se; the perfective pokvario marks the sudden event.

questions.prompt: Which relative form is right: 'Čovjek ___ nam je pomogao nije htio novac.'
questions.explanation: The man is the subject of the relative clause, so masculine nominative koji.

questions.prompt: Complete the genitive after bez: ___ mehaničara ne bismo krenuli dalje. (without)
questions.explanation: Bez triggers the genitive mehanicara and opens the unreal condition.

questions.prompt: Complete the futur drugi time clause, two words: Kad ___ ___ kući, poslat ćemo razglednicu. (we arrive)
questions.explanation: A future kad clause takes the futur drugi budemo stigli, not the present.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'We'll send him a postcard.'
questions.explanation: The futur prvi Poslat cemo carries the dative clitic mu and the accusative object razglednicu.

activities.title: Putovanje koje ću pamtiti
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You tell a friend the story of a trip where your car broke down.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Čula sam da vam je bilo svašta na putovanju, pričaj.
lines.en: I heard you had quite an adventure on your trip, tell me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Putovali smo prema jugu kad nam se pokvario auto.
lines.en: We were driving south when our car broke down.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ajme, i što ste onda napravili?
lines.en: Oh no, and what did you do then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jedan čovjek koji je prolazio stao je i pomogao nam.
lines.en: A man who was passing stopped and helped us.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Baš ljubazno, jeste li mu se nekako zahvalili?
lines.en: How kind, did you thank him somehow?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad stignemo kući, poslat ćemo mu razglednicu.
lines.en: When we get home, we'll send him a postcard.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Prekrasna gesta, takve ljude treba pamtiti.
lines.en: A lovely gesture, people like that are worth remembering.

days.title: Hrana i prehrana
days.objective: Argue a position about healthy versus convenient eating and defend it in conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about food and health is one of the most common everyday debates, and it lets you state and defend an opinion, the core B1 skill.
drills: Say one thing you think is healthy and one that is not, with Mislim da...
Compare two foods using zdravije od...
Give one reason to plan meals in advance.

items: Say 'I think home food is healthier than ready-made'.
Say 'We should eat less sugar' in standard Croatian.
Say 'Bad habits are hard to change'.

activities.title: Zdrava prehrana
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position: home cooking is worth the time. You will state opinions with Mislim da, compare foods and talk about habits.

items.hr: Mislim da je domaća hrana zdravija od gotove.
items.en: I think home-made food is healthier than ready-made.
items.note: opinion + comparison

items.hr: Za tijelo je važno jesti povrće svaki dan.
items.en: For the body it is important to eat vegetables every day.
items.note: tijelo = body

items.hr: Brza hrana je jednostavna, ali ako je stalno jedeš, nije zdrava.
items.en: Fast food is simple, but if you eat it constantly, it is not healthy.
items.note: stalno = constantly

items.hr: Trebamo planirati obroke unaprijed.
items.en: We should plan meals in advance.
items.note: trebamo + infinitive planirati

items.hr: Loše navike teško je promijeniti.
items.en: Bad habits are hard to change.
items.note: navika = habit

activities.title: Zdravo ili praktično
activities.intro: Practise stating opinions about food and reviewing an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian: 'We should eat less sugar.'
questions.explanation: After trebamo Croatian takes the infinitive jesti, and manje governs the genitive šećera; the da-clauses are not standard Croatian.
