questions.prompt: For the body it is important to eat vegetables: Za ___ je važno jesti povrće.
questions.explanation: tijelo is 'body'; after the preposition za it keeps this accusative form here.

questions.prompt: Review the relative pronoun: 'The soup that I cooked is cold.' Juha ___ sam skuhao je hladna.
questions.explanation: juha is the direct object of skuhao, so the relative pronoun is the feminine accusative koju.

questions.prompt: We should plan meals in advance: Trebamo ___ obroke unaprijed.
questions.explanation: After the modal trebamo Croatian uses the infinitive planirati.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I think home food is healthier.'
questions.explanation: Mislim da opens the opinion, then the clause je domaća hrana zdravija.

activities.title: Kuhati ili ne
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend defends fast food for lack of time; you argue that cooking at home is worth it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nemam vremena kuhati, pa često jedem brzu hranu.
lines.en: I don't have time to cook, so I often eat fast food.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Razumijem te, ali mislim da to nije dobro za zdravlje.
lines.en: I understand you, but I think that isn't good for your health.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zar je domaća hrana stvarno toliko bolja?
lines.en: Is home food really that much better?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jest. Zdrava prehrana ne znači uvijek skupu hranu.
lines.en: It is. Healthy eating doesn't always mean expensive food.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Možda, no priprema uzima previše vremena.
lines.en: Maybe, but the preparation takes too much time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ako planiraš obroke, izgubiš manje vremena nego što misliš.
lines.en: If you plan your meals, you lose less time than you think.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro, možda bih mogao kuhati barem vikendom.
lines.en: All right, maybe I could cook at least on weekends.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To je već dobar prvi korak.
lines.en: That's already a good first step.

days.title: Stanovanje i susjedstvo
days.objective: Weigh living in a city flat against a house outside town and defend your choice.
days.paretoFocus: Where and how you live is a rich everyday topic, and describing space, neighbours and trade-offs lets you argue a preference with real reasons.
drills: Describe your home in three sentences: rooms, size, neighbours.
Give one advantage and one disadvantage of a flat in the centre.
Say what matters most to you when choosing a home.

items: Say 'I live in a flat in the city centre'.
Say 'A house is bigger than a flat' with the genitive.
Say 'Being close to shops means a lot to me'.

activities.title: Gdje i kako živim
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on city versus suburb living. You will describe rooms and neighbours and say what closeness is worth to you.

items.hr: Živim u stanu u središtu grada.
items.en: I live in a flat in the city centre.
items.note: stan = flat

items.hr: Zgrada je stara, ali u dobrom stanju.
items.en: The building is old but in good condition.
items.note: stanje = condition

items.hr: Susjedi su tihi i ljubazni.
items.en: The neighbours are quiet and kind.
items.note: susjedi = neighbours

items.hr: Volio bih više prostora i bolje uređenje.
items.en: I'd like more space and a better layout.
items.note: prostor = space, uređenje = layout

items.hr: Blizina trgovina i škole mi puno znači.
items.en: Being close to shops and school means a lot to me.
items.note: blizina = closeness

activities.title: Stan ili kuća
activities.intro: Practise describing housing and reviewing an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian word for 'family': Selim se zbog svoje ___.
questions.explanation: obitelj is the Croatian word for family; after zbog it stands in the genitive obitelji, while the other options belong to another variety.

questions.prompt: The flat has three rooms: Stan ima tri ___.
questions.explanation: After the number tri the noun prostorija takes this counting form prostorije.

questions.prompt: Review the comparison: 'A house is bigger than a flat.' Kuća je ___ od stana.
questions.explanation: kuća is feminine, so the comparative is veća, followed by od plus the genitive stana.

questions.prompt: The neighbours are quiet: ___ su tihi.
questions.explanation: The plural of susjed is susjedi, the subject of the sentence.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'I live in a flat in the city centre.'
questions.explanation: Two locative phrases with u stack up: u stanu and u središtu, closed by the genitive grada.

activities.title: Preseljenje
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants to move to a house outside town; you defend life in the city centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Razmišljam o preseljenju u kuću izvan grada.
lines.en: I'm thinking about moving to a house outside town.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zašto? Meni je život u gradu puno praktičniji.
lines.en: Why? For me city life is much more practical.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: U kući ima više prostora i mira.
lines.en: In a house there is more space and quiet.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, ali svaki dan bi gubio sat vremena na put.
lines.en: Yes, but every day you'd lose an hour on the commute.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne smeta mi vožnja ako imam veći dom.
lines.en: The drive doesn't bother me if I have a bigger home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mene bi to smetalo. Volim sve imati blizu.
lines.en: It would bother me. I like to have everything close by.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Svatko ima svoje potrebe, valjda.
lines.en: Everyone has their own needs, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Točno, zato je izbor tako težak.
lines.en: Exactly, that's why the choice is so hard.

days.title: Priroda i godišnja doba
days.objective: Argue which season and setting you prefer and discuss noticeable climate change.
days.paretoFocus: Seasons and nature are everyday small talk everywhere, and this topic carries the standard Croatian words zrak and vrijeme that Serbian renders differently.
drills: Name your favourite season and give one reason with jer.
Compare two seasons using toplije or hladnije.
Say one change in the weather you have noticed lately.

items: Say 'I love spring most because nature is most beautiful then'.
Say 'The air is cleaner in the mountains' with the Croatian word for air.
Say 'In winter it snows and in summer it is hot'.

activities.title: Kroz godinu
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on the best season and on whether winters are changing. Note the standard Croatian zrak for air and the ijekavian snijeg and vrijeme.

items.hr: Najviše volim proljeće jer je priroda tada najljepša.
items.en: I love spring most because nature is most beautiful then.
items.note: proljeće = spring

items.hr: Zimi pada snijeg, a ljeti je vrlo toplo.
items.en: In winter it snows, and in summer it is very hot.
items.note: snijeg, the ijekavian form

items.hr: Zrak je u planinama čišći nego u gradu.
items.en: The air is cleaner in the mountains than in the city.
items.note: zrak = air

items.hr: Klima se stalno mijenja i zime su blaže.
items.en: The climate is constantly changing and winters are milder.
items.note: stalno = constantly

items.hr: Za vikend planiramo izlet u prirodu.
items.en: For the weekend we're planning a trip into nature.
items.note: priroda = nature

activities.title: Doba godine
activities.intro: Practise seasonal vocabulary and review an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian word for 'air': Planinski ___ je čist.
questions.explanation: zrak is the Croatian word for air; vazduh belongs to another variety, and the case forms zraka and vazduha would not fit the nominative subject here.

questions.prompt: In winter it snows: Zimi pada ___.
questions.explanation: The Croatian word is the ijekavian snijeg.

questions.prompt: Review aspect: 'The weather changed suddenly.' Vrijeme se naglo ___.
questions.explanation: A single completed change takes the perfective past promijenilo; mijenjalo is imperfective and mijenja is present.

questions.prompt: The air is cleaner in the mountains: Zrak je u planinama ___.
questions.explanation: The comparative of čist is čišći.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'In summer it is very hot.'
questions.explanation: The time adverb Ljeti opens, then the impersonal je vrlo toplo.

activities.title: Koje doba je najbolje
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend lives for summer; you defend winter and you both weigh whether winters are changing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne mogu dočekati ljeto, zima mi je grozna.
lines.en: I can't wait for summer, I find winter awful.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zanimljivo, meni je zima najdraže doba godine.
lines.en: Interesting, for me winter is my favourite season.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zar ti ne smeta hladnoća i kratki dani?
lines.en: Doesn't the cold and the short days bother you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne previše. Volim snijeg i mir u prirodi.
lines.en: Not too much. I love the snow and the quiet in nature.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ipak, primjećuješ li da zime više nisu kao prije?
lines.en: Still, do you notice that winters aren't like they used to be?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Istina, snijega ima sve manje. To me zabrinjava.
lines.en: True, there's less and less snow. That worries me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I mene, iako uživam u toplijim zimama.
lines.en: Me too, even though I enjoy the milder winters.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vidiš, ista promjena, dvije različite reakcije.
lines.en: You see, the same change, two different reactions.

days.title: Tehnologija u životu
days.objective: Take a position on how much smartphones help or harm daily life and defend it.
days.paretoFocus: Screens shape everyone's day, so being able to argue about their upside and downside is both very current and a clean B1 opinion task.
drills: Name two things your phone helps you do every day.
Say one problem too much screen time causes.
State whether you think we depend too much on technology, with a reason.

items: Say 'Without a smartphone I can hardly function'.
Say 'Sometimes I turn off the phone to rest'.
Say 'We should use the phone less' in standard Croatian.

activities.title: Život sa zaslonom
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on our dependence on screens. You will describe how technology helps and how you set limits.

items.hr: Bez pametnog telefona teško funkcioniram.
items.en: Without a smartphone I can hardly function.
items.note: funkcionirati = to function

items.hr: Cijeli dan gledam u ekran i to me umara.
items.en: All day I look at a screen and it tires me.
items.note: ekran = screen

items.hr: Nova aplikacija mi jednostavno olakšava posao.
items.en: The new app simply makes my work easier.
items.note: jednostavno = simply

items.hr: Ponekad isključim mobitel da se odmorim.
items.en: Sometimes I turn off the phone to rest.
items.note: isključiti = to switch off

items.hr: Preko interneta lako održavam kontakt s obitelji.
items.en: Over the internet I easily keep in touch with my family.
items.note: kontakt = contact

activities.title: Za i protiv zaslona
activities.intro: Practise talking about technology and review an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian: 'We should use the phone less.'
questions.explanation: After trebamo Croatian uses the infinitive koristiti; the da-clauses are not standard Croatian.

questions.prompt: Sometimes I turn off the phone: Ponekad ___ mobitel.
questions.explanation: isključiti in the first person singular present is isključim.

questions.prompt: Review the relative pronoun: 'This is the app that I use every day.' Ovo je aplikacija ___ koristim svaki dan.
questions.explanation: aplikacija is the object of koristim, so the relative pronoun is the feminine accusative koju.

questions.prompt: All day I look at a screen: Cijeli dan gledam u ___.
questions.explanation: ekran is 'screen'; after the preposition u of direction it takes this accusative form.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Without a smartphone I can hardly function.'
questions.explanation: Bez governs the genitive pametnog telefona, then teško funkcioniram completes the clause.

activities.title: Previše ekrana
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend criticises your constant phone use; you argue technology is useful if kept in check.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne razumijem zašto stalno gledaš u mobitel.
lines.en: I don't understand why you keep looking at your phone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato što preko njega radim, čitam vijesti i pišem poruke.
lines.en: Because through it I work, read the news and write messages.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali izgubiš puno vremena na društvene mreže.
lines.en: But you lose a lot of time on social media.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Priznajem, to je istina. Zato navečer isključim obavijesti.
lines.en: I admit, that's true. That's why I turn off notifications in the evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mislim da bismo svi trebali manje ovisiti o zaslonima.
lines.en: I think we should all depend less on screens.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se, no tehnologija nam ipak olakšava život.
lines.en: I agree, but technology does make our life easier.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sve dok mi kontroliramo nju, a ne ona nas.
lines.en: As long as we control it, and not it us.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro rečeno. Ravnoteža je ključ.
lines.en: Well said. Balance is the key.

days.title: Promet i sigurnost
days.objective: Argue how a city should balance cars, public transport and cyclists, safely.
days.paretoFocus: Getting around and doing it safely is a daily concern, and this topic lets you argue urban choices with reasons, a solid B1 discussion.
drills: Compare two ways to travel across town using brži od...
Name one traffic danger and one way to prevent it.
State whether the city should favour cars or bikes, with a reason.

items: Say 'Public transport is faster than a car'.
Say 'Drivers must pay attention to cyclists' in standard Croatian.
Say 'The traffic sign warns of danger'.

activities.title: Na cesti
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on how a city should move. You will compare transport, name dangers and talk about rules.

items.hr: U gradu je javni prijevoz brži od automobila.
items.en: In the city public transport is faster than a car.
items.note: prijevoz = transport

items.hr: Vozači moraju paziti na bicikliste.
items.en: Drivers must watch out for cyclists.
items.note: moraju + infinitive paziti

items.hr: Prometni znak upozorava na opasnost.
items.en: The traffic sign warns of danger.
items.note: opasnost = danger

items.hr: Sigurnosni pojas može spasiti život.
items.en: A seatbelt can save a life.
items.note: pojas = belt

items.hr: Poštivanje pravila smanjuje broj nesreća.
items.en: Respecting the rules reduces the number of accidents.
items.note: pravilo = rule

activities.title: Sigurno kroz grad
activities.intro: Practise traffic language and review an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian: 'Drivers must slow down here.'
questions.explanation: After moraju Croatian uses the infinitive usporiti, and the plural subject vozači needs moraju, not mora.

questions.prompt: The traffic sign warns of danger: Prometni znak upozorava na ___.
questions.explanation: opasnost is 'danger'; after na it keeps this accusative form here.

questions.prompt: Review the comparison: 'The tram is faster than the bus.' Tramvaj je ___ od autobusa.
questions.explanation: tramvaj is masculine, so the comparative is brži, followed by od plus the genitive autobusa.

questions.prompt: Drivers must watch out for cyclists: Vozači moraju ___ na bicikliste.
questions.explanation: After the modal moraju Croatian uses the infinitive paziti.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Public transport is faster than a car.'
questions.explanation: The comparative brži takes od and the genitive automobila.

activities.title: Auto ili bicikl
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend won't give up the car; you argue the city should do more for cyclists and public transport.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne bih se odrekao auta, prebrzo dolazim posvuda.
lines.en: I wouldn't give up the car, I get everywhere so quickly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Razumijem, ali u centru je bicikl često brži i sigurniji.
lines.en: I understand, but in the centre a bike is often faster and safer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sigurniji? Na cesti je previše opasnosti za bicikliste.
lines.en: Safer? On the road there are too many dangers for cyclists.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato grad treba graditi više biciklističkih staza.
lines.en: That's why the city should build more bike lanes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To košta, a novca uvijek nedostaje.
lines.en: That costs money, and there's never enough.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dugoročno se isplati: manje gužvi i čišći zrak.
lines.en: In the long run it pays off: fewer jams and cleaner air.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Možda imaš pravo, vrijedi barem pokušati.
lines.en: Maybe you're right, it's worth at least trying.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sitni koraci mijenjaju grad.
lines.en: Small steps change a city.

days.title: Rad i slobodno vrijeme
days.objective: Argue where the line between work and rest should fall and defend the balance.
days.paretoFocus: The work-life balance debate is universal and very B1: it needs comparatives, opinions and reasons about how you spend your time.
drills: Name two things you do in your free time to recover.
Give one reason too much work is harmful.
State how many days off you think a person needs, with a reason.

items: Say 'After work I like to use my free time for sport'.
Say 'We should rest more' in standard Croatian.
Say 'It is important to find a balance between work and rest'.

activities.title: Posao i odmor
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on balancing work and rest. You will talk about free time, fatigue and planning days off.

items.hr: Nakon posla volim iskoristiti slobodno vrijeme za sport.
items.en: After work I like to use my free time for sport.
items.note: iskoristiti = to make use of

items.hr: Vikendom se odmaram i družim s prijateljima.
items.en: On weekends I rest and spend time with friends.
items.note: odmarati se = to rest

items.hr: Previše rada dovodi do umora i stresa.
items.en: Too much work leads to fatigue and stress.
items.note: umor = fatigue

items.hr: Važno je pronaći ravnotežu između posla i odmora.
items.en: It's important to find a balance between work and rest.
items.note: ravnoteža = balance

items.hr: Planiram uzeti nekoliko dana odmora.
items.en: I'm planning to take a few days off.
items.note: nekoliko = a few

activities.title: Ravnoteža
activities.intro: Practise talking about work and rest and review an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian: 'We should rest more.'
questions.explanation: After trebamo Croatian uses the infinitive odmarati with the reflexive se; the da-clauses are not standard Croatian.

questions.prompt: It's important to find a balance: Važno je ___ ravnotežu.
questions.explanation: pronaći is the infinitive used after the impersonal Važno je.

questions.prompt: Review the reflexive present: 'On weekends I rest.' Vikendom se ___.
questions.explanation: The first person singular present of odmarati se is odmaram, kept with the reflexive se.

questions.prompt: After work I like to use my free time: Nakon posla volim ___ slobodno vrijeme.
questions.explanation: After volim Croatian uses the infinitive iskoristiti.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Too much work leads to stress.'
questions.explanation: Previše governs the genitive rada, and dovodi do takes the genitive stresa.

activities.title: Raditi bez prestanka
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend works even on weekends; you argue that rest is not wasted time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Radim i vikendom, inače ne stignem sve.
lines.en: I work on weekends too, otherwise I don't get everything done.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ali kad se onda odmaraš? Tijelo treba pauzu.
lines.en: But when do you rest then? The body needs a break.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odmor je gubitak vremena, posao je važniji.
lines.en: Rest is a waste of time, work is more important.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne slažem se. Bez odmora si manje učinkovit.
lines.en: I don't agree. Without rest you're less effective.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Možda, no bojim se da ću zaostati.
lines.en: Maybe, but I'm afraid I'll fall behind.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kratki odmori zapravo povećavaju produktivnost.
lines.en: Short breaks actually increase productivity.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro, pokušat ću barem nedjeljom ne raditi.
lines.en: All right, I'll try not to work at least on Sundays.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To je pametan prvi korak.
lines.en: That's a smart first step.

days.title: Kultura i običaji
days.objective: Argue how much old customs should be preserved as society changes.
days.paretoFocus: Customs and etiquette shape daily social life, and debating tradition versus change is a mature B1 conversation with clear opposing sides.
drills: Name one custom your family keeps when guests come.
Give one reason customs are worth preserving.
State whether you think traditions should change with time, with a reason.

items: Say 'When we visit someone, it is customary to bring a gift'.
Say 'We should preserve old customs' in standard Croatian.
Say 'Some customs change over time'.

activities.title: Naši običaji
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on keeping customs as society changes. You will talk about guests, gifts and how traditions shift.

items.hr: Kad idemo u goste, običaj je donijeti poklon.
items.en: When we visit someone, it is customary to bring a gift.
items.note: poklon = gift

items.hr: Svaka regija ima svoje običaje i jela.
items.en: Each region has its own customs and dishes.
items.note: običaj = custom

items.hr: Gosta uvijek ponudimo kavom.
items.en: We always offer a guest coffee.
items.note: instrumental: kavom

items.hr: Neki običaji se s vremenom mijenjaju.
items.en: Some customs change over time.
items.note: s vremenom = over time

items.hr: Poštivanje starijih dio je naše kulture.
items.en: Respecting elders is part of our culture.
items.note: poštivanje = respect

activities.title: Tradicija i promjena
activities.intro: Practise talking about customs and review an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian: 'We should preserve old customs.'
questions.explanation: After trebamo Croatian uses the infinitive čuvati; the da-clauses are not standard Croatian.

questions.prompt: It is customary to bring a gift: Običaj je donijeti ___.
questions.explanation: poklon is 'gift'; after donijeti it is the accusative, identical to the dictionary form here.

questions.prompt: Review the instrumental: 'We offer the guest coffee.' Gosta nudimo ___.
questions.explanation: To offer someone with something Croatian uses the instrumental kavom.

questions.prompt: Each region has its own customs: Svaka regija ima svoje ___.
questions.explanation: The plural accusative of običaj is običaje, the object of ima.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Some customs change over time.'
questions.explanation: The subject Neki običaji takes the reflexive se, and s vremenom uses the instrumental.

activities.title: Čuvati ili mijenjati
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend fears traditions are dying; you argue culture can change and still stay ours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mislim da mladi više ne poštuju stare običaje.
lines.en: I think young people no longer respect old customs.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nije baš tako. Samo ih slave na svoj način.
lines.en: It's not quite like that. They just celebrate them in their own way.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali neke tradicije jednostavno nestaju.
lines.en: But some traditions are simply disappearing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Neke da, no pojavljuju se i nove. To je prirodno.
lines.en: Some yes, but new ones appear too. That's natural.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bojim se da ćemo izgubiti svoj identitet.
lines.en: I'm afraid we'll lose our identity.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kultura se mijenja, a ipak ostaje naša.
lines.en: Culture changes, and yet it stays ours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Možda imaš pravo, važno je da pamtimo odakle dolazimo.
lines.en: Maybe you're right, it's important that we remember where we come from.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Točno, tradicija i promjena mogu ići zajedno.
lines.en: Exactly, tradition and change can go together.

days.title: Planovi za budućnost
days.objective: Argue whether it is better to plan the future carefully or stay flexible.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about hopes and plans is a core B1 production skill, and it uses the future, the conditional and verbs like nadati se and planirati.
drills: Say one hope you have for five years from now with Nadam se da...
Name one concrete step toward a goal you have.
State whether you plan strictly or improvise, with a reason.

items: Say 'I hope in five years I'll have a stable job'.
Say 'First I need to finish my studies' in standard Croatian.
Say 'Every big goal starts with a small step'.

activities.title: Što me čeka
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on planning versus staying flexible. You will use the future, express hopes and talk about steps toward a goal.

items.hr: Nadam se da ću za pet godina imati stabilan posao.
items.en: I hope in five years I'll have a stable job.
items.note: nadati se = to hope

items.hr: Imam jasan plan, ali sam spreman i na promjene.
items.en: I have a clear plan, but I'm also ready for changes.
items.note: spreman = ready

items.hr: Sanjam o tome da jednoga dana otvorim svoj obrt.
items.en: I dream of one day opening my own business.
items.note: sanjati = to dream

items.hr: Prvo trebam završiti studij, a onda ću tražiti posao.
items.en: First I need to finish my studies, then I'll look for a job.
items.note: trebam + infinitive završiti

items.hr: Svaki veliki cilj počinje malim korakom.
items.en: Every big goal starts with a small step.
items.note: cilj = goal, korak = step

activities.title: Ciljevi i koraci
activities.intro: Practise talking about the future and review an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian: 'First I need to finish my studies.'
questions.explanation: After trebam Croatian uses the infinitive završiti; the da-clauses are not standard Croatian.

questions.prompt: I hope I'll have a stable job: Nadam se da ću imati stabilan ___.
questions.explanation: posao is 'job'; it is the object of imati in the accusative, identical to the dictionary form here.

questions.prompt: Review the future: 'In five years I will open a shop.' Za pet godina ___ trgovinu.
questions.explanation: The Croatian future is the infinitive stem plus the clitic ću, giving otvorit ću.

questions.prompt: I have a clear plan: Imam jasan ___.
questions.explanation: plan is the object of Imam in the accusative, identical to the dictionary form here.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Every big goal starts with a small step.'
questions.explanation: The subject Svaki veliki cilj is followed by počinje and the instrumental malim korakom.

activities.title: Plan ili sloboda
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend prefers to improvise; you argue that a plan gives security while staying open.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne volim planirati, radije prepuštam stvari slučaju.
lines.en: I don't like planning, I'd rather leave things to chance.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meni je bez plana teško, volim znati kamo idem.
lines.en: For me it's hard without a plan, I like to know where I'm going.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali previše planiranja te sputava.
lines.en: But too much planning holds you back.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Možda, no plan mi daje sigurnost.
lines.en: Maybe, but a plan gives me security.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Život te ionako iznenadi, pa čemu onda?
lines.en: Life surprises you anyway, so what's the point?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato imam plan, ali ostajem spreman na promjene.
lines.en: That's why I have a plan, but I stay ready for changes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To zvuči razumno, negdje između nas dvoje.
lines.en: That sounds reasonable, somewhere between the two of us.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Upravo tako, cilj bez fleksibilnosti ne vrijedi puno.
lines.en: Exactly, a goal without flexibility isn't worth much.

days.title: Snalaženje u gradu
days.objective: Argue whether it is better to ask locals for directions or trust a navigation app.
days.paretoFocus: Finding your way and giving directions is a constant traveller's need, and it uses the imperative and place words while carrying a genuine two-sided debate.
drills: Ask the way to one place with Kako da dođem do...?
Give a short route using Idite ravno, pa skrenite...
State whether you trust people or an app more for directions, with a reason.

items: Ask 'Excuse me, how do I get to the main station?'
Give the instruction 'Go straight, then turn left'.
Say 'The tram stop is right around the corner'.

activities.title: Kako do cilja
activities.intro: This lesson builds toward a position on asking people versus using an app. You will ask the way, give directions with the imperative and name places to catch transport.

items.hr: Oprostite, kako da dođem do glavnog kolodvora?
items.en: Excuse me, how do I get to the main station?
items.note: kako da dođem = how do I get

items.hr: Idite ravno, prijeđite cestu, pa skrenite lijevo.
items.en: Go straight, cross the road, then turn left.
items.note: imperative: idite, prijeđite, skrenite

items.hr: Tramvajska stanica je odmah iza ugla.
items.en: The tram stop is right around the corner.
items.note: stanica = stop

items.hr: Ako se izgubite, pitajte nekoga na ulici.
items.en: If you get lost, ask someone on the street.
items.note: netko = someone

items.hr: Treća linija vozi do centra.
items.en: Line three goes to the centre.
items.note: linija = line

activities.title: Put do cilja
activities.intro: Practise directions and review an earlier structure.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian for 'Turn left.'
questions.explanation: The polite imperative is Skrenite, and the direction is the ijekavian lijevo, not levo.

questions.prompt: The tram stop is around the corner: Tramvajska ___ je iza ugla.
questions.explanation: stanica is the stop; here it is the feminine nominative subject.

questions.prompt: Review the relative pronoun: 'the street in which I live' ulica u ___ živim.
questions.explanation: After the preposition u expressing location the relative pronoun is the feminine locative kojoj.

questions.prompt: Line three goes to the centre: Treća ___ vozi do centra.
questions.explanation: linija is the tram or bus line, the feminine nominative subject here.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Go straight, then turn left.'
questions.explanation: Two imperatives join with pa: Idite ravno, then skrenite lijevo.

activities.title: Kojim putem
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend wants to follow the phone; you argue asking people is often faster and more reliable.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Samo upali navigaciju i pratimo ju.
lines.en: Just turn on the navigation and let's follow it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Radije bih pitao nekoga, brže je i pouzdanije.
lines.en: I'd rather ask someone, it's faster and more reliable.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma ljudi te često pošalju u krivom smjeru.
lines.en: Oh, people often send you the wrong way.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ponekad da, ali aplikacija ne zna za zatvorene ceste.
lines.en: Sometimes yes, but the app doesn't know about closed roads.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Istina, jučer nas je odvela u slijepu ulicu.
lines.en: True, yesterday it led us into a dead-end street.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato volim spojiti oboje: kartu i pitanje.
lines.en: That's why I like to combine both: a map and asking.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro, ti pitaj, ja ću pratiti telefon.
lines.en: All right, you ask, I'll follow the phone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dogovoreno, tako sigurno nećemo zalutati.
lines.en: Agreed, that way we surely won't get lost.

days.title: Kako najbolje učiti: pregled
days.objective: Argue which approach to learning a language works best, reusing cases, aspect and relative clauses.
days.paretoFocus: This review day ties together grammar and conversation you have met before, and framing it as a real debate about learning keeps every structure in active use.
drills: Say one thing that helps you learn most, with Mislim da...
Retell one mistake you made and what it taught you.
State whether grammar or speaking helped you more, with a reason.

items: Say 'I think conversation is more important than grammar'.
Say 'I want to speak better' in standard Croatian.
Say 'I need to practise every day'.

activities.title: Učim jezik
activities.intro: This review day builds toward a position on how best to learn a language. The sentences reuse comparatives, relative clauses, aspect and reflexive verbs.

items.hr: Mislim da je razgovor važniji od gramatike.
items.en: I think conversation is more important than grammar.
items.note: comparison + genitive

items.hr: Kad stalno slušam, brže shvaćam.
items.en: When I listen constantly, I understand faster.
items.note: shvaćati = to understand

items.hr: Pogreške koje napravim pomažu mi da naučim.
items.en: The mistakes that I make help me learn.
items.note: relative koje + da-clause

items.hr: Trebam vježbati svaki dan, makar samo deset minuta.
items.en: I need to practise every day, even if only ten minutes.
items.note: trebam + infinitive vježbati

items.hr: Ako se ne bojiš govoriti, brzo napreduješ.
items.en: If you're not afraid to speak, you progress quickly.
items.note: reflexive bojati se

activities.title: Pregled gradiva
activities.intro: Review several earlier structures through sentences about learning.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard Croatian: 'I want to speak better.'
questions.explanation: After želim Croatian uses the infinitive govoriti; the da-clauses are not standard Croatian.

questions.prompt: When I listen constantly, I understand faster: Kad stalno slušam, brže ___.
questions.explanation: shvaćati in the first person singular present is shvaćam.

questions.prompt: Review the relative pronoun: 'The word that I forgot is important.' Riječ ___ sam zaboravio je važna.
questions.explanation: riječ is the object of zaboravio, so the relative pronoun is the feminine accusative koju.

questions.prompt: I need to practise every day: Trebam ___ svaki dan.
questions.explanation: After the modal trebam Croatian uses the infinitive vježbati.

questions.prompt: Build the Croatian for: 'Conversation is more important than grammar.'
questions.explanation: The comparative važniji takes od and the genitive gramatike.

activities.title: Gramatika ili razgovor
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend insists grammar comes first; you argue speaking and mistakes teach you fastest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bez čvrste gramatike ne možeš dobro govoriti.
lines.en: Without solid grammar you can't speak well.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne slažem se. Meni je najviše pomogao razgovor.
lines.en: I don't agree. Conversation helped me the most.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ali griješiš ako ne znaš pravila.
lines.en: But you make mistakes if you don't know the rules.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Griješim, no tako i najbrže učim.
lines.en: I make mistakes, but that's how I learn fastest too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni pravila daju sigurnost prije nego progovorim.
lines.en: Rules give me security before I start speaking.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Razumijem, svatko uči drukčije. Ipak, treba se usuditi govoriti.
lines.en: I understand, everyone learns differently. Still, you have to dare to speak.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Možda je najbolje spojiti oboje.
lines.en: Maybe it's best to combine both.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se, i pravila i praksa vode do cilja.
lines.en: I agree, both rules and practice lead to the goal.
